_comment: ML-review backlog — the parking lot for AI/ML EXPERIMENT follow-ups: new manifests to try, new features to engineer, new dataset families to build, new target horizons / model families to evaluate, promotion-criteria notes (what a model would need to meet next time to clear the shadow->advisory gate). The autonomous /ml-review skill drains this list every run: it triages each open item, converts the resolved ones into experiments_proposed[] or promotion_recommendations[], marks them, or leaves them open with new evidence appended. Do NOT use this for system bugs (those go to health-review-backlog.json) or strategy trading follow-ups (those go to performance-review-backlog.json). Item ids: MB-YYYYMMDD-NNN. The multi-session ML OPTIMIZATION ROADMAP (docs/ml/optimization-roadmap.md) is the strategic plan these items feed: MB-20260601-001=Phase 0.2, MB-20260530-001=Phase 1.3, MB-20260529-001=Phase 3.1, MB-20260601-002=Phase 3.3 — /ml-review reports progress against that roadmap each run.

items.source: trainer-vm-diag run 28739962239: ict-trainer.service Active: failed (Result: oom-kill) since 2026-07-05 06:14:12 UTC after 41min CPU; the 01:32Z cycle trained the manifest fleet OK through ~02:30 (manifest_ok rows) then a later stage was OOM-killed on the 6GB box. Timer healthy (next 2026-07-06 00:57Z). Also: dataset_builds.jsonl not found at runtime_logs/ (path check MISSING).
items.title: Trainer cycle OOM-killed mid-run (2026-07-05 06:14Z) — identify the offending stage and bound its memory; verify dataset_builds log path
items.description: Next trainer session: journalctl around 06:14Z to identify the stage (likely a heavy dataset build / corpus job post-manifest-loop), add a memory bound or split the stage, confirm the next nightly cycle completes end-to-end. Also resolve where dataset_builds.jsonl actually lives (the diag cmd found nothing at ~/ict-trading-bot/runtime_logs/).

updates.note: DIAGNOSED via trainer relay run 28740341472: python pid 1890173 under ict-trainer.service reached 4.8G anon RSS on the 6G NO-SWAP box -> GLOBAL oom (apport invoked the killer); the unit's own journal is silent 05:40-06:15 (a quiet big-allocation stage, likely a corpus/dataset build after the manifest loop). CONTAINMENT APPLIED (autonomous trainer ops, relay issue #5621): systemd drop-in MemoryHigh=4G/MemoryMax=5G on ict-trainer.service (unit-scoped throttle+kill instead of global OOM) + 2G persistent swapfile + reset-failed. VERIFY: next nightly cycle (2026-07-06T00:57Z) completes end-to-end; if a stage still dies at the 5G cap, identify + split that stage.

Trainer-VM pull this session: systemctl is-enabled=disabled / is-active=failed, ExecMainStatus=15 (SIGTERM, not SIGKILL-OOM) for ict-trainer.service; timer enabled+active. journalctl -n150 tail shows clean manifest_ok/manifest_audit_flagged rows through 2026-07-05 02:30Z and a successful DB pull 2026-07-06T04:00-04:00:54Z (trade_journal.db 420MB, signal_audit.jsonl 788871 lines) -- no new OOM signature visible in the tail pulled. Could not confirm end-to-end whether the 2026-07-05 MemoryHigh/MemoryMax containment fix has been cleanly verified across a full nightly cycle post-fix (the service's last recorded ExecMainStatus is 15, which is a plain SIGTERM -- consistent with a manual/deploy-triggered stop, not necessarily a fresh OOM). Left open pending a direct post-nightly-cycle status check (next timer fire ~00:57Z).

ESCALATION: trainer VM 158.178.209.121 is now fully SSH-unreachable — 'Connection timed out during banner exchange' on TWO trainer-vm-diag attempts this session (runs 28925972357, 28926088174). ML training lifecycle cannot be verified: no registry read, no training-cycle journal, no dataset-build confirmation. The /api/bot/backtests/sweeps trainer mirror is stale ~29d (mirror_age_seconds 2510515, newest sweep date 2026-06-09). Shadow-prediction logging on the LIVE VM is healthy (heads accruing, last_seen ~07:36Z, advisory btc-regime-15m-lgbm-v2 scoring) so live inference is unaffected — but no NEW training/dataset work can be landing while the trainer is down. OPERATOR ACTION LIKELY NEEDED: the box is unreachable via the SSH relay, so it may be hung/down post-OOM (this item's original 07-05 OOM-kill is the probable cause) and need an OCI-console reboot / re-provision (trainer is autonomous territory but SSH-dead blocks the relay path).

SWAP HEADROOM FIX + trainer back online. The 07-05 containment (MemoryHigh=4G/MemoryMax=5G + 2G swap) proved INSUFFICIENT: OOM-kills recurred 2026-07-07 08:48Z and 2026-07-08 07:06Z (both Result: oom-kill, status=killed/15, ~40min CPU each) because the service MAIN process blew the 5G RAM + only-2G swap = ~7G effective ceiling (OOMPolicy=continue only contains a per-manifest SUBPROCESS OOM, not the orchestrator itself). The 07-08 SSH-dead escalation has CLEARED — trainer 158.178.209.121 reachable again via the relay this session (runs on issues #6003-#6006); host memory healthy (5.8G total, ~370M used, 5.2G avail) and the two OOMs were cgroup/host-ceiling kills, not a leak visible at idle. FIX APPLIED (autonomous trainer ops, relay #6006): grew /swapfile 2G → 8G (persisted in /etc/fstab; dd+mkswap+swapon verified Swap: 8.0Gi after). With MemorySwapMax=infinity the main process can now swap past the 5G RAM cap (13G addressable) instead of being OOM-killed; the 5G RAM cap still protects the ~6G host. Made durable in deploy/training-vm-cloud-init.yaml (runcmd provisions 8G swap idempotently) + documented in docs/ml/training-center.md. Disk after: 45G/38G used/7.8G free (83%) — acceptable, watch it. VERIFY: next nightly cycle (ict-trainer.timer ~00:43Z) completes end-to-end without oom-kill; a manual run_training_cycle.sh can verify sooner. DEEPER FOLLOW-UP (the real fix, avoids swap-thrash slowdown): run each manifest in its own subprocess so the orchestrator's RSS stays low and any single manifest's OOM is fully contained by OOMPolicy=continue — currently the heavy stage lives in the main process. Also still open: confirm where dataset_builds.jsonl actually lives (original path check MISSING).

RESOLVED — the 8G swap fix HELD through a full nightly cycle. Verified via trainer-vm-diag run 28987682325 (issue #6014) at ~01:31Z: the ~00:43Z ict-trainer.timer cycle ran clean — ict-trainer.service Result=success, ExecMainStatus=0, NRestarts=0; NO oom/killed/out-of-memory/traceback lines in the journal since 00:40Z; NO dmesg oom-kill. The cycle was still actively running (is-active=activating), grinding through market_features/market_raw dataset builds (row_count ~215838) steadily from 00:47Z → 01:20Z+ with no interruption. The proof the fix is what prevented the kill (not a lighter run): free -h showed Swap 8.0Gi total / 4.3Gi USED — the process is actively consuming 4.3G of swap, which is >2× the old 2G swap ceiling, so under the pre-fix config (5G RAM cap + 2G swap ≈ 7G) this exact run would have OOM-killed again. With MemorySwapMax=infinity the main process swaps past the 5G RAM cap instead of dying. Host mem 5.8G total / 4.6G used / 490M free (tight but stable, swap absorbing the overflow). Disk watch note stands: 45G/38G used/7.8G free (83%). The DEEPER follow-up (per-manifest subprocess isolation so the orchestrator RSS stays low and OOMPolicy=continue fully contains a single-manifest OOM — avoids the swap-thrash slowdown this fix trades for stability) remains a documented, non-urgent improvement; opening it as its own forward-looking item rather than keeping this incident item open. Also still deferred: confirm where dataset_builds.jsonl actually lives (original path check MISSING) — carried into the same follow-up. INCIDENT CLOSED.

items.opened_by: claude (trainer OOM verification 2026-07-09, split from MB-20260705-TRAINER-OOM)
items.source: Forward-looking successor to the RESOLVED MB-20260705-TRAINER-OOM. The 8G-swap fix stopped the OOM-kills (verified OOM-free through the 2026-07-09 00:43Z nightly cycle, trainer-vm-diag #6014: Result=success, 4.3G swap in use vs the old 2G ceiling) but it buys stability by letting the orchestrator MAIN process swap past its 5G RAM cap — i.e. it trades an OOM-kill for swap-thrash slowdown on the 6G/1-OCPU trainer.
items.title: Trainer memory: run each manifest in its own subprocess so the orchestrator RSS stays low (avoid swap-thrash); confirm dataset_builds.jsonl path
items.description: The proper fix (deeper than the swap headroom): run each training manifest in its OWN subprocess so the orchestrator's resident set stays small and a single manifest's OOM is fully contained by the unit's OOMPolicy=continue (per-manifest subprocess kill) instead of the main process ballooning to ~9G total footprint and leaning on 4.3G of swap. That removes the swap-thrash the current fix accepts, and keeps the 5G RAM cap protecting the ~6G host without ever needing to page. Scope: identify the heavy stage that lives in the main process (the quiet 05:40-06:15 big-allocation stage from the original incident — likely a corpus/dataset build post-manifest-loop), refactor it (or the manifest loop) into a spawned subprocess per manifest, re-verify a nightly cycle stays low-RSS. SECONDARY (carried from the parent item): confirm where dataset_builds.jsonl actually lives — the original diag path check at ~/ict-trading-bot/runtime_logs/ found nothing; resolve the real path (or the writer) so dataset-build health is queryable. Trainer-VM autonomous territory (no operator gate to implement); non-urgent since the swap fix has the box stable.
items.trigger_condition: Next /ml-review or trainer-touching session with spare cycles; or if the trainer shows swap-thrash slowdown / disk pressure (the box is at 83% disk with the 8G swapfile).
items.resolution_criteria: Each manifest trains in its own subprocess (orchestrator RSS stays well under the 5G cap, swap usage near zero during a nightly cycle) OR a decision is recorded that the swap headroom is sufficient and the refactor isn't worth it; AND the dataset_builds.jsonl path is confirmed (writer + read path aligned).

updates.note: RECURRENCE — the swap-thrash tradeoff this item flagged BIT for real, elevating it from non-urgent to prioritize. 2026-07-10 ~09:34Z the trainer went SSH-DEAD (trainer_reachability_alert DOWN; trainer-vm-diag probe #6085 returned 'Connection timed out during banner exchange … port 22 timed out'). This is the swap-thrash failure mode the 8G-swap 'fix' accepts made concrete: instead of an OOM-kill the box paged so hard on the 1-OCPU trainer that sshd could not complete a handshake — the VM was alive but unreachable, so no in-band recovery was possible. RECOVERED autonomously via an OCI control-plane hard RESET (reset-instance workflow, issue #6086: STOPPING→RUNNING, capacity preserved, no terminate) — the sanctioned recovery for exactly this symptom, no operator console needed. Post-reset verified healthy (trainer-vm-diag #6087): uptime 22m, mem 377Mi/5.8Gi used + 0B swap, ict-trainer-publish.timer active, mirror publishing again (trainer_status.json fresh, registry 80 models). NET: the swap headroom stops OOM-KILLS but does NOT stop swap-thrash-into-SSH-death — so 'the swap fix has the box stable' (resolution_criteria escape hatch) is now DISPROVEN; the subprocess-isolation refactor (keep orchestrator RSS under the 5G cap so it never pages) is the actual fix and should be done, not deferred. Interim mitigation to consider until then: a MemoryHigh throttle low enough that the orchestrator can't reach the swap-thrash regime, or a lightweight watchdog that RESETs on the banner-timeout symptom. Recurring MES/ML-lifecycle-availability risk (each wedge stalls all training until a manual/auto reset).

RESOLVED (PR #6917). (1) Subprocess-isolation premise verified ALREADY TRUE — each manifest is its own `python -m ml train` under the bash orchestrator; the dataset build / calibrators / publish are separate processes too. (2) The real residual (single-process peak RSS) fixed by load-time column projection in ml/experiments/runner.py::_load_jsonl (+ the audit subprocess) — synthetic bench 1401→271 MB @300k×40 rows (5.2×). (3) SECONDARY confirmed: dataset_builds.jsonl lives at runtime_logs/trainer/dataset_builds.jsonl (BUILD_LOG_PATH default in build_trainer_datasets.sh; verified present on-box, diag #6916). Band-aid drop-ins (MemorySwapMax=0/MemoryHigh=infinity/MemoryMax=5G/OOMPolicy=continue) KEPT deliberately as containment backstop; ict-trainer-catchup.timer installed+enabled on-box 2026-07-19 (was never enabled — ml-infra audit F7). LIVE VERIFICATION: see the memfix stage of the session verification bundle.

items.source: Ran the full fleet model scorecard (scripts/ml/fleet_scorecard.sh, MAX_BARS=8000) on the trainer VM as a nohup background job (trainer-vm-diag relays #4697 launch, #4699 read; job pid 1574680). RG3 (Stage-1, clean-candle replay vs the dataset's own regime_label = does it DISCRIMINATE) + RG4 (Stage-2, re-score the EXACT logged-live shadow_predictions.jsonl rows = train/serve SKEW detector) across all 17 shadow regime heads. shadow_predictions.jsonl had 109,641 rows.
items.title: Fleet scorecard (RG3+RG4): replay pre-gate caught 2 MES heads anti-predictive live (skew); BTC 5m/15m fleet healthy + promotion-candidate; BTC 1h degrades live; MES live rows largely unlabeled
items.description: RESULTS (RG3 offline AUC -> RG4 live AUC): ALL 17 heads pass RG3 (TRUSTWORTHY_SIGNAL, AUC 0.58-0.92) -- offline discrimination is NOT the differentiator. RG4 (live-row re-score) is what separates them: (A) KEEP / promotion-ready -- BTC 5m/15m fleet holds on live rows with market features present (mf=True) and near-complete labeling (unlab 0-6): btc-regime-5m-lgbm-yz-v1 0.92->0.82, btc-regime-5m-lgbm-v2 0.89->0.79, btc-regime-5m-baseline-v1 0.82->0.73, btc-regime-15m-lgbm-yz-v1 0.83->0.76, btc-regime-15m-lgbm-v2 0.81->0.72, btc-regime-15m-baseline-v1 0.75->0.69; plus mes-regime-5m-lgbm-v2 0.80->0.77 and mes-regime-5m-baseline-v1 0.68->0.75 (both on thin labeled samples ~200-254 of ~3000). (B) WATCH -- BTC 1h fleet degrades materially live: btc-regime-1h-lgbm-v2 0.78->0.61, btc-regime-1h-lgbm-yz-v1 0.79->0.60, btc-regime-1h-lgbm-funding-svble-v1 0.78->0.55 (AT the 0.55 floor). Clears the floor but the live edge is thin; not promotion-ready until the 1h>5m/15m degradation is understood. (C) FIX-SKEW / KILL -- the gate's catch: mes-regime-15m-lgbm-v2 0.89 offline -> 0.32 live ANTI_PREDICTIVE (the btc-regime-yz failure mode exactly; ~77 labeled rows), and mes-regime-15m-baseline-v1 0.58 -> 0.44 ANTI_PREDICTIVE (weak offline AND inverts live). (D) UNSCOREABLE on live (0 labeled live rows -> RG4 can't judge): mes-regime-1d-lgbm-v2 (242 rows), mes-regime-5m-lgbm-yz-v1 (1629 rows), eth-regime-1h-lgbm-v1 (84 rows, too new), eth-regime-1h-lgbm-xasset-v1 (320 rows, the D2a cross-asset probe, too new). CROSS-CUTTING FINDINGS: (1) MES live rows are largely UNLABELED (unlab 1025-2777 of ~1100-3000) vs BTC unlab~=0 -> a labeling-pipeline gap specific to MES that blinds RG4 on half the fleet. (2) Two btc yz heads (5m, 1h) carry advisory-stage records with mf=False scoring 0.5 NO_EDGE -> a handful of advisory-stage logged rows lack the market-feature block (data-capture parity smell). (3) Offline AUC (RG3) green-lights all 17 incl. the 0.89->0.32 skew head -> confirms the build-time funnel must gate on RG4 (live-row parity + net-of-cost edge), not RG3 AUC.
items.trigger_condition: Next /ml-review; or any session deciding regime-head promotion/demotion, or working live-feature/label parity on the trainer.
items.resolution_criteria: Operator-gated promotion decisions taken on the (A) BTC 5m/15m candidates; (C) the two anti-predictive MES 15m heads either skew-root-caused+fixed or demoted/killed; (1) the MES live-labeling gap and (2) the advisory mf=False capture gap are root-caused (live feature/label parity). Each is its own follow-up; this item is the umbrella record of the scorecard.

items.source: Fleet scorecard RG4 (MB-20260626-001) FIRST flagged two MES 15m heads as anti-predictive live (mes-regime-15m-lgbm-v2 0.89 offline -> 0.32 live; mes-regime-15m-baseline-v1 0.58 -> 0.44) on a THIN labeled sample (~77/~127 rows). After the MES candle base was fixed (BL-20260626-MES-BASE-STALE, fresh yfinance candles to 2026-06-26), RG4 was RE-RUN on the full labeled sample and the verdict REVERSED: both 15m heads are now TRUSTWORTHY (15m-lgbm-v2 0.59 on ~1011 rows; 15m-baseline-v1 0.64 on ~1061). The original anti-predictive reading was a stale-candle / thin-sample artifact. The re-run instead surfaced a DIFFERENT broken head: mes-regime-5m-lgbm-yz-v1 (previously UNSCOREABLE, 0 labeled) is now ANTI_PREDICTIVE at 0.17 on ~1460 labeled rows.
items.title: SYSTEM-REVIEW PICKUP (REVISED): the two MES 15m heads CLEARED on fixed data; instead review mes-regime-5m-lgbm-yz-v1 (now anti-predictive live, 0.17)
items.description: REVISED 2026-06-26 after fixing the MES candle base + re-running RG4. ORIGINAL flag (demote the two MES 15m heads) is RETRACTED — on the full labeled sample with fresh candles they are TRUSTWORTHY (lgbm-v2 0.59, baseline-v1 0.64), not anti-predictive; the 0.32/0.44 readings were the stale-candle thin-sample artifact the caveat warned about. NEW ACTION for the next /system-review: mes-regime-5m-lgbm-yz-v1 — UNSCOREABLE in the first scorecard (0 labeled live rows) — is now revealed by the fixed-data re-run as ANTI_PREDICTIVE live (RG4 AUC 0.17 on ~1460 labeled rows; it strongly inverts). It is shadow-stage (observe-only, no live-trade risk) but pollutes the shadow stream with an inverted signal and must not be promoted. Recommend: demote mes-regime-5m-lgbm-yz-v1 to candidate (observe-only, reversible) and root-cause the inversion (likely a live MES feature-parity / yang-zhang-vol computation issue specific to this yz variant — note its BTC 1h/5m yz siblings also degrade or invert live). Caveat retained: RG4 here uses a global vol_threshold (0.003), not each dataset's calibrated median — a refinement (read the calibrated threshold from market_features metadata) would sharpen the absolute AUCs, but the 0.17 inversion is far below any threshold-sensitivity band. Tier-3 stage flip — operator-gated; the review proposes, the operator runs promote-stage/demote.

By contrast its non-yz sibling mes-regime-5m-lgbm-v2 reads 0.765 TRUSTWORTHY, so this is a yz-variant-specific defect.

2026-06-30 (root-cause from the MES-labeling investigation, /ml-review buildout session): the MES 'all live rows UNLABELED -> RG4 unscoreable' symptom is NOT a labeling-code bug in replay_pregate_live (no symbol-key or threshold issue) — it is a STALE CANDLE BASE: MES market_raw content ends 2026-06-12 (the IBKR->trainer MES market-data sync froze; daily build reports 'ok' but re-emits stale candles), so RG4 cannot join any live MES row after 2026-06-12 to a realized-label bar. Tracked as BL-20260626-MES-BASE-STALE (health backlog #99). => The MES multi-symbol vol-gate extension stays blocked on REFRESHING the frozen MES candle pull (trainer-VM data op), NOT on model or labeling-code work. The per-symbol RG4 threshold fix (MB-20260628-RG4-THRESH) was shipped alongside but does not make stale-base MES rows labelable.

2026-06-30 (MES candle base — durable fix shipped commit 28ddbf5, PR #5167): the MES-labeling blocker (BL-20260626-MES-BASE-STALE) is now durably fixed at the build layer — build_mes_market freshness-gates the stale IBKR shard and falls to fresh yfinance ES=F, so after merge + the next trainer cycle the MES 5m/15m market_raw advances past 06-12 and RG4 can label live MES rows again (mes-5m-v2 / mes-5m-yz become re-scoreable). Deep-history restore (revive the live-VM IBKR pull) is the operational follow-up. Re-run RG4 on the MES fleet once the fresh candles land to reconfirm mes-5m-v2 (promotion candidate) and mes-5m-yz (demote candidate).

items.source: Ran the formal promotion gate-check (python -m ml gate-check, regime profile, WITH --datasets-root for oos_edge + live --db) on the 6 BTC 5m/15m regime candidates from the fleet scorecard, via scripts/ml/gate_check_candidates.sh (trainer-vm-diag #4713 launch, #4716 read). Full results: docs/research/promotion-gatecheck-and-mes-labeling-2026-06-26.md.
items.title: Regime-head promotion is structurally blocked on the live_agreement gate (trade-win AUC on ~11 trades) — decide: re-target it to RG4 regime-discrimination, or wait for BTC real-money flow
items.description: GATE-CHECK RESULT: the two strongest heads — btc-regime-15m-lgbm-yz-v1 and btc-regime-15m-lgbm-v2 — clear EVERY gate (oos_edge +0.276/+0.265 over the modal baseline on 5 purged WF-CV folds; shadow_soak 22-31d; drift clean KS 0.16-0.17; cross_run_stability std ~0.002-0.004; non_degenerate via the imbalance-aware path) EXCEPT live_agreement (0.267 / 0.500 vs the 0.55 floor). btc-regime-5m-lgbm-v2 additionally fails drift (KS 0.262); btc-regime-5m-lgbm-yz-v1 is too freshly retrained (1.6d soak, 1 run). The two baselines (5m/15m) correctly fail (oos_edge 0.0 + degenerate F1 0). THE STRUCTURAL PROBLEM: live_agreement = rank-AUC(regime-volatility score vs realized trade WIN), measured on just 11 live BTC closed trades. (1) It is MIS-TARGETED for a regime head — a volatility-regime classifier predicts the regime, not trade outcomes; the regime-appropriate live signal is the RG4 live regime-discrimination AUC (0.72-0.76 for these two heads, strong). (2) It is SAMPLE-STARVED + structurally un-satisfiable while BTC real-money flow is thin (bybit_2 pinned near min-qty) — the SAME blocker that demoted the first advisory head (MB-20260623-001). DECISION FOR THE OPERATOR (Tier-3, gate-profile / policy): (A) re-target the regime profile's live-track-record gate from live_agreement (trade-outcome) to an RG4-style live regime-discrimination AUC (keeping all other gates) — the regime profile already drops beats_baseline on the same logic; or (B) keep the gate as-is and accept regime heads may never promote until live flow grows. NO PROMOTION recommended today (none is ready; live_agreement is 'not yet measurable', not 'passed').
items.trigger_condition: Next /system-review or /ml-review; or any session deciding regime-head promotion or revising the regime gate profile (ml/promotion/gates.py).
items.resolution_criteria: The operator decides (A) re-target the regime live-track-record gate to RG4-style regime-discrimination (then the two 15m lgbm heads are re-evaluated and promoted if they clear it) OR (B) keep live_agreement and wait for live flow. Either way the decision + rationale is recorded; if (A), gates.py grows a regime live-discrimination gate and the gate-check is re-run.

Re-ran the Option-A gate-check on the trainer (trainer-vm-diag #4755): btc-regime-15m-lgbm-v2 ready=True blocking=[] (oos_edge +0.264 macro_f1 over 5 purged WF-CV, shadow_soak 31.8d, live_regime_discrimination RG4 0.726, drift KS 0.053/PSI 0.0046; live_agreement 0.500 non-blocking). Operator approved promoting v2 (the non-yz head — design prefers v2; yz saturates live). PROMOTION MECHANISM VERIFIED: ran `python -m ml promote-stage btc-regime-15m-lgbm-v2 --new-stage advisory` on the trainer registry — clean shadow->advisory transition recorded in stage_history (#4757) — then ROLLED IT BACK to shadow (#4758). RATIONALE FOR THE HOLD (the load-bearing sequencing finding): the trainer publishes the mirror to the LIVE VM every 2 min, and on `main` the per-bar regime scorer logs SHADOW-stage heads ONLY (advisory per-bar scoring is A/PR #4748, not yet on main). So promoting v2 to advisory BEFORE A merges does not give it order influence (regime heads are excluded from the advisory downsize quorum, the router uses ADX-14, A's flag is default-off) — it merely makes v2 STOP being per-bar-logged on live (a Phase-1 observability loss), the opposite of the intent. CORRECT SEQUENCE: merge A (PR #4748, advisory per-bar scoring + ml_vol_verdict, default-off, Tier-2 operator-ack) to main FIRST, THEN re-promote v2 — so the advisory stage lands on A-ready live code that keeps scoring it AND can accrue the Phase-1 regime_ml_vol_shadow agreement log. Re-promote is one command once A is on main. v2 is at shadow now (live logging intact).
items.update_2026_06_27_pm: DONE this session: A (PR #4748) MERGED to main (squash 113b8522) + DEPLOYED to live (pull-and-deploy #4785) + v2 RE-PROMOTED shadow->advisory (confirmed, no dark-window: A was on live first). A is now Phase-1 LIVE — the live per-bar scorer scores v2 at advisory, accruing the regime_ml_vol_shadow agreement log; ZERO order influence (REGIME_ML_VERDICT_MODE off, trend_vol live cells empty, regime heads excluded from the advisory downsize quorum). The option-2 harness lever (--ml-stage/--ml-model-id) was VALIDATED on the trainer (4 latent bugs found+fixed: scripts/ml sys.path shadow, class_labels source, predict_proba-on-wrapper, opaque errors). VOL-GATING A/B (4 arms, full BTC history, docs/research/A-vol-gating-AB-evidence-2026-06-27.md): POSITIVE — ML-vol-gated net $424/maxDD 8.07%/ret-DD 0.47 vs frozen-vol $59/10.1%/0.05 vs ungated $353/8.24%/0.39 (scored 1123 bars, 0 fallback). Same OFF-cells: the ML vol label decisively beats the frozen-edge label AND beats no-gating; frozen-vol-gating hurt. NEXT before any live flip (each Tier-3, operator-gated): (1) purged walk-forward + multi-symbol re-run of the A/B (the FLIP_POLICY live bar — this was one in-sample BTCUSDT pass); (2) author live config/regime_policy.yaml trend_vol OFF-cells; (3) flip REGIME_ML_VERDICT_MODE=use then REGIME_ROUTER_ENABLED. B stays at annotate (apply path failed its gate).

items.source: WC-3 direction normalization (#3819, merged 2026-06-17, commit 1bbc6171) made trades.direction canonical long/short at the ML dataset read boundary (6 families: trade_outcomes, setup_labels, setup_labels_audit, conviction_meta, account_context, execution_quality) + the backtest_recorder writer. The 04:47Z trainer cycle (head 1bbc6171, cycle_end overall_rc=0 at 04:51:52Z) retrained all manifests on the normalized direction, producing fresh long/short-keyed versions of the two direction-categorical models: conviction-meta-v1 (manifest_ok 04:51:20Z) and setup-quality-lgbm-v2 (manifest_ok 04:51:52Z; e.g. registry run 20260617T045151Z).
items.title: Promote the WC-3-retrained long/short versions of conviction-meta-v1 + setup-quality-lgbm-v2 to shadow (supersede the old buy/sell-keyed shadow versions)
items.description: Both models freeze a categorical map keyed on `direction` at train time and map any unseen value to the -1 'unknown' sentinel at inference (ml/predictors/lightgbm.py:83). The OLD shadow-wired versions learned buy/sell, so after WC-3 they silently encode live long/short rows as -1 — the direction feature is effectively dead for them (a quality regression, NOT a live-trade or correctness risk: these are shadow/observe-only and never influence an order). The new candidate versions retrained 2026-06-17 key on long/short and encode correctly. ACTION for /ml-review: verify the new versions in the registry (list-models), promote candidate->shadow (autonomous, observe-only), and confirm via shadow-stats that the new shadow stream scores sanely; then the old versions are superseded. The setup-candidates-metalabel-* family is unaffected (it already normalized direction to +/-1) and the regime LGBM models do not use direction.

items.resolution_criteria: The long/short-keyed versions of conviction-meta-v1 + setup-quality-lgbm-v2 are at shadow stage and emitting predictions; the old buy/sell-keyed versions are demoted/superseded. Confirmed via list-models + shadow-stats.

items.source: 2026-06-09 live incident (health BL-20260609-001). An operator-approved A/B (set REGIME_BAR_SCORING_DISABLED=1, #3075) proved per-bar regime scoring (S-MLOPT-S13, src/runtime/regime_bar_scoring.py) was saturating the live VM CPU to 95-100% and starving the trader loop into a ~30-40min wedge (06:18-06:57Z). With it OFF, CPU = 0-10% across 18 min (#3076/#3077/#3078).
items.title: Optimize live per-bar regime scoring (regime_bar_scoring.py) so it can be re-enabled without pegging the live VM — currently DISABLED, which pauses the strong regime heads' per-bar track-record accrual (blocks MB-20260529-001 / yz-1h soak)
items.description: Per-bar regime scoring scores every shadow-stage regime head on every closed bar per (symbol, timeframe). On the small live VM that pegs CPU to 95-100% and (with no cgroup isolation) starves the trader's main loop → the 2026-06-09 wedge. Same cost CLASS as MB-20260531-001 (sim per-bar feature reconstruction, which was root-caused to an O(n^2) feature rebuild and fixed byte-identically in sim/engine.py) — but the LIVE path (src/runtime/regime_bar_scoring.py + the ShadowPredictor.predict per head) is unoptimized. It is now DISABLED on the live VM (REGIME_BAR_SCORING_DISABLED=1, observe-only so zero trading impact) — but that pauses the order-influencing per-bar track record the strong heads (1h/MES) need (MB-20260529-001), which gates the yz-1h shadow->advisory promotion evidence (MB-20260527-004). OPTIONS to evaluate: (a) batch/cache the per-bar feature build instead of rebuilding per bar+head (mirror the MB-20260531-001 sim fix); (b) limit per-bar scoring to ONLY the strong heads being promoted (e.g. btc-regime-1h-lgbm-yz-v1) rather than every shadow regime head; (c) coarsen the cadence (score the 1h head on 1h closes only, not every tick); (d) cgroup CPUWeight/nice the trader above the scoring + other sidecars (defense-in-depth, helps regardless). Then re-enable via set-env REGIME_BAR_SCORING_DISABLED=0 and re-run the CPU A/B to confirm it stays bounded.
items.trigger_condition: Next /ml-review; or before re-enabling REGIME_BAR_SCORING on the live VM; or any session touching regime_bar_scoring.py / the yz-1h promotion (MB-20260527-004).
items.resolution_criteria: regime_bar_scoring.py runs on the live VM with CPU staying bounded (no sustained >~50% attributable to scoring, verified via the new status_check.sh CPU snapshot / a re-run A/B) AND the strong regime heads resume accruing per-bar shadow records — so MB-20260529-001 / the yz-1h soak are unblocked. OR a decision that per-bar scoring stays off and the regime-head track record is sourced differently.
evidence_log: 2026-06-09 (fix shipped + deployed + verified, PR #3082): SHIPPED option (a) [batch/cache per (symbol, timeframe)] + option (c) [coarsen cadence via wall-clock fetch gate] in src/runtime/regime_bar_scoring.py. Three changes: (1) Group predictors by (symbol, timeframe) so multiple heads on the same market (e.g. btc-regime-5m-v2 + btc-regime-5m-lgbm-yz-v1) share ONE network fetch + ONE shared closes view per tick instead of fetching the same Bybit/IBKR candles per head; per-head feature_row_for_predictor still runs (each head keeps its own frozen edges/labels/vol_feature_column) but over already-fetched data. (2) Wall-clock fetch gate (_BAR_SECONDS dict + _FETCH_GATE_BUFFER_S=30s): per (symbol, timeframe), skip the fetch until bar_duration - 30s have elapsed since the last fetch — 1h head ~1×/hour instead of 60×, 5m head ~1×/5min instead of 5×. A failed fetch does NOT arm the gate so a transient exchange blip never silences a head for a full bar duration. (3) Cache exchange client per emit_regime_bar_predictions call in _live_fetch_fn so BTCUSDT 5m+15m share one Bybit client. Tier-2 ship: PR #3082 (operator-approved in chat, draft→ready, auto-merge SQUASH, branch updated to base) merged at 834f7ae; pull-and-deploy via system-action #3092 landed cleanly (HEAD 7c45cad→834f7ae, ict-trader-live.service active, boot_audit 0 open packages). Tests: 28 pass (was 14) — new TestFetchGate (6 cases: default-true, unknown-tf permissive, within-gate-false, past-gate-true, 1h gate longevity, no-arm-on-failure) + TestPredictorGrouping (2 cases: two heads same (sym,tf) → one fetch; different (sym,tf) → independent fetches); ruff clean.

2026-06-09 (CPU A/B PASS, #3097): Re-enabled via set-env REGIME_BAR_SCORING_DISABLED=0 (#3093, ict-trader-live.service restarted clean). T+15min steady-state status-check #3097 (two consecutive snapshots 35s apart): loadavg 0.96/0.40/0.66 → 1.34/0.54/0.69 (1m/5m/15m on 2-core box, well under 2.0 saturation); trader process steady at 14.5%/14.6% CPU (vs 95-100% sustained at incident, vs ~5-10% with scoring OFF — i.e. ~5-10pp marginal cost for full regime-head coverage, totally acceptable). Heartbeat fresh (1-25s old at each snapshot), shadow_predictions.jsonl actively writing (size 18,226,341 → 18,226,341 bytes, mtime crossing the snapshot window — the strong heads ARE accruing per-bar records again). All canonical services active; boot_audit clean; no wedge. The brief 1-min load tick to 1.34 between snapshots is the expected sawtooth from bar-close ticks (every (sym, tf) group hits its fetch gate at roughly the same time when a 5m bar closes, then ~270s of dedup-only ticks) — peaks ~half a core, no saturation. Resolution criteria met: bounded CPU verified; strong regime heads resume per-bar track-record accrual → MB-20260529-001 / MB-20260527-004 (yz-1h shadow→advisory soak) UNBLOCKED.
items.status_history: open 2026-06-09 (post-incident kill-switch ON, per-bar evidence pause blocked yz-1h soak) | resolved 2026-06-09 (PR #3082 group-by-(sym,tf) + wall-clock fetch gate + per-tick client cache shipped + deployed + A/B-verified: steady-state load 0.66-0.69 / trader CPU ~14.5% with scoring ON, vs 95-100% at incident — unblocks MB-20260529-001 / MB-20260527-004).

items.source: S-MLOPT-S8 follow-up (roadmap Sessions 1.4+2.1): test whether the two INDEPENDENT levers — S8 cross-symbol pooling and S9 range-vol features — stack in one meta-label. Closes the MB-20260606-002 follow-up lever 'combine with the S9 range-vol/yz features'.
items.title: Cross-symbol + range-vol meta-label (setup-candidates-metalabel-xsym-yz-v1): the S8 and S9 levers do NOT stack -- HONEST NEGATIVE on the real-BTC holdout. research_only, do not adopt.
items.trigger_condition: Any /ml-review run; or any session deciding whether to adopt setup-candidates-metalabel-xsym-yz-v1.
items.resolution_criteria: Resolved as a documented negative: the joint+yz manifest degraded the cross-symbol meta-label on the real-BTC holdout vs joint-only, so it is NOT adopted and stays research_only. The S8 joint-only xsym-v1 remains the family's best meta-label. Re-open only if a future build (more real trades, MES holdout, or a decision-tuned vol feature) changes the verdict.
evidence_log: 2026-06-06 (S-MLOPT-S8-FU): SHIPPED the Tier-1 enabler — setup_candidates builder v1->v2 emits the four S9 range-vol estimators (parkinson/garman_klass/rogers_satchell/yang_zhang) at signal time (past-only, same window + computation as market_features; available for every population so the live_holdout feature space matches). New research_only manifest setup-candidates-metalabel-xsym-yz-v1 stacks the S8 joint pooling (+symbol) with the four range-vol features. PR draft #2903 (branch claude/mlopt-followups-VwDhX).

2026-06-06 (trainer-vm-diag #2906): EVAL = HONEST NEGATIVE. Clean three-way ablation on the IDENTICAL 354-trade real-BTC live_holdout (majority baseline acc 0.7514, base rate 0.2486; joint dataset 22,814 rows = same composition as S8). joint+yz: acc 0.7203 / precision 0.2105 / recall 0.0455 / f1 0.0748 / brier 0.2185. joint-only (xsym-v1): acc 0.7571 / precision 0.5417 / recall 0.1477 / f1 0.2321 / brier 0.2201. BTC-only (metalabel-v1): acc 0.6808 / precision 0.2093 / recall 0.1023 / f1 0.1374 / brier 0.2220. Adding the range-vol features DEGRADED the cross-symbol meta-label: precision collapsed 0.54->0.21 (back to base rate), accuracy fell BELOW the majority baseline, erasing the S8 gain. Purged-WF within-distribution is a wash (joint+yz f1 0.246 / joint-only 0.209 / BTC-only 0.369). CONCLUSION: the two levers do NOT stack. The S9 range-vol lever is SPECIFIC to the volatility-regime label (where it lifted f1_volatile, MB-20260603-004); it does not transfer to the win/loss DECISION (meta-label) target, and at n=354 the extra features overfit the synthetic train distribution. Do NOT adopt; xsym-v1 (joint-only) stays the family's best meta-label. Manifest carries the result; sprint log docs/sprint-logs/S-MLOPT-S8-FU.md.

items.source: Operator asked for a sweep ETA; profiling the running sim sweep (PID 604328) on the trainer VM surfaced an ML-inference cost concern.
items.title: Regime-gate model scoring in sim is ~0.5s/bar — ~100-1000x slower than raw LGBM inference; profile + optimize the per-bar scoring path
items.description: MEASURED, NOT ESTIMATED. The operator's sim sweep (2 variants over 60,000 BTC 5m bars: 5m_roster [no models] + 5m_gate66 [regime-gate model in loop]) ran 9.8h wall / 9.7h CPU at 98.8% CPU sustained (genuinely computing, not stuck) and had completed 0/2 variant outputs (sim writes only at the end). Decomposition: variant-1 (7 strategies, no ML) is ~12 min by local profiling (sim.engine run_replay measured at ~1.66 ms/bar/strategy on the 3.5-day sample); the remaining ~9.5h is variant-2's regime-gate scoring => ~0.57 SECONDS per bar. A trained LGBM regime classifier should infer in microseconds, so the per-bar cost is ~100-1000x what inference itself needs. Ruled out one obvious cause: the model is NOT reloaded per bar (sim/models.py ModelScorer._ensure_loaded guards with self._loaded, loads once). So the cost is in the PER-BAR FEATURE RECONSTRUCTION or the ShadowPredictor scoring wrapper, not the tree inference. Impact: any full-history sweep with a model in the loop is a 10+ hour single-core job, which (a) makes the consolidated sim harness impractical for routine model-in-loop sweeps and (b) is the bottleneck blocking the prem-tier-core work queued this session. NOT yet root-caused — needs a py-spy/cProfile pass on the actual VM scoring path (py-spy is not installed on the trainer; install or use cProfile via a wrapped run). Likely fixes once profiled: vectorize/batch the feature build across bars instead of rebuilding per-bar, precompute regime scores once per bar and reuse across variants, or cache the feature frame. This is ml-pipeline efficiency (sim scoring path), hence ml-review not health-review.
items.trigger_condition: Any /ml-review run; or any session running a model-in-loop sim sweep that takes hours.
items.resolution_criteria: The per-bar regime-gate scoring cost in sim is profiled (py-spy/cProfile on the real path) and the hot spot identified; either an optimization lands that brings a 60k-bar model-in-loop variant under ~30 min on one core, or the finding is documented as inherent with a recommended workflow (e.g. precompute-scores-once) in the sim/ docs.
evidence_log: 2026-05-31 (/health-review): VM pull on sweep PID 604328 — etime=35328s, cpu_ticks=3490149 (cpu=9.69h), bars_total=60000, variants=2, done_outputs=0. Local sim profile: run_replay no-model = 1.66 ms/bar/strategy (3000-bar sample, warmup 300). Implied variant-2 ML cost ~0.57s/bar. ModelScorer loads once (not a per-bar reload).

2026-05-31 (continuation session, LOCAL empirical profile — not the VM): built a representative regime model (200 rounds x 2 classes = 400 trees, num_leaves 31, mirroring btc-regime-5m-lgbm-v2) and profiled the exact sim per-decision hot path (feature_row_for_predictor + ShadowPredictor.predict) over 60k-bar growing history. RESULT OVERTURNS THE LEADING HYPOTHESIS: single-row Booster.predict = ~79 us/call (~4.8s/60k), NOT 0.5s/bar — so 'batch the single-row predict' (the continuation prompt's 'highest-leverage unblock') would save the LEAST (batched is 42x faster per row but the smallest absolute term). Two real per-decision costs measured: (1) the engine:308 closes-rebuild [float(c) for c in history['close'].tolist()] = ~103s/60k, an O(n^2) rebuild that was ~19x pure waste (.tolist() already yields Python floats, so the float() pass re-converted them) — FIXED this session: precompute the close column once + per-bar O(1) slice, byte-identical, Tier-1 sim-only (sim/engine.py); 68 sim tests pass incl. a new byte-equivalence + no-future-leak guard in tests/test_sim_phase2.py. (2) statistics.pstdev inside rolling_log_return_vol uses exact-Fraction arithmetic (math.gcd/Fraction.__new__ dominate its cProfile) = ~13s/60k, second-largest term — a plain float/np stdev would be ~20x faster but would BREAK the #2485 byte-identicality and touches the LIVE path (Tier-2), so logged-not-slammed (follow-up: prove bucket_for_vol stability under the ~1e-15 delta before changing). CRITICAL HONEST CAVEAT: summed, these per-decision terms are ~2 min locally (~6 min at VM-core scale) — the SAME scale as #2485, so they do NOT account for the 9.5h variant-1->variant-2 delta. Ruled out locally: sim/sweep.py builds ONE ModelScorer per variant and _ensure_loaded guards the booster parse (no per-decision re-instantiation/re-parse); the model factor incl. the regime-gate 0.0 does NOT change open_position/bar-skipping (sim/engine.py:347), so decision COUNT should match across variants. Separately found a LATENT O(n^2) in the SHARED path: sim/engine.py:275 future=[df.iloc[j].to_dict() for j in range(i+1,n)] materializes ALL remaining bars as dicts per decision at ~52 us/bar (a single early decision builds ~60k future bars ~= 3s), but it is bounded by variant-1's 12 min (sparse decisions) so it is a latent landmine, NOT the 9.5h cause. NEXT (still needs the VM, per resolution_criteria): cProfile/py-spy the ACTUAL variant-2 run with the real variant configs+model+candles; prime remaining suspect is a decision-COUNT divergence between the real variant configs (does 5m_gate66 trade far more than 5m_roster?), since the per-call scoring cost is now measured-cheap.

2026-05-31 (continuation session, ROOT-CAUSED on the VM via cProfile — definitive): drove the trainer VM through the diag relay (core confirmed idle, sweep PID 604328 gone), authored a 2-variant repro (5m_roster vs 5m_gate66 w/ btc-regime-5m-lgbm-v2 regime_gate) over market_raw/BTCUSDT/5m/v002, and cProfiled it. RESULTS: 5m_roster 67.77s/1800-bars (37.6ms/bar, 594 trades); 5m_gate66 168.3s (93.5ms/bar cProfile-inflated, 594 trades). FIRST KEY FINDING: BOTH variants have IDENTICAL trade count (594) — the decision-COUNT-divergence hypothesis is WRONG; the gate adds only ~1.3-1.5x, not 47x. SECOND, DEFINITIVE FINDING: cProfile cumulative attributes 111.75s of 168s (66%) to sim/engine.py's `future = [df.iloc[j].to_dict() for j in range(i+1,n)]` list comp — the very line my LOCAL pass mis-filed as 'a latent landmine, NOT the 9.5h cause'. It IS the cause: it is O(n^2) (rebuilds ALL remaining bars every decision) AND each row access is pathological under pandas>=3.0 because df columns are PyArrow-backed (the profile shows pandas _ixs/fast_xs/to_dict + arrow array.__iter__ at 4.9M calls dominating). My 2000-bar slice only shows it at ~half the runtime; at the full 60k bars the O(n^2) explodes to the ~9.5h. The DB-init flood (1062 'Database tables created/verified' prints from vwap.py:685 constructing a full Database() per order_package eval) is real noise/wiring-smell but does NOT appear in the top-22 cumulative — it is NOT the bottleneck. FIX SHIPPED (Tier-1, sim-only, byte-identical): precompute all row-dicts ONCE (`all_rows = [df.iloc[j].to_dict() for j in range(n)]`) then index/slice per decision (bar=all_rows[i], future=all_rows[i+1:]) — each row deboxed exactly once, O(n) instead of O(n^2)-with-arrow-debox. 68 sim tests pass; ruff clean. CONFIRMED ON THE VM (same repro, engine patched in place then restored clean): N=2000 5m_roster 67.77s->23.89s (2.84x), 5m_gate66 168.3s->55.35s (3.04x), BOTH still 594 trades (byte-identical); the future listcomp dropped out of the cProfile top entirely (remaining cost is now strategy indicator recompute — turtle_soup._detect_setup, fade_breakout_4h._adx — a separate, smaller O(n^2)). The 2000-bar speedup understates the full-history win: future-build is O(n^2), so at 60k bars it was the dominant term (the ~9.5h), now ~linear. FOLLOW-UP (separate, Tier-2/3): vwap.py self-suppression check builds a write-capable Database() (full DDL + a stray print) per evaluation against the production journal copy — should use a lightweight read-only path and Database.__init__ should not print; logged for an operator-gated fix.

2026-06-01 (/ml-review): Closing: the root-caused sim/engine.py future-bar O(n^2) arrow-debox fix was already VM-confirmed (5m_roster 67.8s->23.9s, 5m_gate66 168.3s->55.4s, both byte-identical 594 trades) per the prior evidence entry. Resolution criteria (hot spot profiled + optimization landed) satisfied; marking resolved.
items.status_history: open 2026-05-31 (continuation: ROOT-CAUSED via VM cProfile — sim/engine.py future-bar O(n^2) arrow-debox is the 9.5h driver, fixed byte-identical Tier-1; predict-cost + decision-count hypotheses both empirically disproved; VM speedup re-measurement pending) | resolved 2026-06-01 (/ml-review): VM-confirmed fix in evidence (engine.py future-bar O(n^2) arrow-debox precompute, 2.84-3.04x on the 2000-bar repro, byte-identical, 68 sim tests pass). Resolution criteria met.

items.source: /ml-review 2026-05-27 — first ml-review session post 2026-05-26 three-way split.
items.title: Add yfinance/ES=F intraday adapter to scripts/ops/build_trainer_datasets.sh so MES regime + setup-quality manifests stop failing every daily cycle
items.description: Three MES manifests fail every trainer cycle with FileNotFoundError at datasets-out/{market_features/MES/{5m,15m},setup_labels/MES/all}/v002/data.jsonl (mes-regime-5m/15m, mes-setup-quality). Root cause already diagnosed in BL-20260526-002 (health-review backlog) — build_trainer_datasets.sh only builds BTCUSDT via the Bybit V5 offvm adapter; the yfinance/ES=F adapter lives in the separate scripts/ops/run_mes_training.sh one-shot but isn't wired into the daily cycle. Net effect: ict-trainer.service exits with rc=1 every cycle even though all 12 BTC manifests succeed. Fix is a build-script extension (Tier-1 trainer-VM tooling, autonomous) — mirror the build_btcusdt_pair helper from PR #2062 for ES=F at 5m + 15m, plus a build_family setup_labels --symbol-scope MES at v002 invocation. Empty dataset is fine (gets manifest_skipped via empty_dataset instead of manifest_failed). This is the ml-review-backlog mirror of BL-20260526-002 — that one tracks the system-health symptom (trainer.service rc=1); this one tracks the ml-pipeline experiment (get the MES models actually training).

items.resolution_criteria: Next trainer cycle log shows mes-regime-{5m,15m} + mes-setup-quality manifests either manifest_ok (real shards built) or manifest_skipped via empty_dataset (no data yet). cycle_end overall_rc=0. Distinct from BL-20260525-004 which is a separate ml-modeling limitation (mes-regime models collapse to majority class even when shards exist).

Root cause: MES datasets existed only at the stale v001 (built by a 2026-05-22 run_serious_baseline.sh one-shot); manifests reference v002. datasets-out build_end overall_rc=0 but only BTCUSDT market_features (215838 rows) built — no MES families. Build-script wiring missing.

Added build_mes_market() to build_trainer_datasets.sh (yfinance ES=F 5m base -> resample 15m -> median-calibrated v002 market_features; symbol-scoped MES setup_labels). All MES manifests now clean: mes-regime-5m + mes-regime-15m => manifest_ok (data built, market_features/MES/5m=10852, 15m=3617); mes-setup-quality + mes-execution-quality + mes-trade-outcome-winrate => manifest_skipped(empty_dataset). cycle_end overall_rc=0 (was rc=1 every cycle). items.source: /ml-review 2026-05-27 — re-verification of BL-20260525-004 (MES regime models collapse to one class).
items.title: Experiment: forward-vol-aware vol feature for MES regime classifiers — backward vol_bucket doesn't separate forward-vol regime label on MES
items.description: After today's cycle: mes-regime-5m macro_f1=0.345, mes-regime-15m macro_f1=0.329 (both flat across 11 runs). The RegimeClassifierTrainer (ml/trainers/regime_classifier.py) is a per-bucket modal-class classifier keyed on the BACKWARD-LOOKING vol_bucket; on MES that backward vol_bucket does not separate the (forward-vol-based) regime_label, so every bucket is range-majority and argmax always picks 'range' (f1_volatile=0). BTC works because BTC vol is autocorrelated; MES intraday isn't. Three concrete experiment paths to try, in order of cheapness: (1) more vol buckets (5+ instead of 2-3) so the trainer can carve out sub-regimes that DO separate forward vol; (2) probability-threshold eval instead of argmax — surface the model's volatile-class probability and tune a threshold over the eval split; (3) a stronger continuous vol feature (e.g. realized-vol-of-realized-vol, or a forward-vol-AWARE feature like the prior bar's range expansion rate). Operator decision 2026-05-25: leave manifests as-is until a dedicated model-training session — this is that session's queue.
items.trigger_condition: Next /ml-review run; or whenever a model-training session is scheduled with capacity for MES experiments.
items.resolution_criteria: mes-regime-{5m,15m} eval shows f1_volatile > 0 with macro_f1 > 0.50 via one of the three approaches; or the operator approves a manifest deprecation. Distinct from MB-20260527-001 (which is about MES *trade-data* manifests, not regime; those are empty_dataset blocked until MES trades).
items.status_history: open -> resolved 2026-05-28 (mes-regime-{5m,15m}-lgbm-v2 shipped in #2226; f1_volatile 0->0.77/0.76, macro_f1 0.69/0.67 on median-calibrated data; registered at shadow in cycle head=71879f0). The three vol-bucket tweaks are moot — the LightGBM port solved the collapse.
evidence_log: 2026-05-28 (/ml-review): MES regime manifests failed again (no fresh MES dataset; blocked on MB-20260527-001). Last successful train 2026-05-26: mes-regime-15m macro_f1=0.329, mes-regime-5m macro_f1=0.345, both f1_volatile=0 (still collapsed to range-majority). NEW RECOMMENDATION: the three vol-bucket tweaks are now lower-priority than porting the winning v2 LightGBM recipe to MES. On BTC the LightGBM head lifted f1_volatile from 0 (modal baseline) to 0.228 (15m) / 0.111 (5m) on the same market_features schema with continuous vol + log-return features and inverse-base-rate class weighting (class_weight volatile:50). Propose mes-regime-{5m,15m}-lgbm-v2 manifests mirroring btc-regime-*-lgbm-v2 once MES data is wired (MB-20260527-001). This is the proven fix for the exact collapse symptom.

2026-05-28 (model-training, PR #2226): RESOLVED pending merge. Created mes-regime-{5m,15m}-lgbm-v2 (+ btc-regime-1h-lgbm-v2) and validated on the trainer against freshly-built median-calibrated MES data. f1_volatile went from 0 (collapsed modal baseline) to: mes-regime-5m-lgbm-v2 macro_f1=0.694 / f1_volatile=0.770 (n_eval=2170); mes-regime-15m-lgbm-v2 macro_f1=0.666 / f1_volatile=0.761 (n_eval=723); btc-regime-1h-lgbm-v2 macro_f1=0.637 / f1_volatile=0.490 (n_eval=4210). Collapse solved. MES eval sets are small (20% holdout of the ~60d yfinance window) — deeper IBKR history (MB-20260528-002) needed before any shadow->advisory promotion. Once merged the models register at shadow on the next cycle.

items.source: /ml-review 2026-05-27 — setup-quality-lgbm-v2 underperforms baseline at n=76.
items.title: Decision: setup-quality-lgbm-v2 (mae 0.071 vs baseline mae 0.048 at n=76) — hold for n>=1000 or demote
items.description: setup-quality-lgbm-v2 trained cleanly (PR #2053's LightGBM stack works end-to-end) but loses to setup-quality-baseline-v0 on the same n=76 eval: MAE 0.0710 vs 0.0478, MSE 0.0114 vs 0.0092. Cause is dataset size, not model quality — 76 closed trades is too small for a gradient-boosted tree to beat a simple per-group-mean baseline. The model is currently target_deployment_stage=shadow + auto-wired into the shadow factory (default-flip 2026-05-19), so it logs predictions on every live signal. That's fine for data collection but means it shouldn't be considered for shadow->advisory promotion until either (a) on a fresh cycle with n_eval >= ~1000 it beats baseline on MAE+MSE, OR (b) it's explicitly demoted to research_only via `python -m ml promote-stage setup-quality-lgbm-v2 --new-stage research_only` (semantic change to the registry, operator-approved). This is the ml-review-backlog mirror of BL-20260526-003 (health-review backlog) — same model, recorded here so /ml-review picks it up on each run.
items.trigger_condition: Each /ml-review run; revisit specifically when n_trades >= ~1000 closed trades suitable for setup_labels, OR a dedicated model-training session reviews the v2 setup-quality manifest.
items.resolution_criteria: Either (a) on a fresh training cycle with n_eval >= 1000 the v2 model beats the baseline on MAE+MSE, in which case promote toward advisory; OR (b) operator approves demotion to research_only (which would also stop the shadow predictions); OR (c) manifest is retired (removed from ml/configs/).
evidence_log: 2026-05-28 (/ml-review, run 20260528T000544Z): re-verified, still loses. setup-quality-lgbm-v2 mae=0.0701 mse=0.0112 vs setup-quality-baseline-v0 mae=0.0464 mse=0.0089 at n_eval=78 (was 76 on 5/27 — only +2 closed trades in a week). Dataset still ~13x below the ~1000 threshold needed for the GBT to beat the per-group-mean baseline. Hold; revisit when setup_labels n_eval crosses ~1000. Shadow predictions confirmed flowing (n=14 in 48-min tail, score range -0.007..0.007 — responsive, unlike the constant baselines).

2026-05-29 (/ml-review, cycle run 20260529T002940Z head=b149b15): unchanged — still loses. setup-quality-lgbm-v2 mae=0.07064 mse=0.01125 vs setup-quality-baseline-v0 mae=0.04619 mse=0.00892, both at n_eval=78 (zero new closed trades since 5/28). Shadow predictions confirmed flowing and responsive: count=438 since 2026-05-28, score_mean=-0.0031, range -0.0098..0.0074 — the only setup-family model that is NOT a constant (the three baselines emit fixed per-group means: setup-quality-baseline-v0 const -0.034, audit-baseline-v0 const -0.057). Hold; still ~13x below the n>=1000 threshold.

2026-05-30 (/ml-review): re-confirmed losing, and REFRAMED from 'wait' to 'decide'. setup-quality-lgbm-v2 still emits responsive shadow scores (latest tail range -0.0098..0.0074; e.g. -0.00098 @ 2026-05-29T23:58:42Z, the last write before the feed stalled) but at n_eval~78 it loses to the trivial per-group-mean baseline (mae ~0.0706 vs ~0.0462). At the observed +0..+2 closed-trades/week cadence, crossing n>=1000 is effectively years out, so 'hold for more data' is functionally 'hold forever' — this is the clearest model NOT moving in a useful direction. RECOMMENDATION: either (a) demote to research_only (operator-gated) to stop it auto-wiring onto strategies via the shadow default-flip and free the slot, or (b) keep it explicitly observe-only and drop n>=1000 as a realistic promotion path. Tier-3 — operator decides. (This is the answer to the operator's 2026-05-30 'which MLs aren't moving in the right direction' question for the setup-quality family.)

2026-06-01 (/ml-review): RESOLVED via option (b): setup-quality-lgbm-v2 manifest now declares target_deployment_stage: research_only (ml/configs/setup-quality-lgbm-v2.yaml:67, set in #2399). Registry confirms target_deployment_stage=research_only; it no longer auto-wires onto strategies and emits ZERO shadow predictions (absent from the live shadow_predictions tail this session). Fresh metrics still lose to baseline (mae 0.0627 vs setup-quality-baseline-v0 0.0426 at n=79) — moot now that it's research_only. The n>=1000 promotion path is dropped as unreachable. Closed.
items.status_history: open -> resolved 2026-06-01 (operator demoted to research_only via manifest target_deployment_stage: research_only, #2399). Option (b) of the decision taken.

items.source: /ml-review 2026-05-27 — v2 BTC LightGBM regime models tracking toward shadow→advisory promotion.
items.title: Promote btc-regime-1h-lgbm-yz-v1 shadow->advisory: re-run gate-check on/after 2026-06-12 (soak complete) under the merged regime gate profile + emit the operator go/no-go packet
items.description: btc-regime-15m-lgbm-v2 (macro_f1=0.568, f1_volatile=0.227, n=16834) and btc-regime-5m-lgbm-v2 (macro_f1=0.546) both beat their baselines (0.491 / 0.499 respectively — baselines collapse to majority with f1_volatile=0). After the 2026-05-26 retraining cycle they entered the current shadow stage at 16:08:23Z (15m) and 16:08:40Z (5m) per BL-20260526-001 — 7-day soak targets ~2026-06-02 16:08Z. Engineering work is fully landed; only the time-based promotion gates remain (shadow_soak >= 7 days; beats_baseline / live_agreement / drift_clean from accumulating live shadow predictions). When the soak completes, this skill should: (1) pull shadow_predictions.jsonl + verify entries exist for both model_ids tied to closed live BTCUSDT trades; (2) compute brier_lift, AUC, and the shadow-drift report so beats_baseline/live_agreement/drift_clean clear; (3) emit a promotion_recommendations[] entry with evidence; the operator decides shadow→advisory. Post-promotion monitoring moves to MB-20260614-001.
evidence_log: 2026-06-09 (/ml-review): HOLD stands — soak completes 2026-06-12, ~3 days out. Trainer infra confirmed HEALTHY via trainer-vm-diag #3054: ict-trainer.timer + ict-drift-retrain.timer + ict-promotion-readiness.timer all enabled+active; today's daily cycle ran clean (training_cycle.jsonl cycle_end 2026-06-09T00:25, overall_rc=0); ict-drift-retrain fired 2026-06-09T06:01:28Z across all deployed shadow heads and returned skip_no_drift for every one (mes-regime-1d-lgbm-v2, mes-regime-5m{-baseline-v1,-lgbm-v2}, prop-mission-policy-baseline-v0, setup-quality + trade-outcome baselines) — ADWIN found no cut, so no drift-triggered retrain is warranted on the yz family's neighbours either. NOTE: promotion-readiness dir runtime_logs/trainer_mirror/promotion_readiness/2026-06-09/ EXISTS but SUMMARY.md was absent at pull time (cat failed) — minor: confirm the daily promotion-readiness report writes SUMMARY.md (or adjust the expected filename) next /ml-review; tracked alongside MB-20260607-002. No gate-check run this review (would need a trainer-vm-diag sync+gate-check cycle; deferred to the on/after-6/12 action already specified). No promotion recommended.

2026-06-07 (session wrap): REGIME GATE PROFILE MERGED (PR #2919) + S15a trend-model tuned (PR #2928). Net state of the yz-1h promotion: oos_edge PASS +0.373 (leak-free purged-WF-CV), non_degenerate + cross_run_stability PASS. Under the now-live regime profile (gate-check auto-applies it) the ONLY remaining blockers are accrual: shadow_soak (2.3/7d -> completes ~2026-06-12T01:00Z), sample_sufficiency (now 2/5 live scored trades, was 2/200), live_agreement (needs >=1 winning AND >=1 LOSING scored BTC trade for AUC; both current scored closes are wins), drift_clean (needs the 2nd window). NEXT ACTION on/after 6/12: on the trainer, `bash scripts/ops/sync_trainer_data.sh` then `python -m ml gate-check btc-regime-1h-lgbm-yz-v1 --target-stage advisory --db data/trade_journal.db --shadow-log runtime_logs/shadow_predictions.jsonl --datasets-root datasets-out` (regime profile + modal baseline auto-selected); if ready=true, emit a promotion_recommendations[] packet and bring the operator the go/no-go (the shadow->advisory flip is Tier-3, operator-gated). HOLD until then.

2026-06-07 (push-ahead session, trainer-vm-diag #2911-#2921): GATE RE-CHECK with corrected paths + dataset + baseline. Engineering fully landed+deployed (S13/S15b/S17 on main); yz 1h per-bar emission HEALTHY (63 records hourly, newest 2026-06-07T06:00Z, full S17 parity feature_row incl yang_zhang_vol). Correct read paths: db=data/trade_journal.db (2493 BTC / 515 closed), shadow=runtime_logs/shadow_predictions.jsonl. market_features BTCUSDT/1h/v002 is CURRENT (builder v7, generated 2026-06-07T00:25Z, 43800 rows) -- NOT stale. gate-check btc-regime-1h-lgbm-yz-v1->advisory = READY:FALSE. QUALITY GATES NOW ALL PASS: non_degenerate PASS (min f1 0.480); oos_edge PASS +0.37254 macro_f1 over 5 purged WF-CV folds (candidate 0.66301 vs modal baseline 0.29047) -- decisive leak-free win; cross_run_stability PASS (std 0.0020/3 runs). REMAINING fails are ALL live-data/time accrual: shadow_soak 2.25/7d (->~2026-06-12); sample_sufficiency FAIL 2/200 live scored trades; live_agreement insufficient (n=2 scored live BTC closes, both WINS, AUC needs a loser); beats_baseline FAIL (brier_lift -0.167 but base-rate brier 0.0 -> degenerate at n=2); drift_clean insufficient (needs 2 windows). At BTC close cadence (~2-3/day) the 200-live-trade sample_sufficiency bar is WEEKS out -- the binding constraint, much longer than the 7-day soak. GATE-CHECK WIRING FINDING (BL-20260607-002): oos_edge silently returns insufficient for ANY multiclass regime head unless gate-check is invoked with --baseline-trainer ml.trainers.regime_classifier.RegimeClassifierTrainer; the default ConstantPredictionTrainer is incompatible with the multiclass evaluator -> None. OPEN DECISION for operator: with oos_edge passing decisively, is the 200-live-trade sample_sufficiency the right bar for a regime CLASSIFIER, or should the regime-head gate lean on oos_edge + soak + drift with a small live floor? HOLD stands; no promotion.

2026-06-06 (S-MLOPT-S8-FU, trainer-vm-diag #2907-#2908): REALIZED-JOIN UNBLOCK SHIPPED. scripts/ops/sync_trainer_data.sh now pulls the live VM's shadow_predictions.jsonl (+_backfill) into the trainer runtime_logs/ (same rsync pattern as trade_journal.db) so `python -m ml gate-check` can compute live_agreement + drift LOCALLY -- the /api/bot/trades/scores web-session unreachability (the standing blocker on this item) no longer applies. Verified: sync pulled 28,666 fresh shadow lines + 11,606 backfill; the yz head carries 51 per-bar records. gate-check btc-regime-1h-lgbm-yz-v1 -> advisory = READY:FALSE -- non_degenerate PASS (min per-class f1 0.483), sample_sufficiency PASS (n_eval 8760), shadow_soak FAIL 1.8/7d (stage entry 2026-06-05T00:59Z, young), beats_baseline/cross_run_stability/live_agreement/drift_clean insufficient. live_agreement now COMPUTES locally but is insufficient by DATA THINNESS not infra: model-attribution for the yz head returns [] -- the 51 records (~1.8d old) don't yet overlap a closed-BTC trade window (~0 closed BTC trades in that window). oos_edge insufficient even with --datasets-root (pre-existing: yz market_features v3 dataset not built in datasets-out; separate follow-up). NET: the honest-promotion-evidence infra the task asked for is wired + verified; HOLD stands -- let the post-6/5 soak + per-bar record accrue ~1-2 weeks, then re-run gate-check (the join will yield a real AUC once trades close inside the record window).

Blocking gates: shadow_soak FAIL 1.7/7d (stage entry 2026-06-05T00:59Z, stable across the 6/6 retrain -> daily re-registration is NOT resetting the soak clock, it is simply young); cross_run_stability insufficient (only 2 of 3 required runs carry macro_f1); live_agreement insufficient (need >=1 live winning AND >=1 losing scored trade for AUC); drift_clean insufficient (no two-window drift report). sample_sufficiency PASS (n_eval=8760). 2026-06-06 (/ml-review): UNBLOCKED but track record RESTARTED — still HOLD. Two prior blockers cleared: (a) per-bar emission is LIVE (MB-20260529-001 resolved — the 1h/15m heads now emit on their own bar cadence, confirmed in the 6/6 shadow tail); (b) feature parity is LIVE (MB-20260604-005 resolved — feature_row carries the full superset incl yang_zhang_vol). 2026-06-03 (/ml-review) — SOAK WINDOW CLOSED (6/2 16:08Z passed, ~14h ago); DECISION = HOLD the shadow->advisory promotion of btc-regime-5m-lgbm-v2. Three reasons: (1) WEAK SEPARATION — fresh 6/3 cycle (run 20260603T003501Z, n_eval=105173) gives macro_f1=0.5242 / f1_volatile=0.0692 (precision_volatile=0.037, recall_volatile=0.473), FLAT vs 6/1 (0.524/0.070); the volatile class this model exists to gate on is barely predicted (+0.025 macro_f1 over its collapsed baseline-v1 0.4992/f1_vol=0). Promoting a near-noise volatile signal to INFLUENCE orders is not justified. (2) The 5y-window degradation (MB-20260601-001) is unresolved — the window/recency sweep that should recover f1_volatile has not run. (3) REALIZED JOIN STILL UNREACHABLE — /api/bot/trades/scores is a /api/bot route, not /api/diag/*, so the web-session diag relay cannot fetch it; beats_baseline / AUC / live_agreement / drift_clean remain UNCOMPUTED on realized trades. Shadow FEED is healthy: btc-regime-5m-lgbm-v2 emitting + responsive in the live log (n~15 over 03:41->05:07Z 6/3, score 0.566..0.879 volatile-prob). STRUCTURAL NOTE — the promotion is blocked both ways: the only regime head with shadow evidence (5m) is the WEAKEST (f1_vol 0.069), while the strong heads (btc-regime-1h-lgbm-v2 f1_vol=0.447, mes-regime-1d-lgbm-v2 0.647, mes-regime-5m-lgbm-v2 0.547) emit ZERO shadow predictions by design (MB-20260529-001). So no regime model can presently clear an order-influencing promotion. The unblock is MB-20260601-001 (recover 5m f1_vol via window sweep) AND MB-20260529-001 (per-bar scoring so the strong heads accrue evidence). No promotion recommended.

2026-05-28 (/ml-review) progress check (soak still running, target ~6/2 16:08Z): (a) shadow predictions CONFIRMED flowing — btc-regime-5m-lgbm-v2 emitting in the live shadow log (n=10 in a 48-min tail window, score mean 0.918 = volatile-class prob); btc-regime-5m-baseline-v1 alongside it (mean 0.988). 15m variants fire on 15m cadence so absent from the short tail but are wired. (b) Training edge holds after today's retrain: btc-regime-15m-lgbm-v2 macro_f1=0.568 / f1_volatile=0.228 vs baseline 0.491 / 0.000; btc-regime-5m-lgbm-v2 macro_f1=0.547 / f1_volatile=0.111 vs baseline 0.499 / 0.000 (n_eval 16853 / 50568). Packet still pending soak completion; no promotion recommended this run.

2026-05-29 (/ml-review) progress check (soak target ~6/2 16:08Z, ~4 days out): (a) Training edge HOLDS, flat run-over-run (cycle b149b15): btc-regime-15m-lgbm-v2 macro_f1=0.5676 / f1_volatile=0.2259 vs baseline-v1 0.4909 / 0.000 (n=16872); btc-regime-5m-lgbm-v2 macro_f1=0.5455 / f1_volatile=0.1066 vs baseline-v1 0.4989 / 0.000 (n=50626). btc-regime-1h-lgbm-v2 is the strongest BTC regime: macro_f1=0.6327 / f1_volatile=0.4830 (n=4214). (b) **SHADOW-FEED ANOMALY — soak evidence at risk:** in shadow_stats since 2026-05-28 the btc-regime-5m {baseline,lgbm} models last_seen=2026-05-28T22:29:34Z (count=362) — they STOPPED emitting ~15.7h before the 14:10Z pull, while all 7 trade-family shadow models kept emitting through 2026-05-29T14:10Z (count=438). The 15m + 1h v2 regime variants emit ZERO shadow predictions at all (not in the shadow log) — likely not wired into the live shadow factory, or a regime-feed stall. This compromises the 6/2 promotion soak: the order-influencing evidence the gate needs is not continuously accumulating. Flagged for /health-review (live-VM pipeline plumbing). (c) Realized join still NOT computable (no /api/bot/trades/scores diag mirror; only shadow_stats aggregates reachable from a web session). No promotion recommended this run; the soak should not be considered clean until the regime shadow feed is confirmed continuous.

2026-05-30 (/ml-review): SOAK COMPROMISED — escalated from the 5/29 anomaly. shadow_predictions.jsonl stopped writing ENTIRELY at 2026-05-29T23:58:42Z and was still silent ~7.75h later at the 07:43Z pull (file size unchanged 5,481,574 bytes across two pulls), while the live trader is healthy + ticking (heartbeat age 74s label=running, last_tick 2026-05-30T07:43:09Z, bot_uptime 39859s ~11h continuous — NO restart). So no shadow evidence has accumulated for btc-regime-{5m,15m}-lgbm-v2 (or ANY model) since 23:58Z. The last write being ~1 min before 00:00Z UTC points at a UTC-midnight log-rollover / dated-path bug in the shadow writer. The 6/2 16:08Z soak cannot be met on a continuous-evidence basis until the write path is fixed; recommend resetting the soak clock once continuous shadow logging is restored. Plumbing → flagged to /health-review. (Trainer-VM relay #2400 was queued behind PR #2399 CI and had not returned at synthesis time; training metrics carried from cycle b149b15.)

2026-05-30 (/health-review) CORRECTION — the 'SOAK COMPROMISED / write-path regression' entry above is WRONG; retracted. Root-caused via the live audit relay: shadow predictions are emitted ONLY as a side-effect of an actionable buy/sell signal (src/runtime/strategy_signal_builders.py::_emit_shadow_preds), NOT per-tick. The shadow writer path is static (ml/shadow/factory.py DEFAULT_LOG_PATH, no date) and rotate_shadow_log.py no-ops below 100 MiB/7d (file was 5.5 MB) — so there is NO midnight-rollover bug. The audit tail 2026-05-30T07:52→08:05Z shows ALL 6 strategies × {BTCUSDT,MES} eval to side=none every ~75s with legitimate reasons (vwap: 'regime_policy_skip: regime=sideways/low'; donchian within channel; fade ADX 34-35 not chop; squeeze no release; turtle/ict_scalp no sweep), zero exceptions, trader healthy + ticking. So the ~8h shadow gap = ~8h with no actionable signal in a quiet sideways/low-vol market (vwap regime-gated), which is CORRECT behaviour. The 6/2 soak is NOT broken — but note it accrues on SIGNAL CADENCE, not wall-clock, so a quiet regime simply slows evidence accumulation. Lesson logged: do not treat the event-driven shadow log as a heartbeat.

2026-06-01 (/ml-review): PROGRESS (soak completes 2026-06-02T16:08Z, ~1 day out): TRAINING EDGE DEGRADED after the 5y-window widening (#2399). btc-regime-5m-lgbm-v2 now macro_f1=0.5244 / f1_volatile=0.0696 (n=105173) vs ~0.546/0.107 (n=50626) on 5/29 — edge over its collapsed baseline (macro_f1 0.4992, f1_vol 0) is now only +0.025 macro_f1 and the volatile-class f1 is ~0.07. 15m similarly weakened (0.568->0.511, f1_vol 0.226->0.144); 1h is the strongest BTC regime model (macro_f1 0.609, f1_vol 0.444) but is NOT the soak model and emits zero shadow predictions (MB-20260529-001). Shadow feed CONFIRMS btc-regime-5m-lgbm-v2 emitting + responsive (score 0.845-0.876 in the tail). RECOMMENDATION: HOLD the 6/2 shadow->advisory promotion — (a) the 5m training edge weakened on the wider window, (b) the realized-trade join (/api/bot/trades/scores) is not reachable via the diag relay this session so beats_baseline/AUC/drift remain uncomputed, (c) only the 5m variant accrues shadow evidence. Re-evaluate at the 6/2 window with the realized join + reconsider the training window (see new MB-20260601-001).

2026-06-13 (/ml-review): GATE-CHECK RUN (soak now complete 6/12). `ml gate-check btc-regime-1h-lgbm-yz-v1 --target-stage advisory --baseline-trainer RegimeClassifierTrainer` (trainer #3465, post sync_trainer_data.sh; shadow log 52,696 lines / 293 yz records) => READY:FALSE. PASSING gates: shadow_soak PASS 8.2d (>=7, since 2026-06-05T00:59Z); oos_edge PASS +0.37561 macro_f1 (cand 0.66605 vs modal baseline 0.29043, 5 purged WF-CV folds) — decisive; non_degenerate PASS (min per-class f1 0.480); cross_run_stability PASS (std 0.0029/9 runs); drift_clean PASS (KS 0.135<=0.2, PSI 0.204<=0.25). REMAINING BLOCKERS are BOTH live-outcome accrual: sample_sufficiency FAIL 3/5 live closed BTC trades scored; live_agreement FAIL rank-AUC 0.000 (3 scored closes lack a winner+loser mix). beats_baseline FAIL (brier_lift -0.128) but NOT required. ROOT CAUSE of the stall = the REAL-MONEY BTC BOOK IS FLAT (bybit_2 pinned at 0.001 BTC target, 0 live closed BTC trades in the 6-day window), so the 2 live-outcome gates cannot accrue. Model-quality side is promotion-ready; HOLD pending live closed-BTC-trade flow. No promotion recommended.

2026-06-13 /ml-review: soak COMPLETE+PASS (8.2d), oos_edge PASS +0.376; only sample_sufficiency 3/5 + live_agreement AUC 0.0 block — both stuck on the flat real-money BTC book. HOLD.

items.source: /ml-review 2026-05-28 — registry sweep found an orphaned MES daily-regime model.
items.title: Orphan model mes-regime-classifier-baseline-v0 (MES/1d) — never retrains, no active manifest; wire a recurring 1d manifest or deprecate
items.description: mes-regime-classifier-baseline-v0 sits at stage=shadow with a single history entry ('initial registration', run 2026-05-22T09:05:39Z, code_rev c0b14ff1) and has not retrained in 6 days. It is NOT in the daily cycle's 17-manifest set (those are mes-regime-5m / mes-regime-15m at 5m/15m, plus the BTC/global set) — so it is an orphan left over from an earlier experiment. Notably its frozen metrics are the BEST of any MES regime model: macro_f1=0.685, accuracy=0.748, f1_volatile=0.543, n_eval=1292 on the MES/1d market_features dataset — i.e. on the DAILY timeframe the regime label IS separable (unlike the 5m/15m models that collapse to f1_volatile=0, MB-20260527-002). Two clean resolutions: (a) if the 1d MES regime signal is worth keeping, add an ml/configs/mes-regime-1d.yaml to the daily cycle so it retrains on fresh data and stays current; (b) if it was a one-off probe, deprecate it (promote-stage research_only or remove) so it stops occupying a shadow slot and auto-wiring onto strategies via the default-flip. Either way it should not linger as a stale shadow model.
items.trigger_condition: Next /ml-review run; or any model-training session touching MES manifests.
items.resolution_criteria: Either (a) ml/configs/mes-regime-1d.yaml exists and mes-regime-classifier-baseline-v0 (or its successor) shows fresh retrains in the cycle log; OR (b) operator approves deprecation (promote-stage research_only / manifest removal) and the model no longer sits at shadow.
evidence_log: 2026-05-29 (/ml-review, cycle b149b15): still orphaned, still not retraining. mes-regime-classifier-baseline-v0 stage=research_only (NOTE: now research_only, not shadow as the 5/28 entry recorded — it does not occupy a shadow slot, lowering urgency), last train still 2026-05-22T09:05:39Z (run 20260522T090539Z, code_rev c0b14ff1), 7 days stale, NOT in the 20-manifest daily set. Its frozen metrics remain the best of any MES regime model: macro_f1=0.6845, accuracy=0.7477, f1_volatile=0.5434, n_eval=1292 on MES/1d v001 market_features. The 1d timeframe separates the regime label where 5m/15m collapse the modal baseline. Now that native IBKR MES history is wired (MB-20260528-002 resolved), a recurring mes-regime-1d.yaml on v002 would let this signal retrain on fresh deep data. Converted to experiments_proposed[] (new_manifest: mes-regime-1d) this run; leaving open until the manifest lands or the operator deprecates.

2026-06-01 (/ml-review): RESOLVED via (a): ml/configs/mes-regime-1d-lgbm-v2.yaml landed (#2399, 2026-05-30) and retrained in today's cycle (run 2026-06-01T00:18:26Z) at target_deployment_stage=shadow with STRONG metrics — macro_f1=0.6555, f1_volatile=0.6525, accuracy=0.6555, n_eval=569. Confirms the 1d timeframe separates the regime label (as predicted), now on a recurring manifest with fresh data. The old orphan mes-regime-classifier-baseline-v0 stays research_only/frozen (last train 2026-05-22, n=1292) and is functionally superseded by the 1d lgbm successor; left as-is (research_only, not occupying a shadow slot).
