items.source: Operator asked why MES isn't getting better (IBKR) data during the 2026-05-28 model-training session.
items.title: Deeper MES history: run the IBKR historical pull ON the live VM (shares the one IB gateway) and sync to trainer; resolve the Error 162 session conflict
items.description: MES regime models currently train on a rolling ~60-day ES=F 5m window from yfinance — the deepest intraday history yfinance serves and the only source reachable from the trainer VM. IBKR would give multi-year 1m MES history, but the architecture blocks it: (1) IB Gateway is a loopback-only socket on the LIVE VM (127.0.0.1:4002 via the gnzsnz socat relay) — the trainer VM cannot reach it. (2) IBKR permits exactly ONE logged-in session per username, so we can't run a second gateway on the trainer without disconnecting the live MES trader. The ibkr_offvm adapter (ml/datasets/adapters/ibkr_offvm.py) is therefore designed to run ON the live VM (guard ICT_IB_HISTORICAL_OK=1), sharing the live gateway on a DISTINCT clientId, paced gently (~12s between chunked reqHistoricalData calls), then the resulting market_raw shards sync to the trainer. (3) Current blocker: run_serious_baseline.sh's MES leg is gated behind MES_IBKR=1 (default 0) and was disabled 2026-05-22 because the gateway returned IBKR Error 162 'session connected from a different IP'. The 2026-05-28 IB-gateway auto-heal watchdog (ict-ib-gateway-watchdog) mitigates the overnight reset-wedge but does not address the historical-pull session conflict. PROPOSED PATH (Tier-2/3, live-VM, operator-gated): add a paced, low-priority IBKR historical-pull job on the LIVE VM (distinct clientId e.g. 450, useRTH=false, pause_s>=12) that writes market_raw/MES/{5m,15m,1m}/v002 and is picked up by the trainer sync; verify it does not perturb the live trading clientId; resolve why Error 162 fires (likely clientId collision or the gateway only accepting one source IP). Until then the ~60d yfinance window is the working fallback and the MES LightGBM models train on it.
items.trigger_condition: Operator wants deeper/cleaner MES training history; or when the IB gateway session conflict (Error 162) is investigated.
items.resolution_criteria: market_raw/MES/{5m,15m} at v002 carries multi-year (or at least multi-month) IBKR-sourced bars synced to the trainer, built without disconnecting or degrading the live MES trading session; mes-regime models retrain on it. OR operator decides the ~60d yfinance window is sufficient and this is closed as won't-do.
evidence_log: 2026-05-28 (model-training): root-caused + mechanism drafted. Live IB gateway is HEALTHY (watchdog probes report healthy=True net_liquidation=1000600.6 every 5 min) — the 2026-05-22 Error 162 was NOT a gateway fault; it came from attempting the pull from the TRAINER (different IP, loopback gateway unreachable / IBKR source-IP guard). Fix = run the pull ON the live VM sharing the one session on a distinct clientId. Drafted (operator-gated, NOT executed): scripts/ops/pull_mes_ibkr_history.sh (live-VM paced ibkr_offvm pull, clientId 450 vs exec 497, pause_s=15, use_rth=false, ~365d MES 5m+15m -> $DATA_DIR/ibkr_datasets); sync_trainer_data.sh extended to rsync those shards to the trainer; build_mes_market prefers the synced IBKR base over yfinance when >1000 rows present. REMAINING (operator): deploy to live VM via pull-and-deploy, then run the script in the CME maintenance/weekend window (shares the gateway pacing budget with live MES candle reqs); verify it does not perturb live trading; confirm the trainer picks up the deeper history on the next cycle.

2026-05-28 (model-training): RESOLVED end-to-end. Operator approved a now-run. pull-mes-ibkr-history system-action added (#2231) + fixed for the live VM's system python / canonical log path / flock single-instance (#2238). Ran on the live VM: clientId 450, paced 20s, system python, live trader heartbeat stayed fresh throughout (zero disruption). Pulled native MES via IBKR: market_raw/MES/5m=16197 rows, 15m=5383, to /data/bot-data/ibkr_datasets. Kicked a cycle: build_mes_market logged 'using synced IBKR MES market_raw (deep history) instead of yfinance' and built MES features from it (5m=16173, 15m=5359, median vol_threshold 5m=0.00056/15m=0.00093); cycle_end rc=0. MES regime models retrained on NATIVE IBKR data: mes-regime-5m-lgbm-v2 macro_f1=0.621 / f1_volatile=0.547 (n=3235); mes-regime-15m-lgbm-v2 macro_f1=0.520 / f1_volatile=0.517 (n=1072) — lower than the earlier ES=F-proxy numbers but HONEST on real data, and still well above the collapsed modal baselines (f1_volatile=0). Depth caveat: ~16k 5m rows (~6mo) for a 1y request — IBKR pages contracts newest-first capped at max_contracts=4; PR #2247 adds MES_MAX_CONTRACTS=8 to page the older 2025 expiries (depth still ultimately capped by IBKR per-contract intraday retention). Item closed; further depth tuning tracked on #2247.

items.source: /ml-review 2026-05-29 — shadow_stats sweep found the higher-timeframe v2 regime models emit zero shadow predictions while only the 5m regime models log (and those stopped at 22:29Z).
items.title: Regime-model shadow coverage: btc/mes-regime-{15m,1h}-lgbm-v2 emit ZERO shadow predictions — wire them into the live shadow factory so they accumulate a track record before any promotion
items.description: shadow_stats since 2026-05-28 returns only 9 model_ids: the 7 trade/setup/exec/prop families (signal-driven, count=438) plus btc-regime-5m-{baseline-v1,lgbm-v2} (count=362). The 15m + 1h v2 regime variants (btc-regime-15m-lgbm-v2, btc-regime-1h-lgbm-v2, mes-regime-{5m,15m}-lgbm-v2) emit NOTHING into shadow_predictions.jsonl — so the strongest BTC regime model (btc-regime-1h-lgbm-v2, macro_f1=0.6327 / f1_volatile=0.4830) and the now-honest MES regime models have no order-influencing track record at all. Separately, the 5m regime models last_seen=2026-05-28T22:29:34Z (stopped ~15.7h before the 14:10Z pull while every signal-driven model continued) — a possible regime-feed stall (that half is /health-review's plumbing domain; logged there too via the recommended_action). ML-coverage gap to own here: confirm which regime model_ids the live shadow factory actually invokes (the default-flip auto-wires shadow models when strategy YAML omits shadow_model_ids, but the regime predictors fire on a market-bar cadence — verify the 15m/1h predictors are actually being called) and extend coverage so all shadow-stage regime models log predictions. Without continuous shadow logging on the higher timeframes, the 6/2 shadow->advisory soak (MB-20260527-004) can only ever evidence the 5m variant.
items.trigger_condition: Next /ml-review run; or any session investigating why a shadow-stage regime model has zero predictions.
items.resolution_criteria: shadow_stats shows non-zero, continuously-updating counts for btc-regime-{15m,1h}-lgbm-v2 and mes-regime-{5m,15m}-lgbm-v2 (last_seen tracking now), so each shadow-stage regime model accumulates an order-influencing track record; OR it is confirmed by-design that only the 5m regime feeds the shadow factory and the higher timeframes are documented as observe-via-training-metrics-only.
evidence_log: 2026-06-04 (S-MLOPT-S13 VERIFIED LIVE — RESOLVED): PR #2778 merged + deployed; diag pull (/api/diag/log_file?name=shadow_predictions, issue #2785) confirms the running trader now writes event_source=per_bar records for EVERY shadow-stage regime head on its own bar cadence — btc-regime-{5m,15m,1h}-lgbm-v2 + baseline-v1, mes-regime-{5m,15m,1d}-lgbm-v2 + baseline-v1 — deduped one-per-closed-bar (1h ~hourly, 1d ~daily, 5m ~5-min). The strong heads (btc-regime-1h-lgbm-v2, mes-regime-1d-lgbm-v2) that had ZERO track record now accrue order-influencing evidence. Resolution_criteria met. CAVEAT (separate item MB-20260604-005): the per-bar row is under-featured vs training (vol_bucket + close-to-close vol only) — the track record must not be the SOLE basis for a shadow→advisory promotion until train/serve parity closes. /ml-review now tracks the accruing per-head volume + drift.

2026-06-03 (/ml-review): STILL OPEN, re-confirmed and now the GATING blocker for any regime promotion. Live shadow_predictions tail (03:41->05:07Z 6/3) carries exactly 8 emitting models: btc-regime-5m-{baseline-v1,lgbm-v2} + the 6 decision baselines (execution/prop/setup-quality/setup-audit/trade-outcome-global/trade-outcome-winrate). ZERO predictions from btc-regime-{15m,1h}-lgbm-v2 or any mes-regime-* — including the strongest heads (btc-regime-1h-lgbm-v2 macro_f1=0.610/f1_vol=0.447 n=8760; mes-regime-1d-lgbm-v2 0.645/0.647 n=569; mes-regime-5m-lgbm-v2 0.621/0.547 n=3235). Confirmed by-design: _emit_shadow_preds fires only on an actionable signal whose (symbol,timeframe) matches the regime model, and live actionable signals are ~all 5m BTC vwap shorts. Net: the regime heads that actually separate the volatile regime have NO order-influencing track record, while the one that emits (5m, f1_vol 0.069) is the weakest — this is exactly why the 6/2 soak (MB-20260527-004) cannot be cleared. Option (a) per-bar regime-scoring path independent of signal emission remains the genuine fix; surfaced again as experiments_proposed this run.

2026-05-30 (/ml-review): ESCALATION — the gap is no longer just the higher-TF regime models; the ENTIRE shadow feed stalled. shadow_predictions.jsonl last write 2026-05-29T23:58:42Z, still silent ~7.75h later (07:43Z pull), file size unchanged, while the trader is healthy + ticking (last_tick 07:43:09Z, uptime ~11h, no restart). So all 9 previously-emitting shadow models (the 7 trade/setup/exec/prop families + btc-regime-5m {baseline,lgbm}) stopped, AND the 15m/1h/MES regime variants were already emitting zero. Last-write ~1 min before UTC midnight => strong UTC-midnight log-rollover / dated-path hypothesis for the shadow writer. Two things to fix, in order: (1) the write-path stall (plumbing → /health-review; verify whether post-midnight records land in a dated file the canonical reader misses); (2) the original coverage gap — confirm the 15m/1h/MES regime predictors are actually invoked per market-bar and wire them into the shadow factory so each shadow-stage regime model accumulates a track record. Until (1) is fixed no model accumulates anything.

2026-05-30 (/health-review) CORRECTION — the 'ENTIRE shadow feed stalled' framing above is WRONG; retracted. The full-feed silence was benign (no actionable signals in a quiet sideways market; vwap regime-gated 'regime=sideways/low'). Verified via the live audit relay (07:52->08:05Z all evals side=none, zero exceptions, trader healthy). There is NO write-path stall. WHAT REMAINS VALID: the 15m/1h/MES regime models emit zero shadow predictions BY DESIGN — _emit_shadow_preds only fires on an actionable signal, and feature_row_for_predictor (src/runtime/regime_shadow.py) returns None for a regime model whose (symbol,timeframe) does not match the firing signal's. Since live actionable signals are ~all 5m (vwap/ict_scalp), only btc-regime-5m ever gets scored; the 15m/1h/MES regime heads can never accumulate a shadow track record through this path. Options: (a) add a per-bar regime-scoring path independent of signal emission so 15m/1h regime models log on their own bar cadence, or (b) document them as evaluate-via-training-metrics-only and don't gate their promotion on shadow evidence. That coverage gap is the genuine open item; the 'stall' was the false part.

2026-06-01 (/ml-review): Re-confirmed STILL OPEN this session. Live shadow_predictions tail carries only btc-regime-5m {baseline-v1, lgbm-v2} among regime models; btc-regime-{15m,1h}-lgbm-v2 and all mes-regime-* (incl. the new strong mes-regime-1d-lgbm-v2 macro_f1=0.656/f1_vol=0.653 and the best-BTC btc-regime-1h-lgbm-v2 f1_vol=0.444) emit ZERO shadow predictions — by design, _emit_shadow_preds only fires on an actionable signal whose (symbol,timeframe) matches the regime model, and live actionable signals are ~all 5m. So the strongest regime heads still have no order-influencing track record. Option (a) (a per-bar regime-scoring path independent of signal emission) remains the genuine fix; gains urgency now that the regime ROUTER (PERF-20260601-002, shipped today) makes the regime stream a first-class signal and phase-4 wants a classifier detector.

2026-06-04 (S-MLOPT-S13): OPTION (a) BUILT — the per-bar regime-scoring path. New src/runtime/regime_bar_scoring.py::emit_regime_bar_predictions, called once per tick from run_pipeline (src/runtime/pipeline.py), scores every shadow-stage REGIME head on its own (symbol,timeframe) bar cadence independent of any signal: discovers shadow-stage models, keeps the regime heads, fetches each head's own candles (connector_for_symbol → BTC/Bybit, MES/IBKR), reuses regime_shadow.feature_row_for_predictor (live vol_bucket vs frozen edges), and writes to the same shadow_predictions.jsonl. Observe-only (only ShadowPredictor.predict; no order-path reach), deduped to one record per closed bar (write-rate control), never raises, kill-switch REGIME_BAR_SCORING_DISABLED. 19 unit tests + 39 reused-helper tests pass; ruff clean. Tier-2 — DRAFT PR opened on claude/m14-progress-next-u260C, pending operator approval before merge+deploy. RESOLUTION pending on-VM verification: after deploy, confirm via /api/diag/log_file?name=shadow_predictions that btc-regime-{1h,15m}-lgbm-* + mes-regime-* now emit non-zero, continuously-updating counts. NOTE: per-bar evidence is computed under the SAME under-featured live row as the signal-time path (vol_bucket + one vol value; range-vol/lag/time features absent → NaN; yz heads' yang_zhang_vol computed as close-to-close) — a pre-existing train/serve parity gap now tracked as MB-20260604-005; close it before promoting any head shadow→advisory on this evidence.
items.status_history: | 2026-06-04 (S-MLOPT-S13): option (a) per-bar regime scoring path BUILT (draft PR, Tier-2, pending operator approval). Item stays OPEN until on-VM verification shows the strong heads emitting non-zero continuous counts; /ml-review to confirm + close. Spawned MB-20260604-005 (feature parity) as the validity prerequisite for any shadow→advisory promotion on this evidence.

items.source: S-MLOPT-S13 — building the per-bar regime scoring path surfaced that live regime scoring is computed under a far smaller feature set than the heads train on.
items.title: Regime-head train/serve feature parity (Phase 4.2): live scoring computes vol_bucket + one close-to-close vol value; heads train on range-vol estimators + log-return lags + time features — and the yz heads' frozen vol_feature_column (yang_zhang_vol) is computed live as close-to-close vol
items.description: src/runtime/regime_shadow.feature_row_for_predictor builds the live regime feature row as base_row + {feature_column: vol_bucket, vol_feature_column: rolling_log_return_vol value}. But the lgbm-v2 / yz regime heads' feature_columns are ~11: vol_bucket, rolling_log_return_vol, parkinson_vol, garman_klass_vol, rogers_satchell_vol, yang_zhang_vol, log_return, log_return_lag_1, log_return_lag_2, hour_of_day, dayofweek. All but vol_bucket + the single vol value are ABSENT live → LightGBM imputes NaN (handled natively, but the served vector differs from the trained one). Worse for the S9 yz heads: their frozen vol_feature_column is yang_zhang_vol, yet the live path computes plain close-to-close rolling_log_return_vol and labels it as that column AND buckets it against yang-zhang-derived edges → the live vol_bucket itself can be wrong for the yz heads. This is PRE-EXISTING (the signal-time path + the v2 heads have the same gap); S13 deliberately reused the same computation so per-bar == signal-time. FIX (Phase 4.2 train/serve parity): in the live scoring path compute (a) the range-vol estimator named by vol_feature_column from OHLC (reuse ml/datasets/volatility_estimators.py — the S9 module), and (b) the log-return lags + hour_of_day/dayofweek, so the served feature vector matches training. IMPACT: until this lands, the per-bar shadow track record S13 generates for the regime heads (incl. the yz heads we want to promote) is computed under-featured, so it must NOT be the sole basis for a shadow→advisory promotion.
items.trigger_condition: Before promoting any regime head shadow→advisory on shadow-prediction evidence; or when Phase 4.2 (train/serve parity) is taken up; or next /ml-review.
items.resolution_criteria: The live regime feature row (regime_shadow + regime_bar_scoring) computes the same feature set the head trained on — at minimum the vol estimator named by vol_feature_column (range-vol via volatility_estimators.py) plus the log-return-lag + time features — verified by a parity test comparing a live-computed row to the training builder's row for the same bar; OR it is documented + accepted that regime heads are scored on vol_bucket only and their manifests are reduced to that feature set.
evidence_log: 2026-06-06 (/ml-review): RESOLVED — feature parity confirmed DEPLOYED + live. Live shadow_predictions tail (#2868, 04:58->05:36Z 6/6) shows btc-regime-5m-lgbm-yz-v1 records whose feature_row carries the FULL market_features superset (rolling_log_return_vol + parkinson_vol + garman_klass_vol + rogers_satchell_vol + yang_zhang_vol + log_return + log_return_lag_1/2 + hour_of_day + dayofweek + vol_bucket), not the old vol_bucket-only row. The S17 fix is in the running trader (sha 5fbee10). The yz heads now accrue a parity-correct shadow track record; promotion reads use post-deploy records. Closed.

2026-06-04 (S-MLOPT-S17): FIX SHIPPED (draft PR, Tier-2, observe-only). src/runtime/regime_shadow.feature_row_for_predictor now emits the full market_features superset (rolling_log_return_vol + parkinson/garman_klass/rogers_satchell/yang_zhang_vol + log_return + log_return_lag_1/2 + hour_of_day/dayofweek) computed live from the trailing vol_window_n OHLC window (new ohlc_from_candles + range_vol_estimators reusing ml/datasets/volatility_estimators.py + log_return_series + _latest_bar_hour_dow), and buckets vol_bucket against the value of the estimator NAMED BY the head's frozen vol_feature_column — so the v2 heads bucket rolling_log_return_vol and the yz heads bucket yang_zhang_vol (the core bug: close-to-close vol bucketed against yz-unit edges). Both callers (_emit_shadow_preds signal-time + regime_bar_scoring per-bar) pass candles_df= so per-bar == signal-time; a close-only fallback preserves legacy/test callers. RESOLUTION CRITERION MET: parity test (tests/runtime/test_regime_shadow_parity.py) uses MarketFeaturesBuilder itself as the oracle — the live row == the builder's row bit-for-bit (rel 1e-9) for the continuous features + a test proving the yz-bucket fix. 176 tests pass; ruff clean. CAVEATS: (1) status=resolved_pending_deploy — the track record accrued BEFORE the S17 deploy is still under-featured; promotion reads must use the record accrued AFTER pull-and-deploy. (2) The yz vol_bucket is an approximation by construction (the frozen yz edges are the per-bucket max of yang_zhang_vol where the bucket was defined by rolling_log_return_vol quantiles → monotonic-ish, not exact); the v2 heads are exact. NEXT: re-deploy, let the parity-correct record accrue, then /ml-review the strong heads (btc-regime-1h, mes-regime-1d) for a shadow->advisory promotion proposal (Tier-3, operator-gated).

items.source: Operator 2026-05-30: 'are the journal-decision models also trained on backtest results? can we backtest strategies to create more data for those models?'
items.title: Augment the journal-backed decision models (setup_quality, trade_outcomes) with PER-TRADE backtest rows to break the n~78 data wall
items.description: STATE TODAY: the journal-backed families explicitly EXCLUDE backtests. ml/datasets/families/setup_labels.py + trade_outcomes.py source trade_journal.db::trades filtered to CLOSED + non-backtest + non-null pnl (setup_labels also requires non-empty setup_type). The only backtest family, backtest_results.py, reads the AGGREGATE backtest_results table (run-level win_rate/sharpe/total_pnl) — NOT per-trade labeled setups. So today these models train ONLY on the bot's live trades (~78), which is exactly why setup-quality-lgbm-v2 loses to its per-group-mean baseline (MB-20260527-003). FEASIBILITY: YES for setup_quality + trade_outcomes (both predict win/R from setup features available at signal time) — a 5y backtest over each strategy yields thousands of labeled setups vs 78 live. NOT for execution_quality (it learns real slippage/fills that backtests don't model — synthetic fills would poison it). REQUIRED WORK: (1) have the backtest harnesses (scripts/backtest_*.py + src/backtest) emit PER-TRADE rows in the setup_labels/trade_outcomes schema (entry/SL/TP/outcome/r_multiple + the same signal-time features), not just aggregate metrics; (2) persist them tagged source=backtest; (3) extend the families with a source column + a builder flag to include backtest rows; (4) TRAIN on live+backtest but EVALUATE on a real-trade holdout ONLY, so metrics reflect live generalization not backtest fill artifacts; (5) keep leakage discipline (features at signal time only). CAVEAT to document: backtest outcomes depend on fill assumptions (TP/SL touch, no slippage/funding/partial fills), so the augmented signal is 'would this setup hit TP before SL under idealized fills' — directionally useful, tag it. PAIRS WITH: the new 5y BTC window (PR #2399) and the FVG-strategy backtesting work — every strategy backtest becomes training data.
items.trigger_condition: Next /ml-review; or any model-training / strategy-backtesting session with capacity to wire per-trade backtest output into the dataset families.
items.resolution_criteria: setup_labels / trade_outcomes can be built with an include-backtest flag that adds source-tagged per-trade backtest rows; a training run shows setup-quality (or a successor) beating the per-group-mean baseline on a REAL-trade holdout with the augmented train set; execution_quality explicitly excluded. OR operator decides synthetic augmentation isn't worth the domain-shift risk and closes won't-do.
check_log: 2026-06-10 /ml-review: Need RE-CONFIRMED: mes-setup-quality + mes-trade-outcome-winrate skip empty_dataset every cycle (incl. 06-10). Promoted the per-trade-backtest-row augmentation to experiments_proposed this review.

items.source: /ml-review 2026-06-01 — fresh cycle metrics showed the 5y-window widening (#2399) degraded all three btc-regime-*-lgbm-v2 models run-over-run.
items.title: BTC regime lgbm-v2 models DEGRADED on the 5y window (#2399) — sweep the training-window length / recency weighting to recover f1_volatile
items.description: After #2399 widened the BTCUSDT training window to a rolling 5 years, all three btc-regime-*-lgbm-v2 models lost headline metric run-over-run even as n_eval ~doubled: 5m macro_f1 0.546->0.524 / f1_volatile 0.107->0.070 (n 50626->105173); 15m 0.568->0.511 / 0.226->0.144 (n 16872->35054); 1h 0.633->0.609 / 0.483->0.444 (n 4214->8760). The extra (older) history appears to dilute the recent volatility regime the models had been separating — a classic stationarity/regime-shift tradeoff. This directly weakens the 6/2 shadow->advisory soak candidate (btc-regime-5m-lgbm-v2, MB-20260527-004), whose volatile-class f1 is now ~0.07. EXPERIMENT: sweep the train-window length (e.g. 1y / 2y / 3y / 5y) and/or add recency weighting (sample_weight decaying with age, or LightGBM's time-decay) on the btc-regime manifests, evaluating on a FIXED recent holdout, to find the window that maximizes f1_volatile on recent data. If a shorter/recency-weighted window restores f1_volatile, propose it as the manifest default before promoting any regime model past shadow.
items.trigger_condition: Next /ml-review; or any model-training session with capacity to sweep the btc-regime training window.
items.resolution_criteria: A window/recency-weighting sweep identifies a configuration whose btc-regime-{5m,15m,1h}-lgbm-v2 f1_volatile on a fixed recent holdout beats the current 5y-window numbers; OR it's confirmed the 5y window is optimal and the degradation is accepted with rationale documented.
evidence_log: 2026-06-06 (/ml-review): RESOLVED — the recency-decay config adopted via merged #2679 (sample_weight 1h=90d/15m=60d/5m=60d) has materialised in the registry. Sweep found the per-model winners and they shipped; the experiment is complete. (Note: the live 5m head's f1_vol stays structurally low ~0.07-0.11 on the imbalanced 5m regime, but that's the regime base-rate, not the window — the recency sweep recovered what was recoverable. Further 5m gains now come from features (S9 yz already shipped) not window.) Closed; the shadow->advisory promotion is the separate operator-gated step.

2026-06-03 (/ml-review): re-confirmed, FLAT day-over-day (same 5y window, +1 day data) and now the gating experiment for the 5m soak (MB-20260527-004). 6/3 cycle (run 20260603T0035xxZ): btc-regime-5m-lgbm-v2 macro_f1=0.5242/f1_vol=0.0692 (n=105173); 15m 0.5106/0.1439 (n=35054); 1h 0.6096/0.4466 (n=8760). All match 6/1 within noise — the 5y window's dilution of the recent volatility regime persists. The 5m volatile-f1 sitting at ~0.07 is the single biggest reason the 5m shadow->advisory promotion is held. The window/recency sweep (1y/2y/3y/5y + age-decay sample_weight, evaluated on a fixed recent holdout) is the concrete next step to recover f1_volatile before any promotion. Notably the 1h head (f1_vol 0.447) held up far better on the wide window than 5m/15m — recency dilution hits the shorter timeframes hardest.

2026-06-03 (S-MLOPT-S2): window/recency sweep RUN (trainer-vm #2677, scripts/ml/window_recency_sweep.py vs a fixed recent purged holdout). CONFIRMED the dilution: f1_volatile falls monotonically with window length, 5y worst in all three models (1h 0.451->0.446 @1y->5y; 15m 0.202->0.143; 5m 0.101->0.069). RECENCY DECAY (5y+half_life=180d) recovers it: 1h 0.4601 = OUTRIGHT BEST (> 1y 0.451, > plain 5y 0.446) AND best macro_f1, keeping full 5y data; 15m 0.1916 and 5m 0.0963 recover ~80% of the 5y->1y gap at 4-5x the data (1y still narrowly best on f1_volatile for 15m/5m). PROPOSAL (Tier-3, operator-gated): adopt sample_weight half_life=180 on btc-regime-1h-lgbm-v2 (clear win); for 15m/5m run a half-life sweep (60/90/180d) before choosing window-vs-decay. Tooling: PR #2676.

2026-06-03 (regime recency follow-up): half-life sweep (#2678, hl=60/90/180/365d at 5y vs 1y baseline) gave a DECISIVE winner per model — a recency-decayed 5y window now beats BOTH the plain 5y AND the 1y window on f1_volatile for all three: 1h hl=90d f1_vol 0.4666 (vs 0.4510 @1y / 0.4455 @5y); 15m hl=60d 0.2129 (vs 0.2023 / 0.1433); 5m hl=60d 0.1154 (vs 0.1005 / 0.0692). 180d (the first guess) was too slow a decay; 60-90d is the sweet spot. Tier-3 DRAFT PR opened adopting sample_weight (1h=90d, 15m=60d, 5m=60d) on the three btc-regime-*-lgbm-v2 manifests — pending operator approval before merge. Closes the experiment; the manifest edit is the remaining (operator-gated) step.

2026-06-03 (wrap-up): Tier-3 PR #2679 MERGED (operator-approved) — sample_weight now on all three btc-regime-*-lgbm-v2 manifests (1h=90d, 15m=60d, 5m=60d). Experiment + adoption complete; the next training cycle materialises the recency-weighted models. Remaining (separate, operator-gated): shadow->advisory promotion of a regime model once it accrues a shadow track record (blocked by MB-20260529-001). This item can be CLOSED by the next /ml-review.
items.status_history: | 2026-06-03 (S-MLOPT-S2): experiment executed; recency-decay verdict in evidence; Tier-3 manifest-default proposal pending operator approval (item stays open until the manifest edit lands). | 2026-06-03 (regime follow-up): half-life sweep done; per-model winners picked; Tier-3 manifest-adoption draft PR open, pending operator approval. | 2026-06-03 (wrap-up): recency config ADOPTED via merged #2679; experiment resolved — close on next /ml-review.

items.source: /ml-review 2026-06-01 — regime-router (PERF-20260601-002) shipped phases 1+2 today; the design names regime-classifier-baseline-v0 as the future phase-4 ML detector, but its fresh metrics collapse to majority class.
items.title: Regime-router phase-4 detector: regime-classifier-baseline-v0 COLLAPSES (f1_volatile=0.0) — phase-4 should wire btc-regime-1h-lgbm-v2, and the manifest(shadow)/registry(research_only) stage drift needs reconciling
items.description: The regime router (shipped phases 1+2 on 2026-06-01) uses an ADX-14 detector today and names a 'classifier-v0' as the phase-4 drop-in (detector.py _SOURCE tag; design doc § 3 calls regime-classifier-baseline-v0 'a shadow model already in the registry'). TWO problems found this session: (1) regime-classifier-baseline-v0's fresh metrics are a MAJORITY-CLASS COLLAPSE — accuracy 0.835 but macro_f1=0.4549, f1_volatile=0.0, n_eval=8760 (BTC 1h) — i.e. it never predicts 'volatile', so it is NOT a usable replacement for the ADX-14 threshold detector. The viable phase-4 candidate is btc-regime-1h-lgbm-v2 (macro_f1=0.609, f1_volatile=0.444, same n=8760), which actually separates the volatile regime. (2) STAGE DRIFT: regime-classifier-baseline-v0's manifest declares target_deployment_stage: shadow (ml/configs/baseline-regime-classifier.yaml:65) but the registry has it at research_only — reconcile (either the manifest is stale or the registry didn't sync), and update the design doc's 'shadow model already in the registry' claim. Net: when phase-4 (PERF-20260601-007) is taken up, wire the lgbm-1h regime head (not the collapsed baseline) as the classifier-v0 source, after it accrues a shadow track record (blocked by MB-20260529-001).
items.trigger_condition: When regime-router phase 4 (PERF-20260601-007) is scheduled; or next /ml-review.
items.resolution_criteria: Phase-4 detector source is wired to a regime model that does NOT collapse (f1_volatile>0, e.g. btc-regime-1h-lgbm-v2) and has a shadow track record; AND the regime-classifier-baseline-v0 manifest/registry stage drift is reconciled (or the model is retired from the router design).
evidence_log: 2026-06-07 (S-MLOPT-S15a, trainer-vm-diag #2925/#2926): TREND-MODEL CLASS-WEIGHT TUNING + ADX HEAD-TO-HEAD DONE. On the v7 market_features 1h dataset (43,800 rows; trend label dist chop 39% / trending 34% / transitional 27%): the leak-free purged-WF-CV ADX-14 baseline (trailing ADX → forward trend label) scores macro_f1 0.3167 (chop f1 0.336 / trans 0.221 / trend 0.393). The UNTUNED LGBM trend head only ties it (0.3243) and starves transitional (recall 0.124, f1 0.167). A 6-point class-weight sweep found mild inverse-freq weights {chop:1.0, transitional:1.45, trending:1.14} are the sweet spot: transitional f1 0.167→0.276 (recall →0.286) WITHOUT collapsing chop → macro_f1 0.3403 (chop 0.393 / trans 0.276 / trend 0.353), which CLEARS ADX-14 by +0.0236 and beats it on 2 of 3 classes (chop + transitional; ADX still wins trending). Heavier weights (≥2×) over-correct (chop collapses, macro_f1 < baseline). Weights baked into ml/configs/btc-regime-1h-trend-lgbm-v1.yaml (stays research_only). VERDICT: a viable, modestly-better-than-ADX trend-axis detector candidate — honest modest win, NOT a strong one (both ~0.32 macro_f1 on a hard 3-class forward-regime problem). Remaining gates to wiring it as the live phase-4 detector (replacing ADX): a shadow track record + backtest/shadow-PnL justification (not macro_f1 alone) — Tier-3, operator-gated. Item stays in_progress (S15b vol-axis gate + the live phase-4 enforcement are the open halves).

2026-06-06 (/ml-review): the S15a TREND-axis detector exists in the registry but is WEAK so far — btc-regime-1h-trend-lgbm-v1 (research_only) accuracy 0.363, macro_f1 0.323, f1_chop 0.454 / f1_transitional 0.167 / f1_trending 0.349. It does not yet beat a naive ADX-threshold base rate, so it is NOT ready to replace the ADX detector for router phase-4. (Stage drift from MB note already reconciled: baseline-regime-classifier.yaml is research_only.) The S15b vol-axis path is wired observe-only. Next: a class-weight / relabel sweep on the trend head (the 3-class trend regime is imbalanced toward chop) + accrue its shadow track record before any phase-4 detector swap. Stays in_progress.

2026-06-03 (/ml-review): both problems re-confirmed in the 6/3 cycle. (1) regime-classifier-baseline-v0 STILL COLLAPSES — fresh metrics macro_f1=0.4547, f1_volatile=0.0 (accuracy 0.8338 = majority-class on the 0.834 base rate), n_eval=8760 (BTC 1h). It never predicts volatile, so it remains UNusable as the phase-4 detector. The viable candidate stays btc-regime-1h-lgbm-v2 (macro_f1=0.6096, f1_volatile=0.4466, same n=8760) — but it has zero shadow track record (MB-20260529-001). (2) STAGE DRIFT PERSISTS — registry has regime-classifier-baseline-v0 at target_deployment_stage=research_only while its manifest (ml/configs/baseline-regime-classifier.yaml) declares shadow. Field-beats-comment: the registry research_only is the effective stage; the manifest line is stale and should be reconciled to research_only when phase-4 is taken up (or the model retired from the router design).

2026-06-04 (S-MLOPT-S15): KEY FINDING + progress. (2) STAGE DRIFT RECONCILED — baseline-regime-classifier.yaml flipped shadow→research_only (field-beats-comment) with an in-file note that it collapses + is NOT the phase-4 detector. (1) reframed: phase-4 as written is a CATEGORY ERROR — the ADX detector + policy table (src/runtime/regime/policy.py _VALID_REGIMES) key on a TREND axis {chop,transitional,trending}, but every registry regime model (incl. btc-regime-1h-lgbm-v2 and the baseline) predicts a VOLATILITY axis {range,volatile}. A vol classifier cannot drop-in replace the trend detector. Operator chose BOTH fixes as separate tracks: S15a (shipped) = a TREND-regime model (new ml/datasets/labeling/trend_regime.py Kaufman-efficiency-ratio forward label → market_features.trend_regime_label v7; research_only manifest btc-regime-1h-trend-lgbm-v1) — the true ADX drop-in; trainer-VM purged-CV A/B vs the ADX base rates dispatched. S15b (queued) = wire the existing vol classifier as a SECOND, observe-only regime axis (2-D trend×vol policy gate, logs not enforces). S13 (now live) supplies the shadow track record both detectors need. Live phase-4 enforcement remains Tier-3 + gated on feature parity MB-20260604-005. Item stays in_progress until a detector model has a track record AND the operator approves phase-4 enforcement.

2026-06-04 (S-MLOPT-S15b): TRACK B BUILT (draft PR, Tier-2, observe-only). The vol axis is now wired end-to-end: new src/runtime/regime/vol_detector.py::detect_vol_regime buckets the live rolling_log_return_vol against the DEPLOYED regime head's frozen vol_bucket edges (resolved once/process from the registry shadow-stage specs via regime_spec_of, the same source S13 uses) and collapses to 2-class calm/volatile (lowest bucket->calm). Parity guard: only adopts edges from heads whose frozen vol_feature_column is rolling_log_return_vol (the yz heads' yang_zhang_vol edges are skipped so they can't mis-place the boundary; a (symbol,tf) served only by yz heads stays unknown/permissive). policy.would_gate gained an optional vol_regime= param (default-preserving: byte-identical 1-D shape when None) evaluating a 2-D trend_vol cell; StrategyIntent.vol_regime + intent_from_signal + _stamp_regime_on_meta (all 11 builders) + _shadow_regime_gate thread/log the vol axis (enforced:false, both axes per row). config/regime_policy.yaml schema_version 1->2 + a documented EMPTY trend_vol:{} block (authoring off cells needs a vol-split of the regime-roster matrix — evidence accruing now via the stamped vol_regime; no fabricated decisions). 31 new tests; 169 in the regime/intent/builder suites pass; ruff clean. NEXT: after ~2-4 weeks of stamped vol_regime + regime_shadow_gate rows, vol-split the matrix to author the first trend_vol off cells (Tier-3). Item stays in_progress; enforcement on EITHER axis remains Tier-3 + gated on feature parity MB-20260604-005 and (trend axis) the S15a class-weight + head-to-head-vs-ADX follow-ups.

items.source: S-MLOPT-S1 re-evaluated registry models under the new purged & embargoed walk-forward CV (trainer-vm-diag #2675, PR #2674).
items.title: Re-run setup-quality baselines under purged WF-CV before any promotion read; confirm whether setup-quality-lgbm-v2 still beats its mean baseline honestly
items.description: Under leak-free 5-fold purged WF-CV, setup-quality-lgbm-v2 scored MAE=0.086 / MSE=0.0175 vs its optimistic 80/20 holdout MAE=0.065 / MSE=0.0094 (399 rows, setup_labels all v002). The manifest notes the per-setup-mean baseline (setup-quality-baseline-v0) at holdout MAE=0.058 — i.e. under honest CV the v2 LGBM may NOT clear its own baseline. The baseline was not re-run under the same WF-CV folds, so this is not yet apples-to-apples. Also: btc-regime-1h-lgbm-v2 weighted_f1 fell 0.7185->0.6742 under WF-CV (smaller, expected optimism gap). Tool: scripts/ml/eval_split_compare.py. Feeds Phase 0.1 (now done) → relates to G4 + Phase 1 meta-labeling in optimization-roadmap.md.
items.trigger_condition: Next /ml-review, or before any setup-quality shadow->advisory promotion discussion.
items.resolution_criteria: Run scripts/ml/eval_split_compare.py (or an equivalent WF-CV pass) on BOTH setup-quality-baseline-v0 and setup-quality-lgbm-v2 with identical purged folds; record which wins MAE under honest CV. If the v2 LGBM does not beat the baseline under WF-CV, log a demotion/hold note instead of advancing it.
evidence_log: 2026-06-03 (S-MLOPT-S1): WF-CV deltas captured in docs/sprint-logs/S-MLOPT-S1.md re-eval table — setup-quality MAE 0.065->0.086, btc-regime-1h weighted_f1 0.7185->0.6742. label_horizon=1 row for setup_labels is a conservative proxy for true trade-hold overlap; Session 0.2 sample-uniqueness weights (or a label-end-time horizon) would tighten this.

2026-06-07 (autonomous-followups, trainer-vm-diag #2964 + #2965): RESOLVED — honest negative. Apples-to-apples re-eval of BOTH manifests via scripts/ml/eval_split_compare.py on the same 5-fold purged WF-CV (n=203 eval rows). setup-quality-baseline-v0 (per-setup-mean): purged MAE 0.07150 / MSE 0.01535; setup-quality-lgbm-v2: purged MAE 0.08249 / MSE 0.01683. **lgbm-v2 LOSES to its own per-group-mean baseline under leak-free CV** (MAE +0.011, ~15% worse). Holdout MAE was the optimistic flatterer (0.0299 baseline vs 0.0545 lgbm — still loses, but the gap looks smaller because both are inflated). Verdict: do NOT advance setup-quality-lgbm-v2 past its current research_only stage (already demoted from shadow → research_only in S-MLOPT-S5 per the original PR — no live behaviour change needed). The data-wall sub-program (S-MLOPT-S5/S6/S6-FU/S6-FU-2 meta-labeling on dense `setup_candidates`) is the path forward for setup-quality; this v2 LGBM stays parked as documented research_only history.
items.status_history: open 2026-06-03 (S-MLOPT-S1 / M14 Session 0.1 execution) | resolved 2026-06-07 (autonomous-followups: leak-free purged-WF-CV apples-to-apples — lgbm-v2 loses to baseline; already at research_only so no demotion action needed).

items.title: Meta-label model loses to baseline on the REAL holdout — close the synthetic↔real domain gap (wire logged signals as the setup_candidates event source)
items.description: S-MLOPT-S6 built the de-Prado meta-label model + the leak-free live_holdout eval (train on synthetic setup_candidates, score on REAL trades). Trainer-VM eval (#2697): on 352 real BTCUSDT closed trades (win rate 0.244) the model scored accuracy 0.670 vs the majority-class baseline 0.756 — it does NOT beat the baseline, so no promotion was proposed. Root cause is a large domain gap: the synthetic CUSUM/triple-barrier candidates have a ~0.457 win rate vs the strategies' real 0.244 — CUSUM momentum events are a different (easier) population than the actual setups. Highest-leverage fix: add a SIGNAL-LOG event source to ml/datasets/families/setup_candidates.py (sample candidate events from trade_journal.db::signals / signal_audit.jsonl at the strategies' real decision points) so the train distribution matches real setups, then re-run the live_holdout eval. Compose with S7 (backtest-augmented real labels, enlarges the real holdout) and S8 (cross-symbol). The live_holdout machinery makes each measurable.
evidence_log: 2026-06-03 (S-MLOPT-S6 trainer-VM #2697): build BTCUSDT setup_candidates (15732 synthetic) + REAL-trade holdout from data/trade_journal.db (352 BTCUSDT closed, win_rate 0.244); meta-label (LightGBMRegressionTrainer->won, ClassificationEvaluator, live_holdout) scored acc=0.670 prec=0.266 recall=0.198 f1=0.227 brier=0.219 on the 352 real trades; majority-class baseline acc=0.756. Model < baseline => not promotable. Synthetic win rate ~0.457 vs real 0.244 = domain gap.

2026-06-03 (S-MLOPT-S6 follow-up, this session): the Tier-1 enabler landed — ml/datasets/families/setup_candidates.py now accepts signal_log_db=<trade_journal.db>; every side=buy|sell row in trade_journal.db::signals (the audit-log dual-write — strategies' real decision points) is located at the bar covering its logged_at_utc and labeled with the SAME triple-barrier as CUSUM. New row tag event_source ∈ {cusum,signal_log,live} disambiguates the three samplers. Backwards-compatible include_synthetic retained; new include_cusum is the preferred name. New manifest ml/configs/setup-candidates-metalabel-siglog-v1.yaml (Tier-3 proposal, research_only) mirrors S6 (live_holdout, LightGBMRegressionTrainer->won) but trains on the signal-log distribution. 8 new unit tests added; full tests/ml/test_setup_candidates.py + cross_symbol + metalabel + splitters = 51 passed; ruff clean.

2026-06-03 (S-MLOPT-S6-FU trainer-VM eval #2716): built v003 dataset (signal_log train rows=8739 win=0.469; live eval rows=352 win=0.244; include_cusum=false), trained the meta-label, eval on the same 352 real BTCUSDT holdout. **Signal-log meta-label**: acc=0.526 prec=0.290 recall=0.651 f1=0.401 brier=0.417. **Majority-class baseline**: acc=0.756. **Signal-log model < baseline; ACCURACY WORSE than the S6 CUSUM run (0.526 < 0.670) — not promotable**. Honest read: signal-log win rate (0.469) is almost identical to CUSUM (0.457), so the 'CUSUM is easier than real setups' hypothesis was wrong — both synthetic populations are equally pulled from a different distribution than real trades (0.244). The recall jumped (0.198 → 0.651) because the train base rate is higher, biasing the predictor upward; precision barely moved (0.266 → 0.290); brier doubled (0.219 → 0.417). **Real root cause: triple-barrier on synthetic candles uses optimistic fills + no real slippage + no real risk-manager filtering; the strategies' actual trades lose more because real execution + real risk gating filter the obvious winners OUT, leaving structurally harder cases.** No amount of 'better train-event-sampling' closes this gap — the LABELS are the wrong distribution. Next lever: backtest-augmented labels with REAL fill modeling from the standalone harnesses (scripts/backtest_squeeze.py / backtest_fade.py / backtest_trend.py / backtest_ict_scalp.py / run_backtest_vwap.py), via S-MLOPT-S7 backtest_recorder ported to setup_candidates — that's the apples-to-apples distribution. The enabler + manifest stand as Tier-1 infrastructure (useful for future event-source mixing); the manifest stays research_only.

2026-06-03 (S-MLOPT-S6-FU-2, this session): backtest-label event source landed as the next lever. setup_candidates.py now accepts backtest_trades_db=<db> (+ include_backtest convenience reusing live_trades_db, + backtest_strategies filter): every is_backtest=1 row (recorded by ml.datasets.backtest_recorder from the standalone strategy harnesses) is located at its entry bar and emitted tagged event_source='backtest' + is_live_trade=False, carrying the harness's ACTUAL realized outcome (won from the recorded R-proxy pnl), NOT a synthetic triple-barrier. New manifest ml/configs/setup-candidates-metalabel-backtest-v1.yaml (Tier-3, research_only) mirrors the S6/S6-FU live_holdout protocol but trains on the backtest distribution. 8 new tests; full tests/ml/test_setup_candidates.py+metalabel+splitters=55 passed; ruff clean; end-to-end build validated (leakage passed, backtest train + live eval partition). Tracking the headline trainer-VM eval under MB-20260603-003.
items.resolution_criteria: A signal-log (real-decision-point) event source is added to setup_candidates and the meta-label model, re-evaluated on the REAL-trade live_holdout under purged WF-CV, beats the majority-class baseline on accuracy AND shows positive precision lift over the base rate — or the negative is documented as inherent at current data scale with the next lever identified.
items.source: S-MLOPT-S6 live_holdout eval returned an honest negative (model < majority baseline on real trades).
items.status_history: open 2026-06-03 (S-MLOPT-S6); in progress 2026-06-03 (S-MLOPT-S6 follow-up — signal-log family + manifest landed); RESOLVED-NEGATIVE 2026-06-03 (signal-log eval did NOT beat the baseline; honest negative documented; next lever = S7-style backtest-augmented labels with REAL fill modeling, via the standalone strategy backtest harnesses); superseded-by MB-20260603-003 2026-06-03 (backtest-label lever — the apples-to-apples real-distribution train the signal-log run approximated)

items.title: Backtest-labeled setup_candidates: re-run the live_holdout meta-label eval with backtest-train + real-eval (the apples-to-apples distribution the signal-log run approximated)
items.description: S-MLOPT-S6 (CUSUM, acc 0.670) and S-MLOPT-S6-FU (signal-log, acc 0.526) both lost to the majority baseline (0.756) on the 352 real BTCUSDT holdout. The signal-log eval (#2716) ruled out the EVENT SAMPLER as the lever — swapping CUSUM momentum events for the strategies' real decision points barely moved the synthetic train win rate (0.457 -> 0.469). The remaining suspect is the synthetic triple-barrier LABEL (optimistic fills, no slippage, no risk gating). S-MLOPT-S6-FU-2 ports the S7 backtest_recorder pattern to setup_candidates: the strategies' standalone harnesses (scripts/backtest_{squeeze,fade,trend,ict_scalp}.py, src/backtest/run_backtest_vwap.py) already model real slippage + actual entry/exit logic, so each backtest trade is a real-distribution label at a real signal time. Tier-1 enabler (backtest_trades_db source, event_source='backtest') + Tier-3 manifest (setup-candidates-metalabel-backtest-v1, research_only) shipped this session; the headline trainer-VM eval (build backtest-train + real-eval -> train -> score the 352 real holdout vs baseline) is the open step.
evidence_log: 2026-06-03 (S-MLOPT-S6-FU-2 enabler): ml/datasets/families/setup_candidates.py — added _load_backtest_trades (is_backtest=1 reader, optional strategy filter) + backtest_trades_db / include_backtest / backtest_strategies kwargs; backtest rows tagged event_source='backtest', barrier_touched='backtest', is_live_trade=False (train side of live_holdout), won from the recorded R-proxy pnl, r_multiple=pnl. Manifest setup-candidates-metalabel-backtest-v1.yaml (research_only) mirrors S6/S6-FU. 8 new tests + full suite 55 passed; ruff clean; end-to-end build leakage_test_status=passed.

2026-06-03 (S-MLOPT-S6-FU-2 trainer-VM eval #2718): built BTCUSDT setup_candidates v001 backtest-train + real-eval (include_cusum=false): 2,653 backtest rows (squeeze 963 + fade 1690, recorded is_backtest=1 from the standalone harnesses via scripts/ml/record_harness_trades) + 352 real BTCUSDT eval rows (win 0.244, majority baseline 0.756). Trained setup-candidates-metalabel-backtest-v1 under live_holdout, scored on the 352 real holdout: **accuracy 0.756, precision 0.50, recall 0.047, f1 0.085, brier 0.196.** Read: this is the BEST of the three label sources (acc 0.756 vs CUSUM 0.670 vs signal-log 0.526 — backtest labels closed most of the synthetic<->real gap) AND the best calibration (brier 0.196 vs 0.219/0.417), with **precision 0.50 lifting off the 0.244 base rate** (a real, if low-volume, signal). BUT accuracy only TIES the majority baseline (0.756) — recall 0.047 means the model essentially predicts the majority class (loss) for almost all rows, so accuracy ≈ baseline by abstention. NOT a promotion-worthy beat. **Eval is incomplete:** (1) backtest_ict_scalp.py lacks an --emit-trades flag (only --json summary) so ict_scalp was excluded — adding the flag is a Tier-1 follow-up; (2) squeeze+fade ran on the 1h market_raw base (timeframe approximation — both are 4h strategies; fade was deeply net-negative across years on 1h, likely poor-quality trades). Recommend a cleaner re-run (add --emit-trades to ict_scalp + run harnesses at native timeframes) before any further call. Manifest stays research_only; promotion past shadow operator-gated.

2026-06-03 (S-MLOPT-S6-FU-2 cleaner re-run #2723/#2724, detached): all THREE BTC harnesses at native timeframes — ict_scalp 5m (60k-bar window, 128 trades) + squeeze 4h (226) + fade 4h (439) = 793 is_backtest=1 train rows + 352 real eval rows (win 0.244, baseline 0.756). TRAIN: accuracy 0.741, precision 0.40, recall 0.116, f1 0.180, brier 0.199. **Slightly WORSE than the 1h run (#2718: acc 0.756, prec 0.50) — does NOT beat baseline (0.741 < 0.756).** The cleaner native-TF set has far fewer rows (793 vs 2653; 4h squeeze/fade produce 1/4 the trades of 1h), and the smaller/cleaner distribution didn't help. CONCLUSION across all 4 label sources (CUSUM acc 0.670 / signal-log 0.526 / backtest-1h 0.756 / backtest-native 0.741): backtest labels are the BEST source by PRECISION (0.40-0.50 > the 0.244 base rate — a real, consistent signal that real-execution labels are closer to the real distribution), but NONE beats the majority baseline on accuracy at n=352 — the model abstains (low recall) because the real holdout is 75.6% losses. This is a DATA-SCALE FLOOR at n~352, exactly as the roadmap predicted. The lever to break it is more real data (time) or better features (S-MLOPT-S9, which DID show a positive regime f1_volatile lift), not another label distribution. The backtest-label infrastructure (event source + recorder bridge + ict_scalp --emit-trades) stands as reusable tooling; the manifest stays research_only.
items.resolution_criteria: The backtest-labeled meta-label, re-evaluated on the REAL-trade live_holdout, beats the majority-class baseline on accuracy AND shows positive precision lift over the 0.244 base rate (=> propose Tier-3 promotion past research_only) — OR the negative persists across all three label sources (CUSUM, signal-log, backtest) and is documented as a data-scale floor at n~352, pivoting to better features (S-MLOPT-S9 range-based vol estimators / Phase 2).
items.source: MB-20260603-002 RESOLVED-NEGATIVE: signal-log eval (#2716) isolated the synthetic triple-barrier label (not the event sampler) as the train<->eval domain gap.
items.status_history: in_progress 2026-06-03 (S-MLOPT-S6-FU-2 — Tier-1 backtest-label enabler + Tier-3 manifest landed; trainer-VM eval pending); eval landed 2026-06-03 (#2718) — backtest labels are the best of three sources (acc 0.756 ties baseline, precision 0.50 > 0.244 base rate, brier 0.196 best-calibrated) but NOT a clean baseline-beat; eval incomplete (ict_scalp harness lacks --emit-trades; squeeze/fade ran on 1h base). Open for a cleaner re-run.; RESOLVED 2026-06-03 (cleaner native-TF re-run #2724: acc 0.741, prec 0.40 — best-precision of 4 label sources but no baseline beat; data-scale floor at n~352 confirmed. Lever exhausted as a standalone fix; next is more data/time or features S9. Infra stands; manifest research_only).
items.trigger_condition: Any /ml-review run; or when the S-MLOPT-S6-FU-2 trainer-VM eval completes.

items.title: Range-based vol estimators: A/B the Yang-Zhang regime head vs the close-to-close v2 head (quantify the f1_volatile lift)
items.description: S-MLOPT-S9 (M14 Phase 2.1) added four range-based volatility estimators (Parkinson, Garman-Klass, Rogers-Satchell, Yang-Zhang) to the market_features family (builder_version v3), computed over the same past window as rolling_log_return_vol from each bar's full OHLC. New manifest btc-regime-1h-lgbm-yz-v1.yaml (research_only) is a clean A/B vs the champion btc-regime-1h-lgbm-v2 — identical trainer/split/weighting/dataset, only the vol feature set differs (adds the four estimators; freezes the live regime spec on yang_zhang_vol). The open step is the trainer-VM A/B: rebuild market_features on v3, run `python -m ml compare btc-regime-1h-lgbm-v2 btc-regime-1h-lgbm-yz-v1` under the Phase-0 CV, read the f1_volatile delta. Attacks gap G5 (no range-based vol estimators).
evidence_log: 2026-06-03 (S-MLOPT-S9 enabler): ml/datasets/volatility_estimators.py (Parkinson/Garman-Klass/Rogers-Satchell/Yang-Zhang variance estimators) + market_features 4 new past-window vol columns (parkinson_vol/garman_klass_vol/rogers_satchell_vol/yang_zhang_vol), builder_version v2->v3. New manifest btc-regime-1h-lgbm-yz-v1.yaml (research_only). Tests: test_volatility_estimators.py + market_features additions (50 core pass); ruff clean; manifest loads. Leakage-safe by construction (past-only window). A/B eval pending the v3 market_features rebuild.

2026-06-03 (S-MLOPT-S9 A/B #2720): rebuilt market_features BTCUSDT 1h v002 on builder_version v3 (range-vol columns confirmed present), trained champion vs challenger under the manifest time_aware_holdout. **Champion btc-regime-1h-lgbm-v2 (close-to-close vol): f1_volatile 0.4624, accuracy 0.744, macro_f1 0.647, weighted_f1 0.771, precision_volatile 0.355, recall_volatile 0.661.** **Challenger btc-regime-1h-lgbm-yz-v1 (range-based vol, spec frozen on yang_zhang_vol): f1_volatile 0.4724, accuracy 0.760, macro_f1 0.659, weighted_f1 0.783, precision_volatile 0.372, recall_volatile 0.646.** **POSITIVE: every metric improved — f1_volatile +0.010, accuracy +0.016, macro_f1 +0.011, weighted_f1 +0.012, precision_volatile +0.017 (recall_volatile -0.015, a precision/recall trade favoring precision).** Modest but consistent lift; the range-based vol features genuinely help regime separation. CAVEAT: this is the time_aware_holdout (same split for both heads, so the delta is fair) NOT the Phase-0 purged WF-CV — a purged-CV confirmation is the rigorous follow-up before promotion.

2026-06-04 (S-MLOPT-S9 finish, purged-CV confirm #2736): rebuilt market_features BTCUSDT 1h/5m/15m on builder_version v4 and ran scripts/ml/eval_split_compare.py (5-fold purged & embargoed WF-CV, label_horizon=5, embargo_fraction=0.01) on BOTH the v2 champion AND the yz challenger, identical folds. **1h purged WF-CV: yz beats v2 leak-free on EVERY headline metric — f1_volatile 0.5036 vs 0.5009 (+0.0027), macro_f1 0.6609 vs 0.6542 (+0.0067), accuracy 0.7372 vs 0.7258 (+0.0114).** (Same run's holdout: yz f1_volatile 0.4694 vs 0.4655, +0.0039 — same sign, so the holdout lift was not an artifact; both heads' purged-CV f1_volatile ~0.50 > holdout ~0.47 because the recent-20% holdout is the harder block.) The S9 range-vol lift HOLDS under the rigorous leak-free CV. **PROMOTION PROPOSAL (Tier-3, operator-gated, NOT flipped):** advance btc-regime-1h-lgbm-yz-v1 research_only -> shadow as the new 1h regime champion candidate (clean leak-free improvement over the current shadow champion). NOTE: full shadow->advisory is separately blocked by MB-20260529-001 (1h regime heads emit zero shadow predictions until the S13 per-bar scoring path). 5m/15m BTC + 5m/15m MES yz A/B manifests shipped (research_only); their purged-CV A/B results + the MES build are appended here as the trainer jobs (#2736 BTC 5m/15m; a follow-up MES job) complete — only positive leak-free heads are proposed for shadow.

2026-06-04 (S-MLOPT-S9 BTC 5m/15m purged-CV A/B #2736 complete): **ALL THREE BTC timeframes POSITIVE on f1_volatile under the Phase-0 purged WF-CV (leak-free), largest lift at the shorter TFs where the volatile class is rarest:** 1h yz 0.5036 vs v2 0.5009 (+0.0027); 5m yz 0.1535 vs v2 0.1362 (+0.0173); 15m yz 0.2532 vs v2 0.2316 (+0.0216). macro_f1 + accuracy also up on all three (5m macro +0.0092/acc +0.0025; 15m macro +0.0149/acc +0.0144). The range-based vol feature genuinely improves volatile-regime separation across the whole BTC regime stack. Promotion proposal extends to 5m/15m: advance btc-regime-{1h,5m,15m}-lgbm-yz-v1 research_only->shadow (Tier-3, operator-gated). MES 5m/15m yz A/B (needs median-calibrated MES market_features rebuild) + the S11 funding A/B are the remaining trainer jobs.

2026-06-04 (S-MLOPT-S9 MES A/B #2745, median-calibrated build): MES yz is MIXED, NOT a clean win. MES 5m: yz f1_volatile 0.7305 vs v2 0.7261 (+0.0044) BUT macro_f1 0.5329 vs 0.5507 (-0.0178); MES 15m: yz f1_volatile 0.7108 vs v2 0.6889 (+0.0219) BUT macro_f1 0.5294 vs 0.5573 (-0.0279). accuracy up on both (5m +0.0155, 15m +0.0093). Read: on MES the regime classes are MEDIAN-CALIBRATED (~balanced ~50/50), so the range-vol feature shifts the precision/recall tradeoff TOWARD the volatile class (f1_volatile up) at the EXPENSE of the range class — macro_f1 (which weights both classes equally) drops. Contrast BTC, which is heavily range-imbalanced (~80-99.5% range), where the same feature lifts f1_volatile with NO macro cost (macro up on all 3 BTC TFs). So range-vol is a CLEAN win on the imbalanced BTC regime heads (proposed research_only->shadow for btc-regime-{1h,5m,15m}-lgbm-yz-v1) but INCONCLUSIVE on the balanced MES heads — NOT proposed for MES promotion (the macro_f1 regression is the blocker; if the operator only cares about volatile-class recall the MES yz heads are arguably better, but on the balanced metric they're worse). Honest mixed result; the MES yz manifests stay research_only as documented A/B candidates.

2026-06-04 (OPERATOR-APPROVED PROMOTION): operator approved advancing the three BTC range-vol heads research_only -> shadow. btc-regime-{1h,5m,15m}-lgbm-yz-v1.yaml target_deployment_stage flipped to shadow (PR #2739). At shadow they log predictions but never influence an order; full shadow->advisory remains operator-gated AND blocked by MB-20260529-001 (the 1h/15m heads emit zero shadow predictions until the S-MLOPT-S13 per-bar scoring path; the 5m head will emit on actionable 5m signals). The MES yz + S11 funding heads stay research_only. On the next training cycle after merge the three yz heads register at shadow alongside their v2 counterparts (parallel A/B logging).
items.resolution_criteria: The Yang-Zhang (range-based-vol) regime head, A/B'd against btc-regime-1h-lgbm-v2 under the Phase-0 purged CV on a v3 market_features rebuild, shows a positive f1_volatile lift => propose promotion to shadow (operator-gated) + extend to 5m/15m/MES; OR no lift, documented, and the range-vol columns stay available for other consumers (e.g. setup_candidates features).
items.source: S-MLOPT-S9 (M14 Phase 2.1) — gap G5 (no range-based vol estimators); the label-distribution arc (S6/S6-FU/S6-FU-2) pointed to features as the co-equal lever at n=352.
items.status_history: in_progress 2026-06-03 (S-MLOPT-S9 — Tier-1 estimators + market_features columns + Tier-3 A/B manifest landed; trainer-VM compare pending); A/B landed 2026-06-03 (#2720) — POSITIVE on time_aware_holdout (f1_volatile +0.010); RESOLVED 2026-06-04 (S-MLOPT-S9 finish #2736) — purged-CV confirm POSITIVE leak-free (1h f1_volatile +0.0027, macro_f1 +0.0067, acc +0.0114); promotion proposal written (research_only->shadow, Tier-3); 5m/15m/MES extension manifests shipped, A/B results appended as the trainer jobs land. shadow->advisory blocked by MB-20260529-001.

items.title: Crypto funding-rate + open-interest features: A/B the funding-augmented regime head vs the v2 champion (quantify the f1_volatile lift)
items.description: S-MLOPT-S11 (M14 Phase 2.3) added the cheapest unused crypto-perp feature family: Bybit funding-rate z-score/extremes + open-interest change, joined into market_features (builder_version v3->v4) as five past-only, as-of-aligned columns (funding_rate / funding_rate_zscore / funding_rate_abs_z / open_interest_change / open_interest_change_zscore) behind an optional funding_oi_path. Research nuance baked in: funding is a TRAILING byproduct of momentum so the signal is in the z-score/|z| EXTREMES, not the level; OI is fed as a change, not a non-stationary level. New manifest btc-regime-1h-lgbm-funding-v1.yaml (research_only) is a clean A/B vs btc-regime-1h-lgbm-v2 (identical trainer/split/weighting; only the funding/OI columns added). The open step is the trainer-VM A/B: (1) fetch the funding/OI side-stream (ICT_OFFVM_BUILD_HOST=1 python -m scripts.ml.fetch_funding_oi --symbol BTCUSDT --start <s> --end <e> --oi-interval 1h --out datasets-out/funding_oi/BTCUSDT/v001), (2) rebuild market_features 1h v4 with funding_oi_path joined, (3) run scripts/ml/eval_split_compare.py on both manifests under the Phase-0 purged WF-CV and read the f1_volatile delta. Attacks gap G5 (no funding/OI features). The build-script opt-in is ICT_BUILD_FUNDING_OI=1.
evidence_log: 2026-06-04 (S-MLOPT-S11 enabler): ml/datasets/funding_oi_features.py (rolling_zscore/extreme_magnitude/log_change/change_zscore/_finite_or_zero) + ml/datasets/adapters/bybit_funding_oi.py (off-VM Bybit V5 funding+OI history fetcher) + scripts/ml/fetch_funding_oi.py (side-stream CLI) + market_features funding_oi_path join (5 columns, v3->v4, default-preserving, as-of past-only) + ml/configs/btc-regime-1h-lgbm-funding-v1.yaml (research_only) + opt-in build_funding_oi in build_trainer_datasets.sh (ICT_BUILD_FUNDING_OI). Tests: test_funding_oi_features.py + test_bybit_funding_oi.py + market_features funding additions; ruff clean; local stdlib smoke (columns present + non-zero with a side-stream, all-zero without, emitted keys == schema, as-of alignment past-only). A/B eval pending the funding-joined v4 rebuild on the trainer VM.

2026-06-04 (S-MLOPT-S11 funding A/B #2745, trainer VM): the off-VM Bybit V5 funding/OI adapter WORKS end-to-end on the trainer (fetch_funding_oi: funding_rows=5460 = full 5y 8h coverage; open_interest_rows=191 = ONLY ~8 days — Bybit's public OI history retention is short). Rebuilt 1h market_features v4 with the funding/OI join; sanity-checked v2 on the funding-joined dataset = f1_vol 0.5009 (identical to the no-funding build → v2 correctly ignores the funding columns). **A/B (purged WF-CV, leak-free) NEGATIVE: btc-regime-1h-lgbm-funding-v1 f1_volatile 0.4859 vs v2 0.5009 (-0.0150), macro_f1 0.6513 vs 0.6542 (-0.0029), accuracy 0.7328 vs 0.7258 (+0.0070).** Funding/OI did NOT improve the 1h regime head's volatile separation. HONEST READ: (1) OI coverage was ~8 days only (retention), so the open_interest_change/zscore columns are ~all-zero across the dataset — effectively only the funding-rate features (z-score/|z|, full coverage) were tested; (2) those added slight noise the booster overfit (f1_vol down 0.015). The research nuance held in spirit — funding's level is a trailing momentum byproduct — but the extracted extreme features did not separate the *volatility* regime label better. No promotion proposed. The S11 infra (estimators + off-VM adapter + as-of join + fetch CLI + opt-in build wiring) stands as reusable, leakage-safe tooling; manifest stays research_only. Follow-up levers (not pursued this session): wire funding/OI into setup_candidates (a momentum/positioning target may suit it better than the vol-regime label); a deeper OI source (Bybit retention limits the public history — a rolling forward capture would accrue it); or a different funding_window. The OI-retention finding is the key blocker for the OI half.
items.resolution_criteria: The funding-augmented regime head (btc-regime-1h-lgbm-funding-v1), A/B'd vs btc-regime-1h-lgbm-v2 under the Phase-0 purged WF-CV on a funding-joined v4 market_features rebuild, shows a positive f1_volatile lift => propose promotion to shadow (operator-gated) + extend funding/OI to setup_candidates; OR no lift, documented, and the funding/OI columns stay available for other consumers. Microstructure alpha decays — if promoted, monitor via the KS/PSI drift gate.

Infra stands; manifest research_only; follow-up levers = setup_candidates funding features / deeper-OI rolling capture).

items.title: Order-flow / VPIN features: build the Tier-2 live L2 capture path, then A/B OFI/VPIN/microprice vs the regime champions
items.description: S-MLOPT-S10 (M14 Phase 2.2) shipped the Tier-1 estimator core (ml/datasets/orderflow_features.py: Cont OFI, Easley-LdP-O'Hara VPIN + bulk-volume classification, Stoikov micro-price, relative spread; CI-tested). UNLIKE S9 (range-vol from OHLC) and S11 (funding/OI from REST history), order-flow needs L1/L2 + trade-tick data we do NOT capture and CANNOT backfill (no public L2 history endpoint), so it cannot be A/B'd offline — S10 is a two-part sprint and the Tier-2 live-capture path is the gate. Design proposal in docs/ml/orderflow-capture-design.md: store PER-BAR aggregates (ofi/vpin/rel_spread_mean/microprice_dev) as a market_microstructure side-stream (mirrors the S11 funding/OI as-of-join + drift machinery, storage-bounded), joined into market_features via an optional microstructure_path (builder_version v4->v5). THREE OPERATOR DECISIONS needed before building: (1) capture host — trainer-VM side-car (preferred, WS9-safe) vs dedicated tiny OCI host vs (rejected) live-VM; (2) transport — free REST polling on Bybit (recommended start) vs paid ccxt.pro WebSocket; (3) scope — BTCUSDT-only to start (recommended) vs also MES/IBKR depth (needs a paid subscription, shares the live IB session). On sign-off: build the capture service (Tier-2) -> accrue >= a few weeks -> build the join columns + A/B manifest (Tier-1/3) -> purged-CV A/B vs the v2/yz champions. Microstructure alpha decays — monitor via KS/PSI drift if promoted.
evidence_log: 2026-06-04 (S-MLOPT-S10 Tier-1 core): ml/datasets/orderflow_features.py (microprice/relative_spread/order_flow_imbalance/bulk_volume_classification/vpin/_finite_or_zero) + tests/ml/test_orderflow_features.py (16 cases incl. OFI sign semantics: rising-ask +OFI, falling-bid -OFI; BVC split sums to volume + skews with price; VPIN one-sided=1/balanced=0). ruff clean; local stdlib smoke confirmed all values. Design proposal docs/ml/orderflow-capture-design.md written with the three operator decisions. NO live-capture / runtime / storage built — that's the Tier-2 gate.

2026-06-04 (S-MLOPT-S10 Tier-2 build, operator-approved option 1 = trainer-VM side-car + free Bybit REST polling + BTCUSDT-only): BUILT the full capture path. scripts/ml/orderflow_capture.py (polls Bybit public order-book+trades ~2s, aggregates per-5m-bar OFI/taker buy-sell volume/spread/micro-price lean -> one market_microstructure row/bar; off-VM-guarded; resilient per-poll) + deploy/trainer/ict-orderflow-capture.service (trainer-only unit, kept OUT of the live-VM installer glob) + market_features microstructure_path join (6 columns ofi/ofi_zscore/vpin/order_imbalance/rel_spread_mean/microprice_dev, builder_version v4->v5, default-preserving) + ml/configs/btc-regime-5m-lgbm-flow-v1.yaml (research_only A/B vs the 5m champion). Tests + ruff clean. **MONITORING NOTE (operator request): review the OFI/VPIN A/B as soon as enough captured bars accrue.** The capture is FORWARD-ONLY (no L2 history) so the older years have ofi=0 — evaluate on the captured window only. Cadence: ~288 5m-bars/day; a meaningful A/B wants >= ~2-4 weeks (~4000-8000 bars). /ml-review: each run, check datasets-out/market_microstructure/BTCUSDT/5m/v001/data.jsonl row-count on the trainer; once >= ~4000 rows, rebuild market_features 5m v5 with microstructure_path joined, run eval_split_compare on btc-regime-5m-lgbm-flow-v1 vs btc-regime-5m-lgbm-v2 RESTRICTED to the captured window, and report the f1_volatile delta here. Deploy to the trainer pending the S10 PR merge to main (the trainer tracks main).

2026-06-04 (S-MLOPT-S10 DEPLOYED + verified, trainer-vm-diag #2752/#2753): #2751 merged to main (head 8680a4a); installed deploy/trainer/ict-orderflow-capture.service on the trainer VM (cp -> /etc/systemd/system, daemon-reload, enable --now). Verified ACTIVE (running) since 2026-06-04T09:52:04Z, Main PID 764562, ccxt 4.5.53 in the venv, zero journal errors; out dir datasets-out/market_microstructure/BTCUSDT/5m/v001/ created. The first market_microstructure row writes at each completed 5m bar boundary (the capture flushes a bar on rollover), so the file populates from the first boundary after start. NOW ACCRUING FORWARD. /ml-review action each run: check the data.jsonl row-count on the trainer; once >= ~4000 rows (~2 weeks), rebuild market_features 5m v5 with microstructure_path joined + run the eval_split_compare A/B (btc-regime-5m-lgbm-flow-v1 vs btc-regime-5m-lgbm-v2) RESTRICTED to the captured window, and report the f1_volatile delta here.

2026-06-04 (S-MLOPT-S10 FIRST ROW confirmed, trainer-vm-diag #2755): the capture wrote its first real market_microstructure row from live Bybit data at the 09:55 boundary — {ts:2026-06-04T09:50:00Z, symbol:BTCUSDT, ofi:9.015, buy_vol:10.649, sell_vol:14.479, rel_spread_mean:1.61e-06, microprice_dev:2.24e-08, n_snapshots:74}. Sensible values (74 order-book polls in the partial start bar, real taker buy/sell split, tight ~1.6bp-relative spread). End-to-end pipeline VERIFIED working + accruing.

2026-06-06 (/ml-review): capture STILL ACTIVE (ict-orderflow-capture.service active on trainer); market_microstructure/BTCUSDT/5m/v001/data.jsonl = 539 rows as of 6/6 06:48Z (~2 days since the 6/4 09:52 start; cadence lower than the ~288/day ideal, suggesting some poll gaps). Still well below the ~4000-bar (~2wk) A/B threshold. btc-regime-5m-lgbm-flow-v1 (research_only) currently shows f1_volatile 0.110 = IDENTICAL to btc-regime-5m-lgbm-v2 — expected, because the flow columns are ~all-zero across the 5y training window (capture is forward-only, only the last ~539 bars carry real ofi/vpin). A/B is meaningless until the rebuild is RESTRICTED to the captured window with >=~4000 bars. Leave in_progress; re-check row-count next run.
items.resolution_criteria: Operator picks capture host + transport + scope; the Tier-2 market_microstructure capture service ships + accrues data; the OFI/VPIN/microprice columns join into market_features and an A/B manifest beats (or is documented not to beat) the v2/yz regime champions under purged WF-CV. OR the operator decides the capture cost isn't worth it and S10 closes with the estimator core as reusable tooling.
