items.title: Macro/cross-asset A/B for MES: eval mes-regime-5m-lgbm-macro-v1 vs mes-regime-5m-lgbm-v2 once MES market_features rebuilds on v6 with a macro side-stream
items.description: S-MLOPT-S12 (M14 Phase 2.4) shipped the cross-asset/macro feature group for MES: ml/datasets/macro_features.py (VIX level/z + term-structure slope VIX/VIX3M-1, DXY z+return, 10y level + 3m-10y slope), the off-VM yfinance_macro fetch adapter + scripts/ml/fetch_macro.py, market_features builder_version v5->v6 (optional macro_path, default-preserving 0.0), and the research_only manifest ml/configs/mes-regime-5m-lgbm-macro-v1.yaml (identical to v2 except the added macro group; same frozen regime spec). The daily build_trainer_datasets.sh fetches the macro stream once (ensure_mes_macro, MES_MACRO=1 default-on, NON-FATAL) and joins it into the 5m/15m + 1d MES market_features via macro_path. ACTION when the next daily cycle has rebuilt MES market_features on v6 WITH the macro stream present (check the build emit for family=macro status=ok + the v002 MES feature dir carries non-zero vix_level): run `python -m ml compare mes-regime-5m-lgbm-v2 mes-regime-5m-lgbm-macro-v1` and read the f1_volatile delta under the Phase-0 purged CV. If macro separates the MES volatile regime where same-OHLC vol did not -> propose promotion to shadow (operator-gated). If not -> HONEST NEGATIVE: log it here and move on (intraday macro alpha is plausibly thin; MES is daily-conditioned at best). Leakage discipline already enforced: daily features are one-day-lagged (a day-D row stamped D+1) + as-of past-only joined (unit-tested).
evidence_log: 2026-06-14 (model-training, MES pull-fix session) — CLOSED NEGATIVE. Ran the formal `python -m ml compare mes-regime-5m-lgbm-v2 mes-regime-5m-lgbm-macro-v1` on FRESH NATIVE MES history. Backstory: the pull tool (scripts/ops/pull_mes_ibkr_history.sh) had been silently broken since the 2026-06-10 gateway-isolation — it still defaulted IB_HOST=127.0.0.1 (the retired same-box loopback), so every MES pull died in ~2s with ConnectionRefused. Fixed in #3500 (default -> 10.0.0.251, the dedicated gateway VM), deployed, re-ran: MES 5m=17,113 rows / 15m=5,717 (~1y native intraday). Cycle head=65a3b6e4 rebuilt MES market_features ON the native data (build emit 'using synced IBKR MES market_raw'). RESULT (holdout n_eval=3418, 20% of 17k): f1_volatile v2=0.623 vs macro-v1=0.459 (delta -0.164); recall_volatile collapsed 0.694->0.387; weighted_f1 0.686->0.637; precision flat (~0.565). Macro conditioning HURTS the MES 5m volatile regime — daily-lagged VIX/term-structure/DXY/rates carry no intraday (5m) vol-regime signal. Consistent with the 2026-06-06 holdout lean + the S9 balanced-class finding. DECISION: keep mes-regime-5m-lgbm-v2 (shadow, the better head); mes-regime-5m-lgbm-macro-v1 STAYS research_only — no promotion. Honest negative per the manifest's pre-registered call. Optional future probe (not queued): macro on the MES 1d TF, where the regime label separates best and daily macro alpha is more plausible than intraday.

2026-06-06 (/ml-review): macro head TRAINED + leaning NEGATIVE on the holdout. Registry: mes-regime-5m-lgbm-macro-v1 (research_only) f1_volatile 0.484 / macro_f1 0.566 vs mes-regime-5m-lgbm-v2 f1_volatile 0.547 / macro_f1 0.621 — the macro-augmented head is WORSE than the plain v2 on both metrics at the same n_eval=3235. Consistent with the S9 MES finding that MES regime classes are median-calibrated/balanced, so added features that help imbalanced BTC don't help balanced MES. The formal `python -m ml compare ... ` purged-CV is the closing step, but the holdout already points negative. Recommend: run the purged-CV compare; if it confirms <v2, close NEGATIVE (macro stays research_only); optionally try macro on the MES 1d TF where the regime label separates best (daily macro alpha is more plausible than 5m).

2026-06-04 (S-MLOPT-S12, branch claude/mlopt-s12-macro-mes): Part A shipped. 50 macro/market_features tests pass; 224 broader ML tests pass (ex pandas-only); ruff clean. End-to-end producer smoke confirmed sensible values + correct D+1 leakage stamp. Manifest at research_only; orchestrator wired (MES_MACRO=1 default-on, best-effort).
check_log: 2026-06-10 /ml-review: Partial read: mes-regime-5m-lgbm-macro-v1 macro_f1=0.566 currently TRAILS plain mes-regime-5m-lgbm-v2 (0.621) — macro/cross-asset features not yet winning. Keep open pending more MES eval coverage.

items.title: Wire the account_context family into a decision model (S12 Part B) -- needs as-of-signal-time account snapshots first (leakage-safe), or confirm prop-account data volume
items.description: S-MLOPT-S12 Part B (roadmap Session 2.4 second deliverable: 'wire the existing-but-unused account_context family -- equity curve / daily PnL / open-trade count -- into the decision models') was scoped but NOT shipped this sprint, for two honest blockers. (1) NO MANIFEST consumes ml/datasets/families/account_context.py today (it produces the was_taken label + mission-rule features but is an orphan family). (2) The equity-curve / daily-PnL / open-trade-count the roadmap wants are NOT recorded as AS-OF-SIGNAL-TIME snapshots: trade_journal.db::daily_risk_state (src/units/accounts/risk.py) stores only end-of-interval daily running values (daily_pnl, daily_high_equity, current_equity), so deriving a signal-time feature post-hoc would LEAK -- the stored daily_pnl includes PnL from trades AFTER the signal. Also the prop accounts account_context targets are sparse/dry, so a manifest today may be untrainable. TWO SUB-OPTIONS: (a) MINIMAL safe wire -- once prop-account was_taken volume is confirmed non-trivial (verify via an /ml-review diag pull of trade_journal.db::trades WHERE account prop + status in {open,closed,rejected}), add a research_only manifest training on the EXISTING leakage-safe columns (mission rules + skip_reason excluded as a leak per the family docstring). (b) PROPER enrichment -- instrument per-signal account-context snapshots (equity, daily-PnL-so-far summed only over trades closed strictly before the signal ts, open-trade count) at decision time, persist them, then join. (b) is the real value but is live-path-adjacent (Tier-2 instrumentation). Recommend (a) gated on the data check, then (b) if (a) shows signal.
evidence_log: 2026-06-04 (S-MLOPT-S12): documented the blockers after reading account_context.py + risk.py::daily_risk_state schema. Part A (macro for MES) shipped in the same sprint; Part B deferred with both sub-options spelled out.

2026-06-07 (S-MLOPT-S12-PartB ship): SHIPPED option (b) -- the proper per-signal snapshot enrichment. New src/units/accounts/context_snapshot.py (AccountContextSnapshot dataclass + ensure_schema/write_snapshots/daily_state_for/open_trades_count_for/drawdown_pct helpers); new trade_journal.db::account_context_snapshots table (UNIQUE(order_package_id, account_id), 11 columns: captured_at_utc + order_package_id + account_id + strategy_name + symbol + direction + equity + daily_pnl_realized + daily_equity_high + daily_drawdown_pct + open_trades_count + writer_version). Coordinator.multi_account_execute calls _capture_account_context_snapshots once per dispatch AFTER eligibility filtering + BEFORE the per-account RiskManager runs -- so the snapshot is leak-safe by construction (state captured PRE-decision). Best-effort writer (swallows every exception, never blocks the trader); flag-gated by ACCOUNT_CONTEXT_SNAPSHOTS_DISABLED (default off -> enabled). account_context dataset family builder_version v1->v2, adds include_snapshots kwarg + 5 nullable *_at_signal columns; the LEFT JOIN goes trades.id -> order_packages.linked_trade_id -> snapshots.order_package_id, default-off so every existing v1 build is byte-identical. New CLAUDE.md env-var doc + trade_journal.db schema row. 30 new tests (writer helpers + family LEFT JOIN + coordinator hook integration); ruff/dry-run-guard/canonical-db-resolver/silent-empty-guard all clean. Tier-2 trader hook (live-VM write path) ships in this PR as draft -- awaiting operator approval before merge+deploy; once enabled, evidence accrues for option (a)'s manifest follow-up. Reasonably resolves the option-(b) blocker; option (a) (research_only manifest on the now-available snapshot columns) is the natural follow-up.
check_log: 2026-06-10 /ml-review: S12 Part B account_context_snapshots writer now exists (observe-only). Still needs as-of-signal-time join into a decision model. Carry.

items.source: /ml-review 2026-06-06 trainer pull (trainer-vm-diag #2870): ict-trainer.service is-active=failed / ExecMainStatus=1; cycle_end overall_rc=1 on 6/4, 6/5 AND 6/6.
items.title: Daily trainer cycle fails overall_rc=1 every run since 2026-06-04 — orphan manifest setup-candidates-metalabel-xsym-v1.yaml FileNotFoundError (its dataset setup_candidates/all/all/v001 is never built)
items.description: Since 2026-06-04 EVERY daily cycle ends overall_rc=1 (6/4 00:12, 6/5 01:02, 6/6 00:13) — ict-trainer.service shows is-active=failed, ExecMainStatus=1 (the timer itself is enabled+active and fires fine). Root cause is a SINGLE orphan manifest: ml/configs/setup-candidates-metalabel-xsym-v1.yaml -> manifest_failed, exit_code 1, FileNotFoundError: dataset data not found at datasets-out/setup_candidates/all/all/v001/data.jsonl; run `python -m ml.datasets build` first (ml/experiments/runner.py:148). The daily build_trainer_datasets.sh builds BTCUSDT/MES setup_candidates families but NOT the cross-symbol 'all/all' dataset the xsym manifest references, so the manifest fails on every cycle. ALL other 31 models train fine and build_end is rc=0 — so this is cosmetic-rc-pollution + a real wiring gap, not a model-quality problem. EXACT analog of MB-20260527-001 (MES manifests FileNotFoundError, fixed by wiring the build). The non-zero rc also masks any FUTURE genuine cycle failure (rc=1 is now the steady state). Two clean fixes: (a) wire the setup_candidates all/all/v001 cross-symbol dataset into build_trainer_datasets.sh so the xsym manifest gets real (or empty -> manifest_skipped) data; or (b) if xsym is a dead/parked experiment, remove it from the daily manifest set (or gate it behind a dataset-present check) so it stops crashing the cycle. (a) is preferred if cross-symbol meta-labeling is still a live experiment (MB-20260603-002 family).
items.trigger_condition: Any /ml-review run; or any session looking at why ict-trainer.service is failed / cycle_end rc=1.
items.resolution_criteria: Next daily cycle shows setup-candidates-metalabel-xsym-v1 either manifest_ok (real shards) or manifest_skipped via empty_dataset, AND cycle_end overall_rc=0 with ict-trainer.service is-active=active(exit 0). OR the xsym manifest is removed from the daily set and the cycle returns rc=0.
evidence_log: 2026-06-06 (/ml-review, trainer-vm-diag #2870): journal shows manifest_failed for setup-candidates-metalabel-xsym-v1.yaml with FileNotFoundError on datasets-out/setup_candidates/all/all/v001/data.jsonl on 6/4 00:12:36, 6/5 01:02:12, 6/6 00:13:43; each followed immediately by cycle_end overall_rc=1. build_end overall_rc=0 every cycle (datasets fine). 32 models in registry all carry fresh metrics. The xsym manifest is the sole failing unit.

2026-06-06 RESOLVED via PR #2886 (squash e9c3efa, merged to main): chose the robust fix (option b, future-proofed) over wiring this one dataset. run_experiment now raises DatasetMissingError (a subclass of EmptyDatasetError) for an absent dataset file instead of a bare FileNotFoundError, so `ml train` maps it to the existing exit-78 skip path and run_training_cycle.sh records manifest_skipped (reason=dataset_absent) with overall_rc unchanged. This protects the cycle from ANY orphan/not-yet-built manifest, not just xsym. VERIFIED LIVE on the trainer VM (HEAD=e9c3efa): `python -m ml train ml/configs/setup-candidates-metalabel-xsym-v1.yaml` returned EXIT=78 with {"skipped":true,"reason":"dataset_absent"} (was the lone overall_rc=1 driver). 9 unit tests pass (incl. a new CLI exit-78/dataset_absent regression guard); CI green. The next daily cycle should report cycle_end overall_rc=0 + ict-trainer.service active. NOTE: whether to actually BUILD the xsym all/all dataset (so the model trains rather than skips) remains a separate /ml-review experiment call (MB-20260603-002 cross-symbol family); this item only fixes the cycle-failure.

If promoted: confirm the next cycle builds setup_candidates/all/all/v001 + the xsym model registers at shadow + emits shadow predictions. If declined: xsym stays research_only, build stays off (the manifest skips cleanly via PR #2886), and cross-symbol is documented as a weak lever pending real MES trades.
evidence_log: 2026-06-06 (model-training, S-MLOPT-S8): DATA FACT that reframes the experiment -- trade_journal.db (trainer copy, 2026-06-06) has 354 real closed non-backtest/non-demo trades and ALL 354 are BTCUSDT; MES has ZERO closed trades (win rate 0.249 -> majority-class baseline acc 0.7514). So the manifest/roadmap success criterion ('joint beats MES-only on the REAL MES holdout') is NOT executable -- there is no real MES holdout (live_holdout's _load_live_trades(db,'MES')=[]; split_live_holdout would raise). The n~352 floor IS the 354 BTC trades. Only the REVERSE transfer is measurable: does pooling MES synthetic + a `symbol` feature help predict the real BTC holdout.

2026-06-06 (trainer-vm-diag #2892): joint setup_candidates/all/all/v001 BUILDS clean (BTC 1h v002 + MES 15m v001 + live_trades_db=data/trade_journal.db): 22,814 rows -- BTC synthetic(cusum) 15,737, MES synthetic(cusum) 6,723, BTC real(live) 354, MES real 0. `symbol` column present + both symbols present. Synthetic won_mean 0.452 vs real-BTC 0.249 (the documented synthetic<->real gap persists).

2026-06-06 (trainer-vm-diag #2893-#2894): CLEAN ablation via scripts/ml/eval_split_compare.py on the IDENTICAL 354-trade real-BTC holdout (BTC-only rebuilt --overwrite from the same BTC 1h v002 + live trades, so the only diff vs joint is {+MES synthetic, +symbol}). JOINT: acc 0.7571 / precision 0.5417 / recall 0.1477 / f1 0.2321 / brier 0.2201. BTC-only: acc 0.6808 / precision 0.2093 / recall 0.1023 / f1 0.1374 / brier 0.2220. Majority baseline acc 0.7514 (base rate 0.2486). The joint beats BTC-only by +0.076 acc / +0.33 precision and is the FIRST meta-label in this family to clear the majority baseline on the real holdout, with precision 0.54 = 2.2x base rate (BTC-only 0.21 is BELOW base rate). CAVEATS: (1) win is on BTC (reverse transfer) not MES; (2) leak-free purged-WF within-distribution does NOT corroborate (BTC-only f1 0.369 > joint 0.209 -- though joint folds pool both symbols, not a clean cross-model compare); (3) n=354 noisy (BTC-only holdout acc swung 0.68-0.74 across builds); (4) cannot fully disentangle 'cross-symbol' from 'more rows' (22k vs 16k) without a same-volume single-symbol upsample control. VERDICT: WEAK-POSITIVE, observe-in-shadow, NOT a decisive floor-break. The floor stays a data-volume problem (354 BTC trades, 0 MES).

2026-06-06 (model-training, S-MLOPT-S8): SHIPPED (PR draft, branch claude/xsym-metalabel-training-3JvRb): (a) Tier-1 opt-in/default-off `build_xsym_setup_candidates()` in scripts/ops/build_trainer_datasets.sh (ICT_BUILD_XSYM=1 builds the joint all/all dataset daily; default off => byte-identical cycle + the xsym manifest keeps skipping via PR #2886); (b) manifest header + notes document the eval result, stage stays research_only; (c) docs/sprint-logs/S-MLOPT-S8.md + roadmap S-MLOPT-S8 line updated. PROPOSAL to operator: research_only -> shadow (Tier-3) so the joint model accrues an observe-only live track record + flip ICT_BUILD_XSYM=1. NOT flipped autonomously. FOLLOW-UP LEVERS once MES accrues real closed trades: (i) re-run with a true MES real holdout to measure the INTENDED BTC->MES transfer; (ii) extend to MGC/MHG when they trade; (iii) combine the joint dataset with the S9 range-vol / yz features (the one feature lever that DID lift) for a stronger meta-label.
items.status_history: opened+resolved 2026-06-06 (model-training, S-MLOPT-S8): joint trained+evaluated; qualified-positive on the BTC holdout, BTC->MES transfer unmeasurable (0 MES trades); research_only->shadow proposed (Tier-3, operator-gated); daily build wired opt-in/default-off (ICT_BUILD_XSYM). Operator decides the promotion.

items.source: Diag relays #3008, #3042, #3045: per-head shadow/stats for the three S9 yz BTC regime heads. row_keys_seen differs across heads.
items.title: btc-regime-15m-lgbm-yz-v1 shadow rows missing 5 fields that 1h and 5m heads have (confidence, direction, killzone, setup_type, strategy_name)
items.description: Snapshot 2026-06-09: btc-regime-1h-lgbm-yz-v1 and btc-regime-5m-lgbm-yz-v1 record row_keys_seen including {confidence, direction, killzone, setup_type, strategy_name} in addition to the bar-derived vol/return features. btc-regime-15m-lgbm-yz-v1 records only the bar-derived features (yz/GK/Parkinson/RS vols, log_returns + lags, symbol, timeframe, vol_bucket, hour_of_day, dayofweek, event_source) — the 5 signal-context fields are absent. That means the 15m head is scoring purely from bar features while the other two heads see strategy-signal context too. Determine if this is intended in ml/configs/btc-regime-15m-lgbm-yz-v1.yaml (legitimate scope difference — e.g. 15m head only scores bar-cadence and not signal events), or a wiring gap in the shadow predictor's feature builder for the 15m head. Block on diagnosis before any 15m promotion past shadow.
items.trigger_condition: before any promotion of btc-regime-15m-lgbm-yz-v1 past shadow
items.resolution_criteria: (a) confirm intended-by-design vs wiring-bug by reading the three manifests + shadow inference call site, (b) if wiring bug, ship a fix and re-validate row_keys; (c) if intended, document the reason in the manifest's description

evidence_log.note: 1h head row_keys include the 5 signal-context fields, count=127
5m head row_keys include the 5 signal-context fields, count=1537
15m head row_keys MISSING the 5 signal-context fields, count=390

status_history.note: Opened by S9 yz monitoring session doc-freshness.

Intended, not a wiring gap. The five fields are signal-time correlation metadata, NOT model inputs; all three yz manifests share an identical 11-feature bar-derived set (verified against ml/predictors/shadow.py + regime_shadow.feature_row_for_predictor). The 15m head logs bar-cadence-only records because turtle_soup (its only 15m BTC strategy) rarely fires; the per-bar path (S13) still accrues its track record. See docs/audits/s9-yz-shadow-promotion-2026-06-09.md §2.

items.source: Diag relay #3042 to /api/bot/shadow/stats for btc-regime-5m-lgbm-yz-v1 returned count=1537 over 3.9 days, score_mean=0.93, score_min=0.50, score_max=0.9998.
items.title: btc-regime-5m-lgbm-yz-v1 score distribution looks class-collapsed (mean 0.93, count ~36% above bar-cadence expectation)
items.description: btc-regime-5m-lgbm-yz-v1 shows score_mean=0.9282 across 1,537 shadow predictions logged 2026-06-05 01:04 → 2026-06-08 22:10 (3.9 days). At a clean 12-bars/hour cadence that window is 12×24×3.9 = ~1,123 bars; the head logged ~36% more, so something is scoring more than once per closed 5m bar (likely signal events overlapping bar-scoring — consistent with the head having the 5 signal-context fields per MB-20260609-001). The high mean and skewed-right distribution (min still 0.50 but max effectively 1.0) suggest one regime class is dominating with near-saturated confidence — a class-collapse / overconfidence pattern. Before any 5m head shadow→advisory promotion (operator review ≈ 2026-06-12), pull the per-class prediction-share distribution and the model's training/eval class balance from the trainer registry, and verify the head is meaningfully discriminating between regimes rather than predicting one dominant class always-with-high-confidence. Compare against 1h head (score_mean 0.77, less skewed) and 15m head (score_mean 0.84). The same anti-pattern has historically gated other regime models past shadow.
items.trigger_condition: before the operator's planned ~2026-06-12 promotion review of the S9 yz heads
items.resolution_criteria: produce a per-predicted-class share table for the 5m head over the shadow window, confirm reasonable spread (no single class > ~75% of predictions, or document why the regime distribution genuinely justifies it); compare to the head's training-set class balance to rule out simple class-imbalance leakage

evidence_log.note: 5m head shadow/stats count=1537, score_mean=0.93, score_min=0.50, score_max=0.9998

status_history.note: Opened by S9 yz monitoring session doc-freshness.

Not class-collapsed. The logged score is max-class probability (range [0.5,1] for these 2-class heads); the 5m head's high score_mean is confident RANGE prediction (range=99.65% of 5m bars) — it predicts volatile only 1.1% of the time. Per-class eval (run 20260609): 1h f1_vol 0.476 > 15m 0.247 > 5m 0.125 (precision_vol 0.082); the score_mean ordering is the inverse of detector quality. Promotion read: 1h promotable, 15m marginal, hold 5m. See docs/audits/s9-yz-shadow-promotion-2026-06-09.md §3.

items.title: ENABLE downsize influence for advisory models (currently annotate-by-default after the 2026-06-13 gate collapse)
items.description: After the 2026-06-13 gate collapse (PR on branch claude/models-strategies-status-qnmj8o), ADVISORY_MODE is gone and a model promoted past shadow influences by the deployment STAGE alone. The permissive default is mode=annotate: advisory-stage models (currently btc-regime-1h-lgbm-yz-v1) score every eligible BTC order and LOG the would-be reductive downsize to runtime_logs/advisory_decisions.jsonl, but DO NOT resize. To let them actually cut live size: set advisory_policy.mode=downsize on the chosen BTC strategy(ies) in config/strategies.yaml (+ optional size_floor/quorum/bearish_threshold). Off-switch = demote the model to shadow. Soak the annotate log first; flip to downsize once it looks sane. Tier-3.

items.title: P4 — graduate conviction to drive real-money sizing (Tier-3, soak-gated, operator-approved)
items.description: Replace effective_risk_pct sizing with per_trade_risk_budget(2%) × conviction × available-margin (design § 3.3); drop the dormant pos_size. This is a deliberate change to the sizing path itself (risk.py / coordinator), governed by the account mode gate + margin/daily-loss guards — NOT a switch flipped on the observe-only annotator. Demo-first (bybit_1), then real money. Open numbers (design § 6 #2, § 10): the no-trade floor (inert 0 today), the throttle-curve shape, the per-trade daily-loss-budget interaction. DO NOT start without explicit operator direction.

items.source: Unified-confidence risk redesign (docs/unified-confidence-risk-DESIGN.md § 5 P5 / § 3.2). v1 = hand-set formulaic conviction blend (DEFAULT_CONVICTION_WEIGHTS, kept per the 2026-06-16 sweep); v2 = learned LightGBM stacker (conviction-meta-v1) currently degenerate at n≈65 with f1=0 baselines (MB-20260615-DEGEN-BASELINES).

Blocked on the same data-sufficiency problem as P4 — the conviction_meta corpus is the n≈65 the degenerate v2 trains on; likely needs backtest-augmented per-trade rows (MB-20260530-001) first. The sweep harness (scripts/ml/sweep_conviction_weights.py) auto-revisits the v1 weights at ≥150 multi-input rows; v2 promotion is the next step beyond that. DO NOT start without explicit operator direction.

items.title: D2b — make the c_reg conviction lens real (regime class-prob vector + alignment/calibrator + signal-time xa feed)
items.description: Today the conviction blend reserves c_reg (weight 0.15, 'P(favorable regime)') but conviction_inputs._default_normalize SKIPS it: a lone regime scalar isn't a usable alignment probability. D2b: (1) expose the regime predictor's CLASS-PROBABILITY VECTOR from predict() (deeper predictor-interface change), (2) add regime_alignment(probs, direction)->[0,1] + an optional fitted calibrator in conviction_inputs.py so c_reg stops being skipped, (3) feed xa_* at SIGNAL time too (strategy_signal_builders shadow capture — needs a bounded peer fetch on the signal path) so the signal-time score isn't NaN-degraded. Still observe-only (feeds the already-soaking meta.conviction, never the order). Build only after the D2a soak has accrued enough that c_reg can be sanity-checked.

items.title: [refinement] DEMOTE the degenerate advisory btc-regime-1h-lgbm-yz-v1 (advisory -> shadow), then refine or retire
items.description: It feeds the live c_reg conviction lens a near-constant saturated value (no discriminating signal; can bias conviction one way). Soft-off first: promote-stage btc-regime-1h-lgbm-yz-v1 --new-stage shadow --by claude --reason 'degenerate live collapse, KS 0.863, AUC 0.0' (Tier-3, operator-gated). Then refine: investigate WHY the live score collapsed to ~0.98 — likely a feature-distribution/pipeline shift at score time (live!=train) rather than the trained model (training macro_f1 stable 0.667, std 0.0023). Retrain on fresh data + verify the live feature vector matches train. Pre-2026-06-13 the system ran formulaic conviction with no advisory model, so demoting is a low-risk revert. [refinement] RE-OPENED — STAGE-GUARD NOW PROPOSES DEMOTE of this model. /ml-review (system-report since-last). `python -m ml stage-guard` on the trainer (#4302): summary={promote:[], demote:[btc-regime-5m-lgbm-yz-v1], hold:39, total:40}. ~4.5h after the 07:05Z promotion, this is the SOLE order-influencing model and is flagged for demote. SINGLE TRIGGER (per-gate detail #4306): 'live calibration worse than base rate (brier_lift -0.11364)' — and ONLY that. The other gates are HEALTHY: live_agreement rank-AUC 0.600 (>=0.55, win_rate 0.625 on n=8), drift only 'moderate' and CLEAN (KS 0.165<=0.2, PSI 0.022<=0.25), score NOT collapsed (range 0.663-0.999, mean 0.931, non_degenerate min-class F1 0.333>=0.30). So this is a CALIBRATION miss, NOT the degeneracy/drift-collapse that demoted 1h-yz — the model still discriminates live, it's just over/under-confident. This is the exact marginal-calibration risk flagged at promotion (negative brier_lift -0.114). PROPOSAL (Tier-3, promotion_recommendations[] this review): soft-off demote advisory->shadow: promote-stage btc-regime-5m-lgbm-yz-v1 --new-stage shadow --by claude --reason 'stage-guard demote: brier_lift -0.114 (live miscalibration); discrimination still OK'. Refinement hypothesis (precise): fit a probability calibrator (isotonic/Platt via scripts/ml/fit_confidence_calibrators.py-style) on the regime head's live shadow scores->realized win, lift brier_lift>0, then re-gate. Lower-risk alt: keep at advisory and let the c_reg lens consume the AUC-0.60 ranking (it discriminates) while the formulaic conviction handles magnitude — operator's call given AAC. Caveat: the demote IS only 1 gate and the model otherwise gates clean, so a HOLD-with-watch is also defensible vs an immediate demote; flagged as 'demote-or-hold (calibration)' for operator. NOTE on registry display: list-models text `status` reads 'candidate' for nearly all models (registry lifecycle field); canonical influence stage is `target_deployment_stage` (5m-yz=advisory, 1h-yz=shadow CONFIRMED). status resolved->open.

items.description: The replacement advisory (after 1h-yz was demoted, MB-20260623-001) is itself degenerate live: negative brier_lift (-0.277) means it is actively worse than baseline, auc 0.40 < random. It feeds the c_reg conviction lens a non-discriminating / harmful signal. updates.note: Opened from /system-review. stage-guard demote fires (brier_lift -0.277, auc 0.40). Surfaced as a Tier-3 operator priority + a loud flag in the consolidated report. Companion to MB-20260623-001 (the prior degenerate yz advisory).

INVESTIGATION (operator chose hold+investigate over demote). ROOT CAUSE FOUND via live shadow_predictions feature_row (diag #4511). (1) PARITY IS WIRED, not the bug: feature_row_for_predictor (S-MLOPT-S17) populates yang_zhang_vol + all 4 range estimators + lags + time features live (both per-bar AND signal-time callers pass candles_df); the live values are real (e.g. btc-1h yang_zhang_vol=0.006493), NOT NaN. The regime_bar_scoring.py module-header 'Feature-parity caveat' (lines 65-73, claiming the estimators are absent live -> NaN and 'must land before shadow->advisory') is STALE doc-drift, contradicted by both the code and the live data. (2) THE REAL BUG = yz vol_bucket edge miscalibration: on the SAME bar, the yz heads bucket vol systematically LOWER than their v2/baseline siblings. btc-1h @ yang_zhang_vol=0.006493 -> v2/funding heads bucket vol_b2 (score 0.56), yz head buckets vol_b0 (score 0.92); btc-15m @ 0.00207 -> baseline/v2 vol_b1, yz vol_b0. The yz heads' frozen vol_bucket_edges (computed on yang_zhang_vol at train time) don't match the live yang_zhang_vol scale, so live vol almost always lands in the lowest bucket b0 -> the model's most important categorical feature is near-constant -> the BTC yz heads saturate (~0.92-0.98) -> degenerate. (Note: the broad btc-regime saturation [baseline+v2+yz all ~0.97-0.99 on 5m/15m] is ALSO partly market-driven — BTC is in a persistent low-vol range — which inflates the stage-guard drift-vs-train metric; regime-confidence != trade-outcome, so the auc/brier-vs-trades attribution is a partial category mismatch. But the yz-specific b0 lock is a genuine calibration defect distinct from that.) FIX (Tier-3, retrain): re-freeze the yz heads' vol_bucket_edges against the live-consistent yang_zhang_vol distribution (verify the estimator's train-vs-live window/annualization parity first), then re-gate before any re-promotion. SEPARATE Tier-1 doc fix: correct the stale regime_bar_scoring.py header caveat (parity landed in S17). Demote remains the safe interim (operator holding); the degenerate yz advisory keeps feeding c_reg a near-constant value meanwhile.

RE-GATE RESULT (operator-approved re-freeze retrain, trainer #4519, python -m ml train --no-register so the SERVED advisory was untouched). Fresh re-frozen vol_bucket_edges: 5m [0.003040, 0.003877] (macro_f1 0.559, f1_volatile 0.125); 1h [0.007615, 0.019984] (macro_f1 0.665, f1_volatile 0.487). DECISIVE: the re-freeze does NOT clear the b0 lock — current live yang_zhang_vol (5m ~0.00156, 1h ~0.006493 from diag #4511) is STILL below the freshly-recomputed b0->b1 edge on BOTH heads (5m 0.00156<0.00304; 1h 0.006493<0.007615) => both still bucket vol_b0. So the degeneracy is NOT a stale-model artifact: BTC's CURRENT low-vol regime sits below the bottom bucket of even a fresh (60d-recency-weighted) edge set, so a simple retrain can't salvage the yz advisory. RECOMMENDATION FLIPS to DEMOTE now (evidence-backed): demote btc-regime-5m-lgbm-yz-v1 advisory->shadow. Re-promotion would need a DEEPER Tier-3 redesign — shorter edge-freeze recency window, finer low-end bucket granularity, or moving off fixed-quantile bucketing — validated before any promote. The 1h head is borderline (0.006493 vs 0.007615) so it'd flip to b1 on a small vol uptick, but is also b0-locked right now.
Tier-1 doc fix LANDED (commit b72646b): corrected the stale regime_bar_scoring.py feature-parity caveat (S17 parity is closed; the real gate is yz edge calibration).

DEMOTE EXECUTED (operator-approved). promote-stage btc-regime-5m-lgbm-yz-v1 advisory->shadow on the trainer (#4526, stage_history records advisory->shadow at 06:45:39Z; advisory count now 0 — ZERO order-influencing models, reverted to known-good formulaic conviction). Mirror published to live (#4527, 06:46:29Z -> 141.145.193.91:/data/bot-data/runtime_logs/trainer_mirror). Takes effect on the live trader's next registry reload. Item stays OPEN for the refinement half: re-promotion needs the deeper edge-redesign (shorter freeze recency / finer low-end granularity / off fixed-quantile bucketing), validated + re-gated first. (NB: the first demote dispatch #4523 — bundled with publish — had its opened-webhook dropped and never ran; re-fired as #4526 promote-stage-only + #4527 publish.)

NEW MECHANISM (RG2 replay-pregate session, 2026-06-25 ~22:20Z) — the advisory degeneracy has a SECOND, distinct root cause this item missed, on a DIFFERENT scoring path than the vol_bucket-edge issue (which was diagnosed on the SHADOW path). Evidence: live /api/bot/shadow/stats for btc-regime-1h-lgbm-yz-v1 (#4596) shows TWO record sets — `shadow` stage: row_keys_seen = FULL market_features (yang_zhang_vol, vol_bucket, rolling_log_return_vol, log_return, hour_of_day...), score spread 0.50-0.99 (the enriched per-bar path #4511 examined); `advisory` stage (count 183, 06-13->06-23 = the exact promote->demote window from stage_history #4601): row_keys_seen = ONLY {confidence, direction, killzone, setup_type, strategy_name, symbol} — ZERO vol features — and score 0.977-0.989, a degenerate CONSTANT. ROOT CAUSE: src/runtime/advisory_sizing.py::compute_advisory_factor scores every advisory predictor on _feature_row_from_pkg(pkg) (6 bare OrderPackage fields, designed for the trade-outcome models) and does NOT call feature_row_for_predictor to enrich — unlike the signal path (_emit_shadow_preds, which enriches-or-skips) and the per-bar path (regime_bar_scoring). So a regime head at advisory is scored on a row with ALL its market_features missing -> LightGBM emits a near-constant ~0.98 -> that constant is what the stage-guard auc-0.40 / brier_lift -0.277 attribution actually measured (NOT the edge-bucketing). IMPLICATION: edge re-calibration alone (the prior conclusion) CANNOT make advisory promotion of ANY regime head work — even a perfectly-calibrated head gets a bare row at advisory and saturates. FIX (Tier-3, order-influence path) — two options for the operator: (A) enrich in compute_advisory_factor via feature_row_for_predictor (needs candles for the pkg symbol/tf at sizing time; skip/score-None a regime head that can't be enriched rather than log a constant) — mirrors _emit_shadow_preds; OR (B, cleaner) EXCLUDE regime heads from the advisory directional-downsize quorum entirely — a regime P(volatile) score is not a bullish/bearish directional view, so feeding it into the 'bearish quorum' downsize is a category error regardless of enrichment; regime conviction belongs in the c_reg lens, not the downsize quorum. RECOMMEND (B) + a promotion-gate guard that refuses to promote a predictor to advisory until its advisory-path score distribution is verified non-degenerate. Surfaced as a Tier-3 proposal on PR #4602. This is the load-bearing reason BOTH yz advisories (1h MB-20260623-001 + 5m here) went degenerate the moment they were promoted.

RESOLVED (structural fix landed + deployed). PR #4602 merged 2026-06-26 — advisory_sizing.py::compute_advisory_factor now EXCLUDES regime heads from the advisory downsize quorum (option B, operator-approved Tier-3): a regime head at advisory was scored on the bare _feature_row_from_pkg row -> degenerate ~0.98 constant (the 'auc 0.40' that demoted both yz heads). With the exclusion that path can no longer fire for a regime head, so the degeneracy is STRUCTURALLY prevented (independent of vol_bucket edge calibration). Deployed live via ict-git-sync (trader restart ~05:27Z, git_sha f96749b8). Both heads remain demoted; RG3 replay-pregate confirmed all 17 shadow regime heads (incl both yz) SOUND (AUC 0.58-0.92). Re-promotion is now safe from the advisory-path angle; vol_bucket low-base-rate edge-redesign is an OPTIONAL future refinement, not a blocker.

items.source: Multi-symbol A (#1) gating of the ETH/MES regime heads: RG3 clean-candle discrimination (replay_pregate_fleet, trainer #4844) + RG4 logged-live-row skew gate (scripts/ml/rg4_targeted.sh, trainer #4846; live shadow log 117,733 rows).
items.title: Multi-symbol A: mes-5m-lgbm-v2 is RG4-confirmed (promotion candidate); ETH + mes-15m/1d blocked by the MES/ETH live-labeling gap
items.description: GATING RESULT for extending Design-A's vol-verdict beyond BTC. RG3 (offline): all 5 ETH/MES heads TRUSTWORTHY (mes-1d 0.92, mes-15m 0.89, mes-5m 0.80, eth-1h 0.73, eth-1h-xasset 0.70) -- offline discrimination is not the differentiator (RG3 green-lights skew heads too). RG4 (live-row, the decider): (A) mes-regime-5m-lgbm-v2 = 0.765 TRUSTWORTHY on 204 labeled live rows (mf=True) -- consistent with the 0.77 from the 2026-06-26 scorecard -> the ONE clear multi-symbol promotion candidate. (B) mes-regime-15m-lgbm-v2 = 0.32 'anti-predictive' but on only 77 of 1194 labeled -- the SAME thin-labeled-sample artifact MB-20260626-002 already caught and REVERSED to 0.59 trustworthy on the full ~1011-row sample; inconclusive, NOT a kill. (C) eth-regime-1h-lgbm-v1 (117 recs), eth-regime-1h-lgbm-xasset-v1 (353), mes-regime-1d-lgbm-v2 (256) = UNSCOREABLE: every live row is UNLABELED. ROOT BLOCKER: the MES/ETH live-labeling gap (MB-20260626-001 finding #1 -- live regime rows logged without regime_label) blinds RG4 for everything except MES 5m. So multi-symbol A is gated on the LABELING PIPELINE, not the models. NEXT: (1) MES path is ready -- per-symbol MES vol-split backtest -> author MES trend_vol cells -> propose mes-5m-lgbm-v2 advisory promotion (operator-gated; MES then rides the same REGIME_ML_VERDICT_MODE=use + REGIME_ROUTER_ENABLED enforce flip as BTC). (2) Root-cause the MES/ETH live-labeling gap to unblock ETH + mes-15m/1d. Tooling added this session: scripts/ml/rg4_targeted.sh + _rg4_print.py (targeted RG4 for a head list).
items.trigger_condition: Next /ml-review or /system-review; or any session promoting a regime head or working the MES/ETH live-labeling parity.
items.resolution_criteria: mes-5m-lgbm-v2 promoted to advisory (operator-gated) with MES per-symbol cells authored AND/OR the MES/ETH live-labeling gap root-caused so ETH + mes-15m/1d become RG4-scoreable and gate cleanly.
evidence_log: 2026-06-30 (/ml-review, trainer-vm-diag #5165): RG4 reconfirms mes-regime-5m-lgbm-v2 = 0.765 TRUSTWORTHY (mf=True) -- consistent with the 0.765/0.77 prior reads. Still the ONE clear multi-symbol promotion candidate. Caveat persists: of 3588 logged-live rows only ~204 are labeled (3384 unlabeled) = the MES live-labeling gap still blinds RG4 to most of the stream. Promotion is gated on the MES cell-authoring path (per-symbol MES vol-split backtest -> author config/regime_policy.yaml MES trend_vol cells -> walk-forward -> promote-stage), NOT on the model. Stays at shadow; lead candidate.

2026-06-30 (root-cause from the MES-labeling investigation, /ml-review buildout session): the MES 'all live rows UNLABELED -> RG4 unscoreable' symptom is NOT a labeling-code bug in replay_pregate_live (no symbol-key or threshold issue) — it is a STALE CANDLE BASE: MES market_raw content ends 2026-06-12 (the IBKR->trainer MES market-data sync froze; daily build reports 'ok' but re-emits stale candles), so RG4 cannot join any live MES row after 2026-06-12 to a realized-label bar. Tracked as BL-20260626-MES-BASE-STALE (health backlog #99). => The MES multi-symbol vol-gate extension stays blocked on REFRESHING the frozen MES candle pull (trainer-VM data op), NOT on model or labeling-code work. The per-symbol RG4 threshold fix (MB-20260628-RG4-THRESH) was shipped alongside but does not make stale-base MES rows labelable.

2026-06-30 (MES candle base — durable fix shipped commit 28ddbf5, PR #5167): the MES-labeling blocker (BL-20260626-MES-BASE-STALE) is now durably fixed at the build layer — build_mes_market freshness-gates the stale IBKR shard and falls to fresh yfinance ES=F, so after merge + the next trainer cycle the MES 5m/15m market_raw advances past 06-12 and RG4 can label live MES rows again (mes-5m-v2 / mes-5m-yz become re-scoreable). Deep-history restore (revive the live-VM IBKR pull) is the operational follow-up. Re-run RG4 on the MES fleet once the fresh candles land to reconfirm mes-5m-v2 (promotion candidate) and mes-5m-yz (demote candidate).

items.source: Root-caused + fixed the MES/ETH live-labeling gap (MB-20260627-002 / MB-20260626-001 #1), then validated and found the ETH head fails RG4 live. Trainer relays #4864 (coverage probe), #4865 (ETH dataset rebuild), #4866 (RG4 re-run).
items.title: Labeling-gap FIXED (alt datasets now built daily); but ETH regime head is NO_EDGE live (RG4 0.46) -> needs retrain before promotion
items.description: ROOT CAUSE: scripts/ops/build_trainer_datasets.sh rebuilt market_raw/market_features for --symbol-scope BTCUSDT ONLY; the alt regime heads' realized-label datasets (ETH built once 06-17 for the cross-asset probe; SOL never) perpetually went stale, so RG4's label join (built from those datasets) never covered the recent live shadow rows -> alt heads UNSCOREABLE. Confirmed coverage: BTC fresh 06-26, ETH 06-17, MES 06-12 (coverage probe scripts/ml/_labeling_gap_probe.py). FIX (commit 7a051e5): generalized build_btcusdt_pair -> build_bybit_pair(symbol,tf) + added ETHUSDT/1h + SOLUSDT/1h to the daily loop (same market_features params as BTC; the eth head is 'identical to btc-regime-1h-lgbm-v2 except the symbol'). VALIDATED: refreshed ETH dataset -> ETH RG4 unlabeled 353/353 -> 6 (gap closed). KEY FINDING: the now-scoreable ETH heads are NO_EDGE on live rows -- eth-regime-1h-lgbm-v1 RG4 0.46 (111 labeled), eth-regime-1h-lgbm-xasset-v1 RG4 0.46 (347 labeled) -- vs RG3-offline 0.70-0.73. That is TRAIN/SERVE SKEW (offline/clean-candle good, live logged-rows ~random), the same class as the BTC yz heads. CONSEQUENCE: the ETH multi-symbol-A backtest A/B ($63->$2336) is optimistic for LIVE -- it scored the head on clean harness candles; live the head feeds a ~random vol label. ETH is NOT live-ready. RG4 prevented a bad promotion. NEXT: (1) retrain the ETH regime head / investigate the offline-vs-live feature parity (why it discriminates offline but not on logged live rows) until it clears RG4 (>=0.55 live); (2) MES 5m (RG4 0.765) is the actually-promotable alt; (3) SOL head training. The fix is durable: any alt head is now RG4-validatable each cycle.
items.trigger_condition: Next /ml-review or /system-review; any session retraining alt regime heads or promoting one; verify the next training cycle's dataset_builds shows ETHUSDT/SOLUSDT market_raw fresh.
items.resolution_criteria: A retrained ETH regime head (target: eth-regime-5m/15m-lgbm-v1) clears RG4 live robustly (>=0.55 across thresholds, not knife-edge) after its shadow soak, the gated ETH A/B is re-confirmed with that head, and advisory promotion is proposed (Tier-3). Root-cause + replacement-head halves DONE 2026-06-28.
items.update_20260628: RETRAIN SESSION (trainer-diag #4869-#4893, branch claude/eth-regime-retrain-skew-mnske0, PR #4868). ROOT CAUSE of the 'NO_EDGE live': both ETH 1h heads emit a near-constant high P(volatile) on live rows (v1 mean 0.776/min 0.508; xasset 0.758/0.500) -> no discrimination. v1: live rolling_log_return_vol +51% vs the 5yr train mean -> 73% of live rows in top vol_b2; xasset: xa_breadth_up all-zeros in TRAINING (dead cross_asset side-stream) but ~0.45 live (see BL-20260628-XA-TRAINING-ZERO). PARTIAL CORRECTION: RG4 defaults vol_threshold=0.003 but the Bybit build labels at 0.005; at matched 0.005 v1 RG4 = 0.58 BUT it's a KNIFE-EDGE (0.46/0.51/0.58/0.48/0.47 across 0.003-0.007 on 111 rows) -> NOT robust; v1 1h stays weak/borderline live, xasset stays NO_EDGE (0.46-0.51 all thresholds). So the original 'needs a better head' stands; only the 0.46 severity was a harness artifact (see MB-20260628-RG4-THRESH). FIX SHIPPED: trained eth-regime-{5m,15m}-lgbm-v1 (mirror the strong BTC 5m/15m heads; the 1h regime family is the weak timeframe). Built ETH 5m/15m market_features (525864/175272 rows) + added them to the daily build. RG3: 5m 0.770 / 15m 0.788 TRUSTWORTHY (recent-fold 0.738/0.751). Both register at shadow -> SOAKING; RG4 future-dated (new heads have 0 live rows). NEXT (post-soak): RG4 the 5m/15m heads at matched 0.005; if robust >=0.55, re-run the ETH A/B + cell-selection WF with the passing head and propose advisory promotion (Tier-3).

items.source: trainer-diag #4892/#4893. RG4 (scripts/ml/replay_pregate_live.py) defaults vol_threshold=0.003, but every Bybit (symbol,tf) market_features dataset is built with vol_threshold=0.005 (build_trainer_datasets.sh::build_bybit_pair). RG4 must score a head against the SAME regime_label definition it trained on; the 0.003 default mis-scores all Bybit heads.
items.title: RG4 harness scores at vol_threshold=0.003 but Bybit heads train at 0.005 -> read the threshold from the dataset, don't hardcode
items.description: Impact measured by a threshold sweep: btc-regime-5m-lgbm-v2 RG4 0.79@0.003 -> 0.83@0.005; btc-1h 0.62->0.58; btc-15m 0.73->0.71; eth-regime-1h-lgbm-v1 0.46->0.58 (the 0.46 'NO_EDGE' in MB-20260627-003 was substantially this artifact, though the ETH head is still only knife-edge at 0.005 so the verdict holds). The fix: replay_pregate_live (and rg4_targeted.sh / fleet_scorecard.sh) should resolve vol_threshold from the dataset build metadata (market_features metadata.json records the build params) or the head's manifest, falling back to 0.003 only when unknown. NOTE MES uses a DATA-DRIVEN median vol_threshold, so a single hardcoded value is wrong for the fleet anyway -- metadata resolution is the correct fix. rg4_vt_sweep.sh (committed this session) is the interim workaround (pass the threshold explicitly).
items.trigger_condition: Next session running RG4 / the fleet scorecard; before trusting any RG4 number as a keep/kill verdict.
items.resolution_criteria: replay_pregate_live resolves vol_threshold from the dataset/manifest (per-symbol, incl. MES data-driven), the fleet scorecard is re-run, and the recalibrated numbers replace the 0.003-default fleet table in MB-20260626-001.
evidence_log: 2026-06-30 (FIX SHIPPED, commit d78a3bb on PR #5167): scripts/ml/replay_pregate_live.py now resolves vol_threshold (explicit flag -> market_features metadata -> per-symbol default Bybit 0.005 -> 0.003 only when unknown) and surfaces vol_threshold_source in the report; ml/cli.py gate-check (_compute_live_regime_auc) passes _VT_UNSET so the shadow->advisory promotion decision inherits per-symbol resolution. MES stays data-driven (median, not hardcoded) — needs metadata or an explicit threshold. tests/ml/test_replay_pregate_live_threshold.py 12 pass. NOTE: a small follow-up to have build_trainer_datasets.sh write vol_threshold into market_features metadata would fully close MES automatic resolution. PENDING operator merge + trainer deploy.
items.status_history: | resolved 2026-06-30 (fix shipped commit d78a3bb, PR #5167; pending merge/deploy).

items.source: scripts/ml/_feature_parity_probe.py (trainer-diag #4869). The ETHUSDT/1h v002 market_features training dataset has xa_breadth_up = all-zeros (mean 0, std 0 over 43800 rows) -- the cross_asset side-stream was dead at build time -- while the LIVE logged rows carry real xa_breadth_up ~0.45. eth-regime-1h-lgbm-xasset-v1 therefore trained on dead peer features and is NO_EDGE live (RG4 0.46-0.51 across all thresholds).
items.title: ETH 1h cross_asset side-stream is all-zeros in the training dataset -> xasset regime head trained on dead xa_* features
items.description: The cross_asset build for the ETHUSDT/1h market_features dataset is producing a zero xa_* block (at least xa_breadth_up; likely xa_peer1/2_* too). The daily build_trainer_datasets.sh::build_bybit_pair for ETH does NOT pass a cross_asset_path, so market_features emits the xa cols as defaults/zeros while the live per-bar scorer (cross_asset_live.py, CROSS_ASSET_LIVE_DISABLED default-off) computes real peer features. That train/serve gap makes eth-regime-1h-lgbm-xasset-v1 untrustworthy. FIX: have the daily build construct the ETH cross_asset side-stream (scripts.ml.build_cross_asset --target ETH market_raw --peer BTC --peer SOL) and pass cross_asset_path to the ETH market_features build (mirroring how the head's manifest header documents it), then retrain the xasset head and RG3/RG4 it. Until fixed, the xasset variant should not be promoted; the base eth-regime-{5m,15m} heads (no xa) are the live path.
items.trigger_condition: Any session retraining or promoting the ETH xasset regime/direction heads; next cross-asset probe work.
items.resolution_criteria: The ETH market_features daily build emits a non-zero cross_asset block, the xasset head is retrained on it, and its RG3/RG4 are re-measured (with vs without xa) for a clean A/B.

updates.note: FIX LANDED (PR #6917): build_trainer_datasets.sh now builds the ETH 1h cross_asset side-stream (peers BTC+SOL) and passes cross_asset_path into the ETH 1h market_features build; loud fail-open (family=cross_asset failed + overall_rc=1) so a regression can't be silent. Local e2e through the exact CLI: all 13 xa_* cols non-zero. CRITICAL COMPANION FIX (ml-infra audit F1): all four ETH 1h manifests pinned dataset.version v001 (frozen ~2026-06-17 snapshot) while the nightly builds v002 — bumped to v002, otherwise the retrain would have silently reread dead-xa v001. Close after the trainer-side rebuild+retrain A/B (verification bundle stage 2) confirms non-zero xa on the real dataset + re-measured RG3; RG4-live re-measure accrues on the shadow soak (the xasset head IS actively scoring — vm-diag #6923).

items.resolution: RESOLVED — fix (PR #6917: nightly ETH 1h cross_asset side-stream + cross_asset_path + v001->v002 manifest pin bump) verified LIVE on the trainer (verification bundle #6931 stage 2, read via trainer-diag #6939): real v002 rebuild emits all 13 xa_* cols non-zero (xa_breadth_up nonzero 26535/43800 mean 0.502 stdev 0.445 — was all-zeros); both eth-regime-1h heads retrained + registered candidate; clean with-vs-without-xa A/B on the rebuilt data: weighted_f1 0.5757 (base) -> 0.5893 (xasset), delta +0.0136. RG4-live re-measure accrues on the shadow soak (the xasset head is actively scoring, vm-diag #6923) — pick up at the next /ml-review.

status_history.note: Live verification bundle stage 2 PASS — non-zero xa block on real v002 + retrain + A/B (+0.0136 weighted_f1 for xasset).

items.source: Operator approved enforcing the Design-A trend_vol OFF-cells live. Reading the code surfaced two blockers before any real-money flip: (1) REGIME_ML_VERDICT_MODE=use was a documented PLACEHOLDER (intents.py) — the gate always used the frozen vol label, never the ML label; (2) the live code resolves the advisory head by the STRATEGY timeframe (trend_donchian 1h / squeeze_breakout_4h 4h) but the validated A/B used the BTC 15m advisory head (btc-regime-15m-lgbm-v2) as the single per-symbol vol label for ALL BTC cells. So live ml_vol_regime returns unknown for 1h/4h -> frozen, and #4834 evidence shows the cells LOSE money under the frozen label (-$32) vs +$1526 under the 15m-head ML label.
items.title: Design-A vol-gate go-live: use-substitution WIRED (#4896); need per-symbol vol-head resolution (15m head for BTC cells) + enforce guard before real-money flip
items.description: DONE: wired REGIME_ML_VERDICT_MODE=use to substitute the advisory head's ML vol label into the gate decision (intents._decision_vol_regime, fail-permissive -> frozen on unknown), tests + CLAUDE.md doc-fix (#4896). KEY DESIGN FINDING: the validated A/B (clock_tf=15m, ml_model_id=btc-regime-15m-lgbm-v2) applied the SINGLE 15m advisory head's vol label to ALL BTC strategy cells regardless of the strategy's own timeframe -> the vol regime is a per-SYMBOL market label, not per-strategy-timeframe. So the go-live does NOT need 1h/4h advisory heads; it needs the LIVE resolver to use the symbol's advisory head (btc-regime-15m-lgbm-v2, already advisory + RG4-robust 0.71-0.83) for the BTC cells. REMAINING (production plan): (A2) per-symbol vol-head resolution (resolve by symbol, not strategy timeframe); (A3) ML-only-enforce guard (only drop a vol cell when vol_label_source==ml, never enforce on a frozen fallback); (B) deploy + REGIME_ML_VERDICT_MODE=use + soak to confirm vol_label_source=ml resolves live for the 1h/4h BTC strategies; (C) REGIME_ROUTER_ENABLED=true to enforce, verify regime_hard_gate enforced:true + vol_label_source=ml. Also add a set-env system-action for REGIME_ML_VERDICT_MODE / REGIME_ROUTER_ENABLED (the #4834 env-flip gap). Open decision: keep or drop the squeeze_breakout_4h (4h) cell (15m label -> 4h trade is a big tf gap, small sample -$55/30t).
items.trigger_condition: This session's vol-gate go-live build (A2/A3); before enabling REGIME_ROUTER_ENABLED on the live VM; next /ml-review.
items.resolution_criteria: Per-symbol vol-head resolution + enforce guard merged; REGIME_ML_VERDICT_MODE=use live with the agreement soak confirming the 15m label resolves for BTC 1h/4h strategies (vol_label_source=ml); REGIME_ROUTER_ENABLED=true enforcing; verified via regime_hard_gate enforced:true + vol_label_source=ml on the live audit.
items.update_20260628_golive: GO-LIVE EXECUTED 2026-06-28 ~08:06 UTC (operator-approved, Tier-3). Sequence: merged #4896 (use-wiring + per-symbol resolution + ML-only-enforce guard) -> pull-and-deploy (e0d052e, #4897) -> set-env REGIME_ML_VERDICT_MODE=use (#4898) -> SOAK PASSED (#4909: regime_ml_vol_shadow row at 07:03 shows vol_regime_ml=calm p_volatile=0.039 ml_source=ml-advisory:btc-regime-15m-lgbm-v2 for a live BTC candidate, vs vol_regime_frozen=unknown -> the per-symbol 15m resolution works live) -> set-env REGIME_ROUTER_ENABLED=true (#4910) -> trader healthy (#4911: heartbeat age 14s, ticking, no crashloop). NUANCE (#4912): REGIME_ROUTER_ENABLED was ALREADY active for the 1-D TREND router (historical regime_hard_gate enforced:true rows on htf_pullback_trend_2h, newest 06-27 19:19, all vol_gated:false) -> the set-env was idempotent and the enforce PATH is battle-tested; what activated TODAY is the 2-D VOL gate (use + per-symbol 15m label + the #4868 trend_vol cells). WATCH: no vol_gated:true + vol_label_source:ml hard-gate row observed yet (requires an infrequent BTC trend_donchian(1h)/squeeze(4h) candidate in a volatile/calm OFF cell) -- confirm the first live vol-fire on the next /ml-review. ROLLBACK: set-env REGIME_ROUTER_ENABLED=false (or REGIME_ML_VERDICT_MODE=off) + restart. OPEN FOLLOW-UPS: (a) the squeeze_breakout_4h 4h-cell was KEPT (all 4 cells, matching the validated A/B which used the 15m head for every cell); operator may drop it (15m label->4h trade, small sample -$55/30t) as a 1-line config PR. (b) GATE FRAGILITY: REGIME_ROUTER_ENABLED + REGIME_ML_VERDICT_MODE are env flags whose loss on a redeploy/migration would silently revert the gate (the POSITION_NETTING_GUARD/Ampere-migration failure class) -- baseline them (router default-on with a *_DISABLED kill-switch; use as the default label source) once soaked, per the contract-clean discussion. (c) confirm REGIME_ROUTER_ENABLED hadn't silently drifted off pre-flip (the 13h gap in regime_hard_gate rows is consistent with infrequent signals, not proof of drift).
items.update_20260628_followups: FOLLOW-UPS WORKED 2026-06-28 (operator: 'keep moving on these'). (1) 4h-cell keep/drop DECISION = KEEP all 4 trend_vol cells. The walk-forward validated the cell set as a whole (4/4 folds); dropping the weakest post-hoc (squeeze_breakout_4h calm/short -$55/30t) is itself a form of overfitting (cherry-picking validated cells). Each cell is individually evidence-backed. The squeeze_breakout_4h cell is the one to WATCH (thinnest sample, biggest 15m-label->4h-trade tf gap) — drop later only if it shows live drift. No config change. (2) GATE-FRAGILITY hardening -> PR #4920 (DRAFT, Tier-3, operator-gated): _regime_router_enabled -> _regime_router_active BASELINE-ON + REGIME_ROUTER_DISABLED kill-switch (+ legacy explicit ENABLED=0 honored for rollback). Closes the netting-guard/Ampere failure class: an env drop on a future migration can no longer silently stop the enforce we just shipped. Backtest harness sets DISABLED on non-'on' runs so the A/B baseline stays shadow-only; 190 regime/intents/backtest tests pass; env-gate-guard clean. Merging is a behavioural no-op on the current live VM (already ENABLED=true). (3) SOL regime head -> PR #4918 (DRAFT, Tier-1): sol-regime-{5m,15m}-lgbm-v1 (mirror the validated ETH/BTC recipe) + SOL 5m/15m in the daily dataset build; trainer building+training+RG3 now. CORRECTION recorded in ROADMAP + CLAUDE.md + intents.py docstring: the earlier 'BTC 1h/4h cells resolve unknown->frozen, enforce gated on 1h/4h advisory heads' note was WRONG — resolution is per-SYMBOL, so BTC cells resolve the 15m advisory head and ENFORCE NOW (confirmed live). ETH/SOL cells (when authored) await promoting their own 15m heads shadow->advisory.
evidence_log: 2026-07-06 ~11:33Z (session wrap-up soak inventory, diag #5714): ETH/SOL 15m base regime heads accruing healthily at shadow - eth-regime-15m-lgbm-v1 856 preds, sol-regime-15m-lgbm-v1 820 preds (both since 2026-06-28, ~8 days; scores non-degenerate 0.50-0.99). REMAINING for the go-live, in order: (1) per-symbol trend_vol OFF-cell evidence - an ETH/SOL vol-split cell-attribution study mirroring docs/research/A-vol-gating-OFFcell-design-2026-06-27.md (BTC's cells are BTC-derived; do NOT copy them across symbols) + per-symbol walk-forward; (2) promote eth/sol-regime-15m heads shadow->advisory (Tier-3, evidence-gated - resolution is per-SYMBOL so each symbol needs its own advisory head); (3) author the cells (Tier-3). Steps 1-3 belong to the next research session - flagged as its first candidate work item.

2026-07-06 (ETH/SOL vol-gate evidence session, trainer-vm-diag #5726/#5729/#5733/#5741/#5745; docs/research/A-vol-gating-ETH-SOL-OFFcell-evidence-2026-07-06.md): per-symbol cell-attribution vol-splits re-derived under eth/sol-regime-15m-lgbm-v1 (shadow-pinned, fell_back=0 both) + 3-arm A/B + fixed-cell + cell-selection walk-forwards. RESULT: NEITHER symbol clears the BTC promotion gate. ETH: A/B $850->$1374 net / DD -23%, fixed-cell net 4/4 but maxDD 2/4, cell-selection 2/3 net / 3/3 DD (fold-2 DD regression +$193; -$277 giveback in the profitable 2025-26 fold; 3 of 4 ungated folds are net-negative - an ETH strategy-review problem, not a gate problem). SOL: clean negative - book already healthy (ret/DD 1.72), A/B trades -$198 net for -50% DD, fixed-cell net only 2/4, cell-selection 1/3+1/3 unstable. Cross-symbol: BTC's winning cell (trending|calm|long Donchian) is ETH's/SOL's biggest LOSER - cells do not transfer, per-symbol derivation was mandatory. DECISION: no Tier-3 proposal; both 15m heads stay at shadow; no ETH/SOL trend_vol cells authored. Also found+fixed BL-20260706-VOLGATE-REPLAY: the offline harness --regime-router on had silently stopped enforcing vol cells since the #4896 guard (all A/B arms identical) - fixed on branch claude/eth-sol-volgate-evidence-84bkhz with a regression test.

items.opened_by: claude (Direction-A: finish the SOL/ETH regime pipeline)
items.source: Follow-on to the BTC vol-gate go-live (MB-20260628-VOLGATE-GOLIVE). The ETH 5m/15m heads (RG3 0.770/0.788) and SOL 5m/15m heads are at shadow, soaking toward a post-soak RG4 that can only score once they accrue enough live shadow rows. Built docs/runbooks/regime-head-soak-to-advisory.md codifying the deterministic shadow->RG4->A/B->walk-forward->cell-author->advisory pipeline (RG4 is the gate, NOT the mis-targeted/sample-starved live_agreement metric).
items.title: Multi-symbol regime heads (ETH/SOL) soak->advisory readiness — re-check RG4 when soaks mature
items.description: WHEN each 15m head has accrued >= ~300-500 live shadow rows (a 15m head ~96 rows/day, so ~3-5 days from 2026-06-28 registration), run the pipeline in docs/runbooks/regime-head-soak-to-advisory.md: (Stage 2) rg4_targeted.sh --vol-threshold 0.005 (threshold-matched; MB-20260628-RG4-THRESH) + rg4_vt_sweep.sh; gate RG4 >= 0.55 TRUSTWORTHY, not knife-edge. If PASS: (Stage 3) per-symbol vol-split A/B UNDER THE 15m HEAD'S LABEL (the pre-drafted ETH cells docs/research/regime_policy_eth_trend_vol-2026-06-27.yaml used eth-regime-1h-lgbm-v1 which FAILED RG4 -> must be re-derived under eth-regime-15m-lgbm-v1); (Stage 4) walk-forward; then (Stage 5/6, Tier-3) author config/regime_policy.yaml cells + promote-stage 15m head -> advisory. Heads: eth-regime-{5m,15m}-lgbm-v1, sol-regime-{5m,15m}-lgbm-v1. Only the 15m head needs advisory (per-symbol resolution prefers the 15m clock); the 5m head stays a finer observer.
items.trigger_condition: Next /ml-review after ~2026-07-02 (>= ~300-500 live shadow rows per 15m head); or sooner if a soak readiness check shows enough rows.

Also found+fixed BL-20260706-VOLGATE-REPLAY: the offline harness --regime-router on had silently stopped enforcing vol cells since the #4896 guard (all A/B arms identical) - fixed on branch claude/eth-sol-volgate-evidence-84bkhz with a regression test.
