items.opened_by: claude (Portfolio Capital Allocator AI — design/proposal session)
items.source: Inventory pass for docs/research/capital-allocation-ai-DESIGN.md (M18). Assessed whether the live-logged substrate is sufficient to TRAIN a cross-strategy expected-net-R ranker. Join order_packages -> trades is clean (realized_R = pnl / (|entry-sl|*qty) derivable; trades schema src/units/db/database.py:119-148), and decision-time features are rich (order_packages.model_scores database.py:228, account_context_snapshots context_snapshot.py:35-52, conviction inputs). The single biggest blocker: per-trade transaction cost is NOT separately logged. Fees/funding are modeled in backtests only (FEE_BPS_ROUNDTRIP=7.5 backtest_system.py:97; taker 0.055%/maker 0.02% backtester.py:30) and offline prop MC (swap 0.033%/day breakout.yaml); at live decision/close time the cost component is not separable in trades.pnl, and multi-day perpetual FUNDING may not be fully captured in the local-compute PnL path.
items.title: Per-trade cost capture (fee_*/funding_* on the close path) — the data gap blocking a clean net-R ranker label/feature; enables MB-20260629-ALLOC-RANKER
items.description: Add per-trade cost fields written on the close path (Tier-1, no order-flow mutation): fee_taker_usd / fee_maker_usd (broker fill fees), funding_paid_usd (cumulative perp funding), and a per-cell swap accrual for prop. Writer: execute.py / order_monitor.py close path, sourced from the broker's fill + funding records where available, else a fixed cost-model estimate flagged as approximate. WHY: realized net-R is the ranker's LABEL and cost is a discriminable FEATURE; without it labels are inflated ~10-20 bps (worse for multi-day perps) and the model can't learn 'high-fee scalp into small TP' vs 'low-fee swing'. Interim: Phases P0-P2 use a fixed cost model (round-trip taker bps from instruments.yaml + per-cell swap from the prop ruleset). This item is the Tier-1 substrate that must land in M18 P0 before the P3 learned ranker is trained on clean labels. Compose with db-wiring (new columns wired to the canonical store, no stray journal).
items.trigger_condition: M18 P0 (substrate) kickoff; or next /ml-review assessing allocator-ranker training readiness.
items.resolution_criteria: fee_*/funding_* columns added to trades + populated on the live close path (verified non-null on real closed trades across brokers that expose fees); a clean offline net-R label query (order_package -> trade -> net_R) reproducible for the ranker dataset. Honest negative acceptable if a broker cannot expose per-fill fees (record the fixed-estimate fallback + its calibration gap).
updates: 2026-07-17 (Slice A landed, #6750): the fixed-estimate cost floor is live — backfill-trade-costs system-action stamped fee_taker_usd + cost_source='estimate' on the whole historical book (712/712 uncosted rows), so a net-R label has a consistent modelled cost. fee_maker_usd / funding_paid_usd stay NULL pending broker truth.

2026-07-17 (Slice B / B0 built, branch claude/broker-truth-cost-backfill): the broker-truth PLAN is B0 join-key -> B1 funding pull -> B2 exact-join fee/funding sweep. B0 shipped this PR: the exchange-fills store (runtime_state/exchange_fills.sqlite) already carries per-fill fee + is_maker keyed by exchange_fills.order_id (Bybit orderId), and the trade's ENTRY orderId was already captured — but only inside notes.trade_id (JSON), not a first-class column. B0 promotes it to an indexed trades.broker_order_id column (_migrate_add_broker_order_id + the open-path insert now writes it + a backfill-broker-order-id system-action copies it from notes.trade_id for history), so the eventual fee sweep is an EXACT indexed join, not a fuzzy (account,symbol,side,qty,time-window) heuristic that could double-count fills across overlapping same-symbol trades. Observability-only (never read on the order path). TWO GAPS the Explore map surfaced that B0 does NOT close (operator-visible, tracked for B1/B2): (1) FUNDING has no source in the fills store (fetch_my_trades = execution list, no perp funding) -> funding_paid_usd needs a NEW Bybit /v5/account/transaction-log pull (B1); (2) broker SL/TP EXITS fill under an orderId the bot never sees (clients.py:775-778), so the entry-orderId join catches only the ENTRY leg -> the B2 sweep must attribute the exit leg by FIFO over the fills stream (each fill consumed once) rather than a naive time-window join, and netting (two strategies sharing one exchange position) makes per-strategy-trade fee split inherently approximate on a netted account. DECISION FOR OPERATOR before B2 --apply: (a) fees-now / funding-as-B1-later acceptable, or funding required in one slice; (b) exit-leg FIFO attribution method sign-off. No cost overwrite ships until that steer + a dry-run coverage report on the live book.

items.opened_by: claude (Portfolio Capital Allocator AI — design/proposal session)
items.source: docs/research/capital-allocation-ai-DESIGN.md (M18) Phase 3. Today nothing ranks competing candidate trades head-to-head by expected value: conviction.py is a hand-weighted P(win) blend (observe-only), regime heads are gates not rankers, flip_ev.py is pairwise only, run_ev_montecarlo is offline per-cell. The allocator needs a learned 'expected net-R given decision-time features' model to rank the full candidate batch.
items.title: Train a cross-strategy expected-net-R ranker (LightGBM, new task family) for the portfolio allocator — rides candidate->shadow->advisory; advisory-only influence
items.description: New model family (a regressor/ranker, NOT a vol classifier — a genuinely new task vs the current regime heads). TARGET: realized net-R per order package (needs MB-20260629-ALLOC-COSTCAP for clean labels; fixed-cost-model labels for the first pass). FEATURES (already logged): confidence, model_scores {model_id:{stage,score}}, meta (setup_type/killzone/bias), conviction inputs (c_strat/c_setup/c_wr/c_reg), the per-SYMBOL advisory regime label (ml_vol_regime_for_symbol), and the account_context_snapshots row (equity, daily PnL, daily DD%, open-trades-count) keyed by (order_package_id, account_id). FEATURES TO ADD: per-symbol/per-asset-class exposure at decision time (today only a raw open_trades_count) + the correlation feature from MB-20260629-ALLOC-CORR. SAMPLE-SIZE REALITY: ~500-2000 live closed real-money trades exist — thin per-strategy; pool across strategies (strategy as a feature) + augment with backtest-labeled decisions for the first model, treat live shadow accrual as the validation stream. Manifest under ml/configs/, dataset built on the trainer VM, scored through ShadowPredictor (logs to shadow_predictions.jsonl). Output ranks the candidate batch; only influences the order package at ADVISORY stage after operator-approved promotion; rules EV (conviction-based) stays the fail-permissive fallback + the shadow baseline it must beat.
items.trigger_condition: After MB-20260629-ALLOC-COSTCAP (clean labels) + M18 P1/P2 (rules EV + greedy selector soaking); when >= ~1000 pooled labeled decisions are queryable; next /ml-review.
items.resolution_criteria: Ranker trained + at shadow, beating the rules-based EV baseline on held-out net-R (purged WF-CV, leak-free) and passing an RG-style robustness/skew check on logged-live rows; a promotion recommendation (shadow->advisory) prepared for operator approval. Honest negative recorded if it cannot beat the rules baseline (data-scale floor — keep the rules EV).
evidence_log: 2026-07-16 (T1.3 research session): RESOLVED -- HONEST NEGATIVE. Built the tooling (per-fold AUC + a frozen market-only P(win) ranker as a learned selection arm in allocator_multisymbol_backtest.py, #6632) and ran the full pre-registered gate. AUC gate PASSED: walk-forward pooled OOS AUC 0.611 market-only up to 0.680 with owner/cell, per-fold min >= 0.55 on every stack (no knife-edge) -- materially above the 06-30 ~0.51. BUT the sizing-normalized concurrency-capped selection backtest FAILED: the learned-ranker arm net -586 vs shared_priority -547 (learned_beats_priority_net=false, delta -39) and vs the EV scorer arm -375 (delta -211). The AUC is between-owner base-rate, not a within-tick cross-symbol selection edge; the rules EV scorer stays the selector (it DID beat priority in this window, +172). M18 P2/P3 (allocator selects the subset) stay PARKED. Full write-up: docs/research/T1.3-ranker-findings-2026-07-16.md. Track closed; reopen only with a within-tick contrastive target + clean cost labels (ALLOC-COSTCAP) + a net-positive opportunity set.

2026-07-06 (post vol-gate follow-up session): confirmed the live allocator soak scores score_kind=ev_net_r (P1 #5098, intent_multiplexer.py:726) and that the 06-30 findings stand — the EV scorer's SELECTION edge is negative (OOS AUC ~0.51), P2/P3 parked. This item is now the substance of ROADMAP row T1.3 (cross-sectional ranker, possibly with encoder embeddings — the one place the T1.2 corpus embedding might still pay off). Gate before ANY routing plumbing: OOS AUC materially > 0.51 in the sizing-normalized allocator harness (scripts/research/allocator_{candidate_dataset,ranker_eval,multisymbol_backtest}.py). Next research session's mandate.

items.opened_by: claude (Portfolio Capital Allocator AI — design/proposal session)
items.source: docs/research/capital-allocation-ai-DESIGN.md (M18) Phase 3 / gap #2. Nothing live computes correlation or covariance between symbols/positions: config/cross_asset.yaml peer features feed shadow regime heads only (cross_asset_live.py, observe-only) and are not a portfolio risk input. Two highly-correlated longs are sized as two independent risk_pct trades, so correlated exposure the caps never see as one.
items.title: Decision-time correlation/covariance feature — doubles as the allocator's covariance-adjusted risk-budget input and a ranker feature
items.description: Compute a rolling cross-pair correlation/covariance over the candidate set + the open book at decision time, log it (extend account_context_snapshots or a sibling table), and use it two ways: (1) as the covariance-adjusted marginal-risk term in the M18 capital allocator (discount a candidate's risk budget by its correlation to the existing/selected book, so correlated trades don't both get full budget); (2) as a feature for MB-20260629-ALLOC-RANKER (learn 'this signal fired when portfolio vol was already elevated' vs 'isolated entry'). Build once, log once, reuse. Reuse the offline cross_asset.yaml peer pure-fns where possible (live==train). Fail-permissive (a correlation error degrades to no-discount, never strands a signal).
items.trigger_condition: M18 P3 (correlation budgeting); after the greedy selector (P2) shows budgets bind and a single-axis EV/risk selector leaves correlated-risk regret on the table; next /ml-review.
items.resolution_criteria: A logged decision-time correlation/covariance feature; the allocator's risk-budget term consumes it in shadow and the soak shows it reduces correlated-exposure regret vs the greedy-only selector; the ranker A/B shows the correlation feature adds signal (or an honest negative that it does not).

items.source: During the 2026-06-30 /ml-review, `python -m ml stage-guard` proposed DEMOTE for the live advisory regime head btc-regime-15m-lgbm-v2 (advisory->shadow) with reason 'live calibration worse than base rate (brier_lift -0.16256)' computed from rank-AUC/Brier of the regime-vol SCORE vs realized trade WIN on n=15 closed trades. But Option A (PR #4700, merged 2026-06-27) already established this trade-outcome axis is MIS-TARGETED for a regime head and made live_agreement NON-BLOCKING in `gate-check`, replacing it with live_regime_discrimination (RG4). Same-session RG4 for this head reads 0.801 (advisory) TRUSTWORTHY -- it is NOT degrading on its proper axis.
items.title: stage-guard's DEMOTE path for advisory REGIME heads still uses brier_lift(score vs trade-win) -- the mis-targeted axis Option A retired from gate-check; align it to RG4 regime-discrimination
items.description: The shadow->advisory gate (ml/promotion/gates.py) was redesigned under Option A so a regime head is judged on live_regime_discrimination (RG4), not live_agreement (trade-win AUC). But the DEMOTE half of the lifecycle -- ml stage-guard's advisory-degradation trigger -- still demotes a regime head on brier_lift of the head's score vs trade WIN (an inherently mis-targeted axis: a vol-regime classifier predicts the regime, not trade outcomes; n is also tiny, 15 trades). Result: stage-guard mechanically recommends demoting a perfectly healthy advisory regime head (RG4 0.80) on a metric the promotion side already deemed invalid for regime heads. This is a false-positive demote signal that, if acted on, would strip the BTC vol-gate's order-influencing head right as the enforce decision (MB-20260627-001) matures. FIX (Tier-1 code, then the demote is sound): make stage-guard's advisory-degradation check for regime-family heads gate on RG4 live-regime-discrimination collapse (e.g. RG4 < 0.50 over a sufficient labeled sample) rather than brier_lift(score vs win); keep the trade-win/brier_lift trigger for the decision/outcome model families where it is the right axis. Until fixed, /ml-review and /system-review must NOT act on a stage-guard demote of an advisory REGIME head without an RG4 cross-check (this review did: HOLD, not demote).
items.trigger_condition: Next /ml-review or /system-review reading stage-guard; or any session touching ml/promotion/stage_guard demote logic or ml/promotion/gates.py.
items.resolution_criteria: stage-guard's advisory-degradation/demote trigger for regime-family heads gates on RG4 live-regime-discrimination (not brier_lift score-vs-win); verified by re-running stage-guard on btc-regime-15m-lgbm-v2 and getting hold (not demote) while RG4>=0.55; the decision/outcome families keep their trade-outcome trigger.
evidence_log: 2026-06-30 (trainer-vm-diag #5164/#5165): stage-guard demote btc-regime-15m-lgbm-v2 on brier_lift -0.16256 (n=15, win_rate 0.467, auc 0.554, drift minor); same-session RG4 advisory 0.801 / shadow 0.727 TRUSTWORTHY (2576 recs). /ml-review ruled HOLD advisory -- the demote is a mis-targeted-metric false positive.

2026-06-30 (FIX SHIPPED, commit e9345f0 on PR #5167): stage_guard._demote_triggers now takes is_regime (via gates.is_regime_classifier, the canonical Option-A helper) and suppresses the brier_lift<0 + auc<0.5 trade-win triggers for regime-family heads; significant-drift + score-collapse demote triggers retained; decision/outcome families unchanged. Verified: a regime head with negative brier_lift -> HOLD (not demote); a decision head with the same -> still demote. tests/ml/test_stage_guard.py 13 pass (4 new), test_gates.py 25 pass. Read-only (stage-guard only proposes) — no live behaviour change. PENDING operator merge + trainer deploy.
items.status_history: open 2026-06-30 (/ml-review: stage-guard demoted a healthy advisory regime head on the trade-win axis Option A retired; filed the misalignment). | resolved 2026-06-30 (fix shipped commit e9345f0, PR #5167; pending merge/deploy).

items.source: M19 T0.1 vol_threshold sweep on the BTC-15m regime head (purged-CV, base features): the head is DATA-STARVED at its shipped vol_threshold=0.005 (4.6% volatile base rate) where f1_volatile is only 0.24, and becomes materially more discriminative at a lower threshold — f1_volatile 0.24 (0.005) -> 0.30 (0.004) -> 0.38 (0.003). Surfaced while characterizing where the frozen-TSFM embedding lift lives (trainer issues #5311/#5320/#5321).
items.title: BTC-15m vol-regime head is weak at its own 0.005 operating point (f1_volatile 0.24); a lower vol_threshold (~0.004) is materially more discriminative — evaluate the head's operating threshold on the RG4 axis
items.description: Independent of embeddings, the base BTC-15m regime head classifies the volatile class poorly at the vol_threshold the live vol-gate uses (0.005 -> 4.6% base rate -> f1_volatile 0.24, recall 0.51/precision 0.16). The class is so rare at 0.005 that the head can barely learn it. Dropping to 0.004 (8.4% base rate) lifts base f1_volatile to 0.30; 0.003 (11.7%) to 0.38. QUESTION FOR THE REGIME TRACK: is 0.005 the right 'volatile' definition for the gate, or should the head be trained/operated at ~0.004 where it genuinely separates the classes? A lower threshold ALSO makes the T0.1 frozen-embedding lift worthwhile (Δmacro_f1 +0.037 at 0.004 vs a marginal +0.012 at 0.005 — the embedding only helps where the class is populated). This is Tier-3 because the head's vol_threshold defines the live vol-gate's 'volatile' operating point (REGIME_ML_VERDICT_MODE=use, BTC enforce is LIVE) — evaluate + propose, do not change unilaterally.
items.trigger_condition: Next /ml-review or /system-review assessing the BTC regime fleet / vol-gate; or any session revisiting config/regime_policy.yaml vol thresholds or retraining btc-regime-15m-lgbm-v2.
items.resolution_criteria: A decision on the BTC-15m regime head's operating vol_threshold backed by RG4 live-regime-discrimination + the gate's backtest A/B: either (a) confirm 0.005 is the intended gate semantics and accept the weaker head, or (b) retrain/operate at ~0.004 (better head + the embedding lift comes along) and re-validate the vol-gate. Cross-referenced to the M19 T0.1 threshold-sensitivity evidence (docs/research/T0.1-embedding-followup-evidence-2026-07-01.md § Threshold sensitivity).
evidence_log: 2026-07-01 (trainer-vm-diag #5311/#5320/#5321, purged-CV BTC-15m, base vs base+32 tsfm_emb): vol_threshold sweep — base f1_volatile / (Δmacro_f1 from embeddings): 0.003=0.38/(+0.052), 0.004=0.30/(+0.037), 0.005[shipped]=0.24/(+0.012), 0.006=0.19/(-0.004). The head's own volatile-class quality and the embedding lift both decay monotonically with the volatile base rate; the shipped 0.005 sits in the weak/marginal zone.

2026-07-16 (POSITIVE FIRST-GATE; candidate manifest btc-regime-15m-lgbm-vt004-pcv-v1, purged 5-fold CV, n_train 175272 / n_eval 87636): a live-faithful mirror of lgbm-v2 with a denser volatile label reads f1_volatile 0.4377 / macro_f1 0.6275 / recall_volatile 0.769 / precision_volatile 0.308 vs the shipped 0.005 head's ~0.24 — thesis confirmed (0.005 is data-starved; a denser label separates the classes). CAVEAT: the vol_threshold=0.004 build produced a 14.05% volatile base rate (12314/87636), which matches the T1.1 track's 0.003 mapping (0.004→7% there), NOT 0.004 — so the specific operating threshold is NOT pinned; the build-param base-rate semantics disagree across tracks and must be reconciled. Item stays OPEN: live threshold unchanged (0.005); remaining Tier-3 gates = mapping reconcile → RG4 (scripts/ml/rg4_targeted.sh) → vol-gate backtest A/B → operator. Evidence: docs/research/MB-20260701-vt004-evidence-2026-07-16.md.
items.status_history: open 2026-07-01 (M19 T0.1 threshold characterization surfaced that the BTC-15m regime head is data-starved at its shipped 0.005 operating point; filed for the regime track to evaluate the operating threshold on the RG4 axis). 2026-07-16 positive first-gate (purged-CV vt004 probe: f1_volatile 0.44 vs 0.24) but threshold not pinned (base-rate cross-track disagreement) — stays open pending mapping reconcile + RG4 + vol-gate backtest A/B.

items.source: M19 Tier-1 T1.1: trained a small causal TCN regime head (btc-regime-15m-tcn-v1) on a GPU burst (RunPod RTX 3090, issue #5472, $0.0269) and gate-checked it vs the incumbent LightGBM regime head under purged-CV (5 folds, ~87.6k eval bars) via trainer-vm-diag relay #5473. Full arc: PRs #5468 (design) + #5469 (trainer/predictor/ONNX-parity/market_sequences family) + #5470 (burst-pod wiring).
items.title: T1.1 deep-sequence (TCN) head is a CLEAN NEGATIVE vs the LightGBM regime head - price-only depth exhausted; pivot to T1.2 breadth + graduate the T0.4 fc head
items.description: RESULT: btc-regime-15m-tcn-v1-gpuburst (purged-CV): macro_f1 0.534, f1_volatile 0.162 (3.6% volatile base rate). Incumbent btc-regime-15m-lgbm-base-pcv-v003: macro_f1 0.560-0.582, f1_volatile 0.190-0.390 (on-disk metrics at 2%/14% base rates). The TCN is BELOW the tree on BOTH macro_f1 and f1_volatile against either incumbent baseline -> win bar (beat both by >0.01) met on neither -> left at candidate (inert), NOT promoted. CONCLUSION: a deep sequence model over raw bars does not beat the class-weighted boosted tree here. With T0.1 (frozen embeddings, marginal) this is the SECOND independent negative on PRICE-ONLY representation -> depth over a single instrument own bars is exhausted; the untested lever is BREADTH: T1.2 self-supervised encoder on the WIDE MULTI-ASSET CORPUS feeding the boosting heads (no labels needed). ALSO graduate the proven T0.4 fc_* head (candidate) to shadow via the mirror-publish serve job. DROP T1.1b (Transformer): TCN did not near-miss (~0.05 macro_f1 gap). CAVEAT: base rates not perfectly matched; a controlled re-run on identical market_features (vol_threshold 0.005) would tighten but not overturn the ~0.05 gap.
items.trigger_condition: Next /ml-review or /system-review; or any session picking the next M19 lever.
items.resolution_criteria: Operator picks the next lever (both fc-graduation + T1.2 corpus chosen 2026-07-03). Optional controlled LightGBM-vs-TCN re-run for a tighter number. T1.1 itself CLOSED as a decisive negative.
evidence_log: 2026-07-03 (gate-check, trainer-vm-diag #5473): TCN macro_f1 0.534 / f1_volatile 0.162 vs incumbent 0.56-0.58 / 0.19-0.39; TCN loses on both. Model at candidate, namespaced -gpuburst (production advisory head untouched). GPU spend: $0.0269 + $0.0136 transient = $0.0405.

2026-07-06 (label-sensitivity arms, issues #5647/#5650, $0.076): the 0.005 negative is SPARSITY-CONFOUNDED. Same TCN/CV/eval-rows at denser labels: f1_volatile 0.162 (3.6% base rate, 0.005) -> 0.282 (7.0%, 0.004) -> 0.423 (14.0%, 0.003); macro_f1 0.534 -> 0.590 -> 0.635. At matched 14% prevalence the vt003 TCN reads above the LightGBM REGISTRY-row numbers (0.423/0.635 vs 0.390/0.560) but that row is a different protocol - NOT a controlled A/B. Decider: matched-label LightGBM control (market_features @ vol_threshold 0.003, identical purged CV) - cheap CPU train on the trainer, next session. Neither arm promotion-eligible (non-production label). Full write-up: docs/research/T1.1-tcn-label-sensitivity-evidence-2026-07-06.md.

2026-07-06 08:46Z (matched-label control, #5658/#5669, $0.10): VERDICT AGAINST THE TCN. LightGBM at the SAME 0.003 label / purged CV / inverse-frequency weighting reads f1_volatile 0.4441 vs the TCN's 0.4229 (TCN loses by 0.021) and macro_f1 0.6287 vs 0.6351 (TCN +0.006, inside the >0.01 noise bar). The sparsity confound is RESOLVED: density lifts both families roughly equally; the tree matches-or-beats the causal TCN at every matched operating point tested. T1.1 negative CONFIRMED, unconfounded. No further TCN arms planned. Full table: docs/research/T1.1-tcn-label-sensitivity-evidence-2026-07-06.md (VERDICT section).
items.status_history: open 2026-07-03 (T1.1 clean negative; pivot to T1.2 breadth + T0.4 fc graduation; drop T1.1b).
items.resolution: Clean, unconfounded negative: the deep-sequence TCN does not beat the class-weighted LightGBM regime head at the production label NOR at denser labels (0.004/0.003) under matched purged CV + matched weighting. Representation frontier stays closed (D3 dormant per the 2026-07-05 ranking).

items.opened_by: claude (M19 T1.2 SSL corpus-encoder A/B session)
items.source: M19 T1.2 P2 A/B: the self-supervised corpus encoder's corpus_emb block was A/B'd into the BTC-15m vol-regime boosting head under purged-CV across two corpus widths (13-series v005 and 28-series widened v006, #5539). CLEAN NEGATIVE both times: corpusemb macro_f1 0.584 (< T0.1 emb 0.611) and f1_volatile 0.303 thin / 0.269 widened (worse than both base 0.389 and emb 0.423). Widening cut the encoder's OWN overfit (val_loss 2.0->1.3) but did not transfer downstream — the embedding buys majority-'range' accuracy (0.825, f1_range 0.898) by crushing minority 'volatile' recall. Evidence: docs/research/T1.2-ssl-encoder-AB-evidence-2026-07-04.md; log S-M19-T1.2-SSL-AB-2026-07-04.
items.title: T1.2 corpus-SSL embedding: task-matched-head probe (regime head is a closed negative)
items.description: The daily-panel SSL embedding fails the intraday BTC-15m vol-regime head because of a representation-target CLOCK mismatch (daily macro/rates/FX/credit backdrop vs 15-min volatility bursts). The reusable follow-up is NOT more corpus width or a bigger encoder (both fail the same way) — it is a head whose TARGET lives on the same daily/cross-asset clock as the corpus: e.g. a daily direction/risk head, or the M18 cross-sectional expected-net-R ranker over a multi-symbol daily universe (the M18 tabular ranker hit only OOS AUC ~0.51, and embeddings were the untested lever there). Only pursue if/when such a head is on the table; the corpus store + ssl_corpus_encoder + parity gate are already built and reusable.
items.trigger_condition: A daily or cross-asset head (daily direction/risk, or the M18 ranker revival) becomes an active experiment.
items.resolution_criteria: Either: corpus_emb clears the beat-BOTH-baselines gate on a task-matched head (→ promote-candidate), OR a task-matched head is A/B'd and corpus_emb still misses (→ T1.2 closes as a full negative, price+context representation exhausted for boosting heads).
evidence_log: 2026-07-04: BTC-15m regime head — clean negative across 13- and 28-series corpus widths (evidence doc + S-M19-T1.2-SSL-AB-2026-07-04).

2026-07-05: M19 next-direction research adds a MANDATORY spectral-overlap pre-check to the trigger (assess corpus-vs-target dominant frequency bands before any embedding work - arXiv 2511.05619 practitioner recommendation), and notes the literature's first lever on a revived daily/cross-asset ranker is hand-engineered features + learning-to-rank (LambdaMART ~3x Sharpe, arXiv 2012.07149), not the corpus embedding. Ranked D3/priority-4 (dormant) in docs/research/M19-next-direction-recommendation-2026-07-05.md.

status_history.note: Opened on the T1.2 regime-head clean negative; scopes the only remaining place the corpus embedding might pay off.

items.opened_by: claude (M19 T0.4 Phase-2 fc→SL/TP geometry backtest session)
items.source: Ran the confound-removed 3-arm fc SL/TP-geometry backtest (scripts/ml/fc_sltp_geometry_backtest.py, #5587) on the trainer VM (relay #5588) over the full synced trade_journal.db: BTCUSDT n=552 sims / 458 real, full fc coverage (no_fc_cover=0), fc span 2021-07..2026-06. Arms: real-realized (pnl/risk_$, the anchor), fixed-resim (actual SL/TP re-simulated forward), fc-vol-scaled (SL/TP distance × clamp(fc_range_rel/median,[0.5,2.0]), same engine, R:R preserved).
items.title: fc→SL/TP geometry offline backtest INCONCLUSIVE: forward triple-barrier re-sim fails reality-calibration (real-realized -0.68R vs fixed-resim -0.06R, ~0.6R gap) so the fc-vol-scaled +0.42R 'edge' is an in-simulator artifact, not a real-money result.
items.description: The anchor arm exposed the confound: real-realized (ground-truth pnl/risk_$, mean_R -0.675) and fixed-resim (same SL/TP re-simulated, mean_R -0.058) diverge by ~0.6R. The forward triple-barrier engine answers a counterfactual the live system does not run ('hold to SL/TP or the 24h cap') — live trades close mostly on fees/slippage, monitor exits, flip-policy closes, and netting-guard/reconciler closes, NOT clean barrier hits. Since fc-vol-scaled uses the SAME engine, its +0.47R apparent improvement over fixed-resim is measured inside a simulator ~0.6R too optimistic vs a losing ground truth (real BTC trades lose 0.68x intended risk) — an artifact of how scaled barrier distance interacts with the max-hold mark-to-close cap, not a validated edge. fc's validated use (vol-regime classifier feature, at shadow) is unchanged; no order-path change proposed.
items.tier: Tier-1 (research/observability); any eventual fc→SL/TP-geometry order change would be Tier-3.
items.trigger_condition: Revisit if/when a live observe-only fc-geometry shadow-soak is built, OR if the offline simulator is made faithful (reproduces live exit behavior so fixed-resim matches real-realized).
items.resolution_criteria: Either (a) a live fc-geometry shadow-soak (exit_ladder_soak shape: log the fc-scaled SL/TP alongside the placed SL/TP per opening order, compare realized outcomes) shows a real net-R/maxDD improvement under the account rulesets, gating a Tier-3 proposal; or (b) the lever is closed as not-worth-pursuing after the soak shows no edge. A pure offline backtest is NOT sufficient (proven unfaithful here).
evidence_log: docs/research/T0.4-fc-sltp-geometry-evidence-2026-07-05.md — full 3-arm table + interpretation + reproduce steps.
Trainer relay #5588 raw output (BTC real-realized n=458 mean_R -0.675 / fixed-resim n=552 -0.058 / fc-vol-scaled n=552 +0.416; ETH n=20 too thin).

2026-07-05: M19 next-direction research ranks the live fc-geometry shadow-soak D1/priority-2 (build now - months-scale accrual clock). Design requirement added from the shadow-mode literature: counterfactual SL/TP outcomes are only PARTIALLY identified from the placed order's realized path - the logger must resolve counterfactual barriers against the bar path and carry an explicit censored flag (never silently score truncated counterfactuals). See docs/research/M19-next-direction-recommendation-2026-07-05.md section 1.

items.opened_by: claude (M19 T0.4 fc-graduation session — soak-tracking handoff)
items.source: The T0.4 fc forecast heads (btc-regime-15m-lgbm-fc-pcv-v1, eth-regime-15m-lgbm-fc-pcv-v1) are at SHADOW, soaking live across BTC+ETH via the CPU mirror-publish serve chain (producer ict-trainer-forecast.timer 15-min; forecast_live reader; zero torch on the money-box). fc is M19's one durable classifier win. The only RG4 (train/serve-skew) look so far was LOW-POWER (stale mirror 03:26 UTC, ~48 labeled BTC-fc rows, 0 ETH, no volatile episodes) and returned ANTI_PREDICTIVE / AUC=None — noise-dominated, NOT a red alarm, but not yet clean positive discrimination either.
items.title: fc heads (BTC+ETH 15m) at shadow, soaking toward the fc→advisory Tier-3 money gate — CHECK the soak maturity and re-run a POWERED RG4 before proposing promotion; do not rush.
items.description: This is the durable 'check the fc soak, act when ripe' record so a future ml-review/system-review picks up the fc→advisory decision at the right time (it is otherwise only in the ROADMAP T0.4 row + sprint logs). The shadow→advisory promotion is the Tier-3 live money gate (operator-approved) — the switch that turns fc from observing to influencing the vol-gate decision. It must NOT be proposed until: (1) the live shadow soak has accrued real VOLATILE episodes on BTC and ETH (the fc head's edge is vol-regime-specific; a soak with no volatile bars can't validate it — base_rate=0 → AUC=None); (2) a POWERED RG4 replay on a FRESH live→trainer shadow-log mirror shows clean positive discrimination (TRUSTWORTHY_SIGNAL, real AUC > ~0.55), NOT the current low-power ANTI_PREDICTIVE/AUC=None; and (3) a head-pinned money-gate walk-forward (resolve the vol head by ID via a scratch-registry step, per the ROADMAP note) passes. If RG4 stays anti-predictive WITH power, that is a live train/serve skew that BLOCKS promotion — investigate fc feature-serving parity before anything else.
items.tier: Tier-3 (shadow→advisory is the live money gate, operator-approved). This backlog item is the readiness TRACKER; the promotion itself is proposed only when the criteria below are met.
items.trigger_condition: Re-check on each ml-review / system-review: has the BTC+ETH fc shadow soak accrued volatile episodes AND has a fresh-mirror powered RG4 been run? First meaningful re-check ~2026-07-19 (≈2 weeks soak); firm read once volatile episodes are present.
items.resolution_criteria: Either (a) volatile episodes accrued + powered RG4 = TRUSTWORTHY_SIGNAL + head-pinned walk-forward passes → propose fc→advisory to the operator (Tier-3); or (b) powered RG4 stays anti-predictive → open a train/serve feature-parity investigation and keep fc at shadow. Resolved when the promotion is made OR the head is demoted/retired.
evidence_log: ROADMAP.md T0.4 row — fc live shadow soak + RG4 low-power watch-flag.
docs/sprint-logs/S-M19-FC-GRADUATION-PROGRAM-2026-07-04.md — RG4 first-look caveats + money-gate walk-forward note.
scripts/ml/rg4_targeted.sh + scripts/ml/replay_pregate_live.py — the RG4 harness (re-run on a fresh mirror).

2026-07-05 (deep-research next-direction session): ranked D4/priority-1. Powered evidence standard quantified from the rare-class AUC literature (events drive AUC quality, PMC12667734; assurance-based CI planning needs ~36-48 positives even for a strong classifier, Stat Med 10.1002/sim.7992 - use logit-transformed CIs at small n): >=40-50 labeled volatile-class bars PER SYMBOL spanning >=5 DISTINCT volatile episodes, on a FRESH mirror. Fresh soak pulls: diag #5610 BTC-fc 199 preds since 07-03 (score 0.51-0.999), #5611 ETH-fc 74 preds since 07-04 => ~96 preds/day/symbol, ~4.4 expected volatile bars/day/symbol => first powered read ~mid-July. Full derivation: docs/research/M19-next-direction-recommendation-2026-07-05.md section 4.

2026-07-06 ~09:45Z (first RG4 run WITH the D4a rails, trainer relay #5690/#5692): the rails fired end-to-end. Freshness gate flagged the 190-min-stale mirror loudly (RG4_NO_SYNC=1 run); power verdict marked BOTH fc heads UNPOWERED with honest counts - btc-regime-15m-lgbm-fc-pcv-v1: 274 recs, n_pos=0/40, episodes=0/5 (its ANTI_PREDICTIVE read explicitly non-evidence); eth-regime-15m-lgbm-fc-pcv-v1: 145 recs, n_pos=1/40, episodes=1/5 (its TRUSTWORTHY 0.6833 read likewise non-evidence). Zero/one labeled volatile bars in the window is consistent with the ~4.4/day/symbol accrual arithmetic -> powered read still ~mid-July. The thin-sample ANTI_PREDICTIVE watch-flag from 07-04 can no longer masquerade as evidence.

2026-07-06 ~10:45Z (overnight program, SOL 3rd-symbol leg): SOL fc-vs-base purged-CV A/B is a CLEAR fc WIN on identical rows (ONE dataset build, market_features/SOLUSDT/15m/v530 built with forecast_path; feature-set-only comparison - cleaner than the BTC/ETH cross-build precedent): fc arm sol-regime-15m-lgbm-fc-pcv-v1 f1_volatile 0.3957 / macro_f1 0.5491 vs base control sol-regime-15m-lgbm-base-pcv-v530 0.3719 / 0.5044 (+0.024 / +0.045, both beyond the 0.01 noise bar; n_eval 82,788, volatile base rate 15.4%). Third symbol confirms the fc family generalizes. Registered candidate; candidate->shadow is PROPOSED but gated on extending the forecast producer to SOLUSDT (FORECAST_SYMBOLS - a trainer-timer env/service change, flagged to the operator, not applied). Evidence: docs/research/SOL-fc-family-AB-evidence-2026-07-06.md; trains ran via the relay no-git path (BL-20260706-TRAINER-GIT-AUTH-BROKEN).

2026-07-06 ~11:13Z (operator-approved SOL producer extension, relay #5701): FORECAST_SYMBOLS default extended to BTCUSDT,ETHUSDT,SOLUSDT (run_forecast_producer.sh — repo PR + applied on the trainer via the no-git relay path); first production wrote a valid SOLUSDT.json artifact (fresh as_of_ts, populated fc_row); sol-regime-15m-lgbm-fc-pcv-v1 promoted candidate->shadow (stage_history 11:12:55Z, observe-only, mirrors the BTC/ETH graduation) and the mirror publish pushed the flip to the live VM. fc now soaks across THREE symbols (BTC+ETH+SOL) - widens the volatile-episode accrual toward the powered RG4 read (~mid-July, now with a 3rd accrual stream). SOL soak-accrual verification on the live VM follows the BTC precedent (shadow_stats + populated fc_* feature_row).

2026-07-06 ~12:18Z (vol-gate session, diag #5722): fc-head soak counts - btc-regime-15m-lgbm-fc-pcv-v1 303 preds (since 07-03), eth-…-fc-pcv-v1 174 (since 07-04), sol-…-fc-pcv-v1 7 (since 11:14Z 07-06). UNPOWERED for the RG4 volatile-episode gate (needs >=40-50 labeled volatile bars/symbol across >=5 episodes); powered read stays ~mid-July as planned. No RG4 run forced.

2026-07-16 (fc powered-read #1, trainer relay #6608 on fresh mirror + trainer reset to 614423bc — the earlier #6602/#6605 runs used STALE trainer code missing the power-accounting, so their AUCs/verdicts are superseded). Authoritative RG4: btc-regime-15m-lgbm-fc-pcv-v1 auc=0.4041 ANTI_PREDICTIVE n_pos=2 episodes=1 (far unpowered, non-evidence); eth-regime-15m-lgbm-fc-pcv-v1 auc=0.556 TRUSTWORTHY n_pos=26 episodes=5; sol-regime-15m-lgbm-fc-pcv-v1 auc=0.5776 TRUSTWORTHY n_pos=27 episodes=6. VERDICT: STILL NOT READY — no head is fully powered (gate is >=40-50 volatile bars AND >=5 episodes; the AUC>=0.55 'TRUSTWORTHY' verdict is AUC-only and does NOT itself confirm power). ETH+SOL cleared the 5-episode gate but sit at 26-27 bars (~two-thirds of target); their 0.556/0.578 AUCs are encouraging but not yet trustworthy on that n. NOTE the stale-code run mislabeled SOL as NO_EDGE — on correct code SOL is as strong as ETH, so the candidates are ETH+SOL (not ETH alone). BTC lags (2 bars). No promotion proposal now. Next re-check ~2026-07-20 when ETH/SOL cross ~40 volatile bars; if still TRUSTWORTHY at power, next gate is the head-pinned money-gate walk-forward per symbol before any Tier-3 fc->advisory proposal.

The fc->advisory decision remains gated on a powered BTC read (daily-report window or more vol episodes) — no proposal yet, per the no-proposal-before-powered rule.

Partial re-check: this session's stage-guard gate-check pull found btc-regime-15m-lgbm-fc-pcv-v1 already AT advisory stage with only n=3 live closed trades attributed and a NEGATIVE brier_lift (-0.1235, worse than base-rate) -- thin, currently-unfavorable live evidence for a model already influencing orders. drift_verdict='moderate'. This doesn't by itself trip a demote (stage-guard says 'no demote trigger tripped'), but the sample size is razor-thin for a live-influencing model. Flagging for the next full /ml-review to weigh alongside the powered RG4 re-run this item calls for. Not resolved -- kept open.

items.source: M19 next-direction deep-research (D2). The trade-outcome heads are an order of magnitude under any defensible sample-size standard (conviction n_eval=20/5 positives; ranker 214 labelable order-packages vs 16-32 dims; EPV literature: ~10+ events/variable as a floor and even that unreliable - Riley et al.). But the journal already holds 3,179 trades / 2,756 order-packages INCLUDING paper (diag #5613) - ~10x the ~350 real-money rows, same strategies/signal builders/feature surface. The meta-labeling literature (JFDS series, Joubert et al.; Hudson & Thames reproduction; E-mini triple-barrier study arXiv 2209.04895) validates the secondary-model-on-own-outcomes frame the conviction stacker already uses.
items.title: D2 label-wall spike A: meta-labeling trade-outcome heads on the FULL journal (real+paper pooled, account_class domain flag), real-money slice as the held evaluation
items.description: Retrain the conviction/trade-outcome heads on real+paper pooled with an account_class domain-flag feature (~2,700+ labelable rows), purged CV, EPV-disciplined feature budget (<= n_positives/10 effective dims), probability calibration per the JFDS meta-labeling calibration paper (calibration helps fixed sizing methods, not learned ones). EVALUATION: real-money rows as the decisive held slice - paper execution differs (fills/slippage/sizing), so paper-trained lift must show up on the real slice or it does not count. Triple-barrier labels over historical bars are allowed ONLY as auxiliary/pre-training signal (our own anchor result shows barrier outcomes diverge ~0.6R from live closes - MB-20260705-FC-SLTP-GEOMETRY); a promotion decision label stays a realized trade outcome. NO GAN/synthetic augmentation (memorization risk documented at n~100). Offline, Tier-1, $0 (CPU; a burst retrain would be <$1 if wanted).
items.trigger_condition: Re-run when the harness's pre-cutoff paper n_train approaches ~1x the real n_train (contemporaneous accrual), or a new paper account back-fills history.
items.resolution_criteria: Either (a) a pooled-label head clears purged-CV on the real-money slice with a defensible n -> promote-candidate + shadow-soak proposal; or (b) paper->real transfer fails on the real slice -> D2 closes until real labels accrue (account-sizing decision), recorded as an honest negative.
evidence_log: 2026-07-05: ranked D2/priority-3 with full external citations in docs/research/M19-next-direction-recommendation-2026-07-05.md section 2 (meta-labeling evidence base, EPV/sample-size arithmetic, augmentation counter-evidence).

2026-07-05 (addendum, authentic-brief Q1 grounding): the M14 track record calibrates spike A - S-MLOPT-S6's meta-label on ~15.7k SYNTHETIC candidates FAILED the majority baseline on 352 real BTC trades (acc 0.670 < 0.756; synth->real domain gap, win rate 0.457 vs 0.244) => barrier/synthetic labels are auxiliary-only, real slice is the evaluation. S-MLOPT-S8's cross-symbol POOLED variant (+domain feature) was the first to clear the baseline (0.7571 > 0.7514, precision 0.54 = 2.2x base rate) but weakly (purged-WF not corroborating) => pooling+domain-flag is the one direction with an internal partial win; spike A retries it with real-execution paper rows (smaller domain gap) instead of synthetic events.

2026-07-06 (spike-A RUN, relay #5632, fresh sync + trade_outcomes v002 rebuild w/ snapshots): ANSWERED-NEGATIVE, STRUCTURAL. 605 closed trades (375 real @ 26.7% win / 230 paper @ 34.4% / 0 unknown). Under the leak-free chronological design the pool barely exists: only 6/230 paper rows predate the primary real holdout window (n_train 306 vs 300) and ZERO at the 0.3 cut - the paper accounts ramped 2026-06+, after most real history. All three arms produce identical predictions (AUC 0.697 primary / 0.622 fold-0.3; acc = majority baseline 0.653; recall_win 0 at the 27% base rate). S-MLOPT-S6 bar NOT cleared; the S8 analogy does not transfer (S8 pooled cross-SYMBOL = contemporaneous; real-vs-paper is cross-TIME = chronologically disjoint). EPV 5-7 across arms - unpowered on feature quality regardless. Disposition: keep OPEN for re-run as contemporaneous history accrues (re-run when pre-cutoff paper n_train approaches ~1x real n_train; harness prints it). Full write-up: docs/research/D2-spike-A-pooled-labels-evidence-2026-07-06.md.

items.source: M20 phase-2 ML-exit probe (trainer relay #6168): joining regime-head shadow scores to in-hold trade paths is currently uninformative — the mirrored shadow log spans only since 2026-07-07 (1 overlapping closed trade) AND the vol-regime heads read P(volatile)>=0.6 essentially always in this window (lo-bucket n=0 over 18k records), so they carry no exit discrimination as-is.
items.title: Train a dedicated EXIT head: P(recover >= X R from here) over in-trade state
items.detail: Experiment spec: build a per-bar in-trade dataset from historical closed trades (features: age_bars, open_r, mfe_so_far, mae_so_far, chop_frac_so_far, dist-to-trail in R, native-TF vol/trend features; label: whether the trade's FINAL realized R exceeds its current mark by >= +0.25R, i.e. 'holding from here paid'). scripts/research/m20_exit_analysis.py::trade_path already computes the path primitives; the label needs no simulation (pure truncation observables, same honesty as the M20 memo counterfactuals). Train LightGBM per timeframe-family; evaluate OOS AUC + a threshold sweep as an exit trigger vs the shipped hard stale-stop. Gate: only worth wiring if it beats the hard rule's delta on the same history. Second experiment: fc-range-scaled TRAIL distance (trail_mult x fc_range_rel/median) evaluated with the same harness lever pattern. UPDATE 2026-07-12 (E0 done): first E0 build landed on the trainer (#6176/#6181/#6182) — datasets-out/exit_head/{1h,2h}: donchian 34,919 rows (1,662 harness + 15 live trades, holding_pays 20.7%), pullback 30,512 rows (614 harness + 26 live, holding_pays 28.9%). Pullback-2h CLEARS the E1 coverage gate (>=300 harness AND >=20 live); donchian-1h at 15/20 live. NEXT: E1 — LightGBM holding_pays/future_r_delta heads with purged walk-forward + offline policy replay per docs/research/M20-exit-head-PROGRAM.md § E1; train-on-harness/validate-on-live distribution check is the hard stop. UPDATE 2026-07-12 (E1 done, #6184/#6186): pullback-2h FAIL (fold AUCs 0.47-0.54, chance-level — honest negative, hard levers stand). Donchian-1h PROMISING below-gate: AUC 0.56-0.62 every fold; tau=0.1 policy beats actual on maxDD and net_R/pos-day in 5/5 folds and on aggregate net_R (86.3 vs 73.7) but loses the big trend years (2023/2024) and the n=15 live set disagrees in sign. E1->E2: no pass. QUEUED: (a) E1.5 conditional policy shapes on donchian (arm head only in chop states, protect the tail); (b) re-run live half at donchian live>=20 trades; (c) pullback ML parked absent new features. Evidence: memo section 8. UPDATE 2026-07-12 (E1.5 done, #6193/#6194): conditional shape below_half_r@tau0.1 on donchian-1h PASSES every E1->E2 criterion — beats actual on net_R AND maxDD AND net_R/pos-day in 5/5 folds incl. the trend years (aggregate 133.9 vs 73.7 net_R at ~half the dd/hold), beats both hard levers everywhere, live n=15 agrees in sign (rpd 3x better). items.source: trainer promotion-readiness cli_stderr
items.title: promotion-readiness CLI can't parse exit-head/peak-head shadow records (missing 'row_keys') -> empty output
items.description: `python -m ml promotion-readiness` writes empty cli_stdout.json + a stderr full of `shadow_log_skip ... err=missing field: 'row_keys'`. The regime-head shadow records carry `row_keys`; the exit-head family records (exit-head-donchian-1h-v1 advisory, exit-head-donchian-peak-1h-v1 shadow) use a different schema (event_source:exit_head, feature_row, would_exit, tau, below_r, score — NO row_keys). The parser skips them and produces no readiness summary. Impact: the exit-head + peak-head models get no automated promotion-readiness assessment right when the peak-head is soaking toward its graduation decision. Data is safe (shadow_predictions.jsonl accrues fine); only the readiness summary tool is broken for this family.
items.trigger_condition: next /ml-review: peak-head shadow soak is approaching a graduation decision and needs a readiness read
items.resolution_criteria: promotion-readiness parser tolerates the exit-head record schema (row_keys optional; derive keys from feature_row when absent) OR the exit-head shadow writer emits a row_keys field; cli_stdout.json is non-empty and includes the exit-head family.
items.resolution: RESOLVED (stale entry — the fix was already merged). ml/shadow/inspector.py::_parse_record derives row_keys from feature_row when absent (the exit-head schema), so exit-head/peak-head shadow records are parsed (not skipped) and get promotion-readiness reads; malformed records still skip gracefully. Landed via #6570 (7a2983f) with tests/ml/test_shadow_inspector.py coverage (test_row_keys_derived_from_feature_row_when_absent + test_explicit_row_keys_win_over_feature_row; 37 tests pass). Confirmed by the M25-P1 verification 2026-07-17. This is M25 Phase-1's readiness-tooling-repair item — already satisfied.

status_history.note: Already fixed by #6570; entry was stale. M25 P1 readiness-CLI-schema item satisfied.

items.title: btc-regime-5m-lgbm-flow-v1 (+ 5m siblings) OOMs ALONE on the 6 GB trainer -- pick a Rule-3 disposition (shrink / GPU-burst / drop)
items.description: The 5-minute BTC regime heads exceed the 5 GB MemoryMax cgroup cap on their own (not contention -- the heavy-job queue already serializes heavy jobs). The cycle now auto-quarantines a manifest after 3 consecutive OOM/timeout failures (BL-20260717-TRAINER-SINGLE-MANIFEST-OOM, src/utils/trainer_manifest_health.py) so it stops burning the 30-min window every cycle, and emits a manifest_quarantine_tripped cycle event as the durable escalation. That stops the WASTE but the manifest still needs a human disposition (Rule 3, docs/claude/trainer-resource-protocol.md): (a) shrink its peak RSS -- LightGBM is CPU-bound so chunk the dataset / shorten the 5m history window / cut feature count (preferred; a GPU won't speed a LightGBM fit); (b) run it on the GPU burst just to get it off the 6 GB box; or (c) drop/split it. Decide, apply, and the quarantine self-clears on the next successful train. Confirmed manifests: btc-regime-5m-lgbm-flow-v1 (exit 137 on 2026-07-03 + 2026-07-15; the 18.7h wedge that motivated the per-manifest timeout), plus the cascade siblings btc-regime-5m-lgbm-v2 and btc-regime-5m-lgbm-yz-v1.
items.source: trainer-vm-diag issue #6798 (2026-07-17): free -m shows 5571/5909 MB used, 158 avail (box full); journal shows ict-trainer.service oom-kill on Jul 03/04/07/08; training_cycle.jsonl shows btc-regime-5m-lgbm-flow-v1 exit_code 137 x2 + 143, cascading to v2/yz-v1. (mes-* exit-1 failures are a SEPARATE missing-dataset issue, not OOM.)

updates.note: Rule-3 disposition APPLIED (PR #6917): (a) shrink, fleet-wide — the whole 5m OOM class shared one root cause (full-column list-of-dicts dataset load ≈5.2G anon-rss on ~500k-row 5m datasets; the paired memcg kills ~20s apart were the AUDIT subprocess + train). Load-time column projection landed (manifest-referenced cols + safety set; TRAINING_LOAD_ALL_COLUMNS=1 opt-out); audit heredoc projects to features+target. 6th OOM-class manifest identified from the 2026-07-19 cycle: eth-regime-5m-lgbm-v1 (exit 137) — included in live verification. Quarantine module kept (self-clears on success). Close after the live verify confirms the five+1 manifests train under the 5G cap.

items.resolution: RESOLVED — Rule-3 disposition (a) shrink, verified LIVE on the trainer (verification bundle #6931, read via trainer-diag #6939): all six 5m OOM-class manifests (btc-regime-5m-lgbm-v2, btc-regime-5m-lgbm-yz-v1, btc-regime-5m baseline, eth-regime-5m-lgbm-v1, sol-regime-5m-lgbm-v1, btc-regime-5m-lgbm-flow-v1) retrained rc=0 under the 5G cap with load-time column projection (PR #6917); Max-RSS 0.24-1.08 GB vs the prior ~5.2G exit-137 profile. Quarantine self-cleared on each. The MemoryMax=5G/MemorySwapMax=0 drop-ins are KEPT DELIBERATELY as the containment backstop (not to be removed on 'fixed' grounds). Final acceptance check: next nightly cycle clean end-to-end (successor session verifies ~2026-07-20).

updates.note: P2 label-volume run BUILT + landed (PR #6917): scripts/ml/m23_phase2_labelvol.sh pools BTC+ETH+SOL (3-strategy harness roster per symbol on the train side; each symbol's real closed trades appended to the live_holdout eval book), new p2pool manifests (won @v020, won_r @v021, `symbol` categorical per the xsym lesson), gate references recomputed from the pooled eval book, m23_ev_gate net-R sweep at tau∈{0.5,0.75}. Interface-drift fix that protects the run: record_harness_trades override precedence (squeeze rows were mislabeled). Run + findings doc: verification bundle stage 3.

P2 (3-symbol label-volume pooling) COMPLETE — HONEST NEGATIVE, NO-GO on P3 (docs/research/M23-phase2-labelvol-findings-2026-07-19.md). Pooling BTC+ETH+SOL x 3 strategies tripled the backtest TRAIN pool (1,685->5,077 rows, 9/9 legs emitted) but the LIVE eval book grew 376->383 only (ETH +7, SOL 0 — the roster has ~no real closed trades off BTC). Pooled `won` head FAILS the recomputed gate (acc 0.7258 < majority 0.7311; prec 0.2500 < base 0.2689); pooled `won_r` C1 never crosses net-positive at any threshold (tau=0.5 best n=1 @ -3.93R; tau=0.75 best n=3 @ -2.78R) — it LOST P1's n<=11 positive tip (cross-symbol dilution). The label wall is EVAL-side; pooled backtests cannot manufacture real labels. NEXT LEVER = P2b eval-side coverage: rerun with the FULL real closed-trade fleet as the eval book once the WS-B candle shards (alt-USDT 15m + equities/metals 1d, PR #6934) land with the next nightly build (216/491 closed trades currently have no shard). items.source: M24 P1 net-R label pipeline (#6806) + P2 re-grade scorecard (#6808, run #6829). The net-R denominator is honest but thin: of 814 closed trades, only 3 carry FIFO broker-truth fees (cost_source=broker) and funding_paid_usd is 0 for EVERY trade. The per-symbol Bybit funding puller (#6826) deployed and ran cleanly but returned 0 funding records for bybit_2 over 30 days across all four traded perps (BTCUSDT/ETHUSDT/XRPUSDT/ADAUSDT), no exception. Most likely cause: same as BL-20260713-BYBIT2-PNL-UNDERRECORD -- bybit_2 funding accrued on a PRIOR sub-account the current API key cannot read. So crypto-perp net-R is fee-only and understates cost on funding-heavy holds; the whole scorecard is an ESTIMATE-cost view, not broker-truth.
items.title: Close the M24 cost-coverage gap: bybit_2 funding visibility (0 records) + widen broker-truth fee coverage (3/814) before M24 P3/P4
items.description: Two data steps gate M24 mattering. (1) FUNDING VISIBILITY: the funding puller returns 0 for bybit_2 -- almost certainly a sub-account the current key cannot read (mirror of the realized-pnl under-record). Fix is a DATA-VISIBILITY one, not a puller code change: an operator Bybit UM funding export -> a broker_truth-style funding ledger, same pattern as comms/broker_truth_ledger.json for realized. (2) FEE COVERAGE: only 3/814 trades carry broker-truth FIFO fees; the exchange-fills store history needs widening (more of the fills window pulled + attributed) so cost_source=broker becomes the majority. Until BOTH improve, M24 P3 (cost-aware EV scorer refresh) + P4 (within-tick net-R ranker) are BLOCKED -- feeding a ~99%-estimate net-R distribution into the EV scorer just re-derives the fixed cost model, so P3/P4 wait on real coverage. Both P3/P4 are also Tier-3 (order-routing/sizing-affecting), operator-gated. The one sign-flip found (spy_pullback_1h/SPY gross +1.456R -> net -0.457R) is estimate-driven (SPY=alpaca equity, no broker-truth row) and must be confirmed against broker-truth before any Tier-3 demote. Refs: findings doc docs/research/M24-net-r-regrade-findings-2026-07-17.md; scorecard issue #6829; net_r_label #6806; regrade #6808; net-r-regrade action #6826.

updates.note: DIAGNOSED (issue #6831 backfill-broker-truth-costs dry-run). The fee-coverage bottleneck is NOT the join key -- 691/814 closed trades already carry broker_order_id. It is fill availability + netting: only 98 fills in the store (recent ~7d window), 682 join-keyed trades have NO matching fill, and even in-window bybit_2 partial-fill netting leaves 5 ambiguous -> 0 new clean broker-truth attributions. Widening the puller --days is a DEAD lever: broker-truth is bybit-only (BROKER_PNL_READER_EXCHANGES={bybit}), the puller does a single non-paginated fetch_my_trades call, and Bybit execution retention aged out the 682 historical fills. CONCLUSION: all three cost gaps (realized-PnL under-record BL-20260713 + funding=0 + fee coverage) collapse to ONE operator action -- a Bybit UM trade-history + funding export -> an offline reconciled broker-truth cost+funding ledger (extend comms/broker_truth_ledger.json). Data-origination operator hand-off, not a tooling change. Automation is exhausted here. Details: docs/research/M24-net-r-regrade-findings-2026-07-17.md section Cost-coverage diagnostic.

items.source: The daily promotion-readiness report (2026-07-17T17:35Z, issue #6835) proposes ONE demote: btc-regime-15m-lgbm-v2 advisory -> shadow, because its score-distribution drift verdict is "significant". This is the LIVE BTC vol-gate advisory head -- every BTC regime cell resolves its ML vol label into the real-money gate decision (REGIME_ML_VERDICT_MODE=use, BTC enforce LIVE). So the live BTC vol-gate is currently running on a significantly-drifted head. Coherent partial explanation for the NEGATIVE vol-gate operating-curve study (MB-20260701-001): the gate is only as good as the head feeding it.
items.title: TIER-3 DECISION: btc-regime-15m-lgbm-v2 (live BTC vol-gate advisory head) shows significant drift -> demote advisory->shadow, retrain, or hold?
items.description: Operator decision required (Tier-3, order-routing-affecting). Options: (a) DEMOTE advisory->shadow via the promote-stage CLI with --new-stage shadow (plus --by/--reason) -- FAIL-PERMISSIVE: with no advisory BTC head the gate reverts to the frozen vol_detector label (ml_vol_regime_for_symbol -> unknown -> frozen); it strands no signal, just stops using the drifted ML label. (b) RETRAIN/REPLACE: a fresh btc-regime-15m-lgbm-v2 run, or promote a cleaner sibling once one clears live_regime_discrimination + drift_clean (none do as of 2026-07-17). (c) HOLD + re-check the next daily report if the drift is a transient reference-window artifact. NOT actioned autonomously -- demoting/promoting a live advisory head is Tier-3. Evidence: docs/research/M25-readiness-assessment-2026-07-18.md; report runtime_logs/trainer_mirror/promotion_readiness/2026-07-17/. Related: MB-20260701-001 (vol-gate operating curve, negative), MB-20260628-VOLGATE-GOLIVE.

updates.note: REFRAMED after diagnosis (gate-check + shadow-drift, issue #6837). The demote is NOT warranted right now: the 'significant' drift that triggered the 2026-07-17 report has SELF-CORRECTED. As of 2026-07-18T08:13 gate-check drift_clean PASSES (KS 0.191 <= 0.2) and shadow-drift verdict is 'moderate' / PSI 'no_change'. The head is retrained DAILY (training_cycle manifest_ok 07-16, 07-18), and the 07-18 retrain pulled the drift back under threshold -- so drift is a transient daily-snapshot signal here, not a demote trigger. The REAL persistent problem is live_regime_discrimination: FAIL, RG4 AUC 0.530 (< 0.55) -- the live head barely beats random at separating regimes, and it STAYS there across daily retrains, so retraining the same manifest does not fix it. A dataset audit flagged dead feature(s)/degenerate label for this manifest (manifest_audit_flagged 07-16 + 07-18) -- the likely root cause; specifics being located (#6839). VERDICT: neither demote nor naive-retrain. The lever is a FEATURE/LABEL fix shipped as a NEW model version (forward research), then operator promotes the validated replacement; the daily retrain keeps drift in check meanwhile so the live head is not actively harmful. This case seeded the standing drift-remediation process: docs/runbooks/model-drift-remediation.md + .claude/skills/drift-remediation/SKILL.md (distinguish transient drift from persistent capability failure; never reflex-demote on one daily snapshot). oos_edge still uncomputed (gate-check --datasets-root drops the inline relay; run it in-cycle).

ROOT CAUSE FOUND (dataset_audit.jsonl, runtime_logs/trainer/, issue #6839): the 'vol_bucket' feature is 100% NaN -- a DEAD feature (nan_fraction 1.0, 'column absent/null/non-numeric for ~all rows', quarantined) across the BTCUSDT 15m regime manifests (btc-regime-15m-lgbm-vt005-pin-v1, -yz-v1, and v2 which shares the same market_features BTCUSDT 15m v002 dataset + feature set). vol_bucket is the PRIMARY categorical feature in btc-regime-15m-lgbm-v2's manifest (feature_columns[0], categorical) -- for a volatility-regime classifier it is THE key vol signal. So the head trains with its main vol feature entirely absent, which directly explains the near-random live_regime_discrimination (AUC 0.530). The label is healthy (range 167294 / volatile 7978, 2 classes, not flagged). THIS IS A DATASET-BUILD BUG, not a model-architecture problem: vol_bucket is not being populated in the market_features 15m build. FIX (Tier-1 data-pipeline, autonomous): restore vol_bucket population in the 15m market_features dataset family builder -> rebuild -> retrain -> re-check live_regime_discrimination. If the AUC clears 0.55, the retrained version is the promotion replacement (operator Tier-3). NEXT ACTION: investigate the market_features family vol_bucket column build for the 15m timeframe (why it is null for all rows) and fix it. Runbook: docs/runbooks/model-drift-remediation.md worked example.

CORRECTION of the prior update: the 'vol_bucket dead feature = root cause' claim was WRONG. Verified against ml/datasets/audit.py: _audit_feature computes nan_fraction via _to_float() numeric coercion, so a categorical STRING column (vol_bucket = vol_b0/vol_b1/vol_b2) reads as 100% NaN even though it is fully present and healthy (the same audit row shows n_unique=3). Reproduced locally: vol_bucket -> nan_fraction 1.0 BUT n_unique 3, flagged True (false positive); a genuinely dead column -> n_unique 1. So vol_bucket is NOT dead and is NOT the cause of the poor discrimination. The real cause of live_regime_discrimination AUC 0.530 is the GENUINE fleet-wide discrimination weakness (the vol-gate operating-curve study MB-20260701-001 conclusion), with no single dead-feature smoking gun. Forward lever = genuine feature/label research (better discriminating inputs), shipped as a new version, operator-promoted only once it clears the gate. Neither demote nor a quick data-repair. SEPARATELY FIXED the underlying audit bug (BL-20260718-AUDIT-CATEGORICAL-FALSEPOS): _audit_feature now counts only truly-missing (absent/None/'') values toward nan_fraction and never flags a multi-value categorical as dead (+ a constant-categorical catch), with tests. Honesty note: I published the wrong root cause to main (PR #6840) and told the operator before verifying the audit's coercion semantics; corrected here + in the runbook worked example.

updates: 2026-07-18 diagnosis (docs/research/M25-btc-regime-robustness-diagnosis-2026-07-18.md, issue #6842): NOT a dead label -- a same-symbol/same-TF sibling btc-regime-15m-lgbm-fc-pcv-v1 (forecast features + purged-CV) already PASSES live regime-discrimination at 0.614 (vs v2's 0.530) with clean drift (KS 0.072), 14.9d soak. So option (b) resolves concretely: the robust replacement exists in shadow. Forward path = mature fc-pcv (run gate-check --datasets-root for oos_edge; extend soak to >=20.9d; confirm live fc-feature parity) -> Tier-3 promote fc-pcv->advisory + demote v2->shadow. Also: drift self-corrected on v2 (KS 0.173 <= 0.2 PASS on the 2026-07-18 retrain) so a demote-for-drift is NOT warranted; the real issue is live discrimination, not drift. yz-v1 ruled OUT (live AUC 0.463 below random). Do NOT demote v2 meanwhile -- fail-permissive, not actively harmful, daily retrain keeps drift clean.

updates.note: Recent-window RG4 corroboration 2026-07-19: the live advisory head btc-regime-15m-lgbm-v2 reads 0.165 ANTI_PREDICTIVE on the fresh mirror — UNPOWERED (4 vol-class bars / 2 episodes in the current low-vol stretch), so NOT standalone demote evidence, but directionally consistent with the drift finding. The fc-pcv replacement reads 0.605 TRUSTWORTHY on the same window (also unpowered). The swap packet (promote fc-pcv -> advisory + demote v2 -> shadow) ripens on fc-pcv soak >=20.9d ~2026-07-24; gate-check with --datasets-root + live fc-feature parity are the remaining packet inputs.

ESCALATION -- surfacing, not deciding (per health-review's 'soak decisions due' role): this Tier-3 decision has sat 'diagnosed_replacement_identified_awaiting_tier3' since 2026-07-18, unactioned across at least one full system-review cycle (2026-07-20) and this partial one. btc-regime-15m-lgbm-v2 is the LIVE order-influencing advisory head driving the BTC vol-gate (REGIME_ML_VERDICT_MODE=use, enforced 2026-06-28). This session did NOT re-check this specific model's drift (only spot-checked its 5m sibling, btc-regime-5m-lgbm-v2, which reads stable: KS=0.088 minor, PSI=0.0002 no_change -- a DIFFERENT model/timeframe, not evidence either way for the 15m head in question). Recommend the next full /ml-review or /system-review prioritize actually closing this decision (demote/retrain/hold) rather than re-surfacing it again.

items.source: trainer-vm-diag #6922: dataset_audit.jsonl holds 401 flagged rows across 62 of 86 manifests — the observe-only audit fires on most of the fleet nightly, so the xa-zero-class signal it exists for drowns (alarm fatigue).
items.title: De-noise the build-time dataset audit (62/86 manifests flagged nightly), then consider enforcement
items.description: Extract WHICH columns trip the dead-feature flag per manifest (FeatureAudit.name), classify expected-dead (optional side-streams a manifest doesn't use should NOT flag — the audit scores only manifest feature_columns, so investigate why base heads flag), add a per-manifest expected-dead whitelist or tighten thresholds, and once a flagged row is rare + real, flip the observe-only FLAGGED branch in run_training_cycle.sh to skip (enforce). An audit nobody reads is the same as no audit.
items.trigger_condition: Next /ml-review or trainer session with spare cycles.
items.resolution_criteria: A nightly cycle where every audit flag is a genuine data problem (or whitelisted with a reason), and the enforce/observe decision is recorded.

updates.note: PRIORITY RAISED — operator directive 2026-07-19 (see-something-say-something rule, PR #6956): this item is now the poster child for the normalization failure mode. 62/86 manifests alarming made the audit background noise, which concealed the ETH-xa dead-feature bug through weeks of soak. Fix shape: make the audit HIGH-PRECISION (alarm only on decision-relevant defects: dead/zero-variance features, label-coverage collapse, train/serve divergence) so that ANY alarm demands action, and wire it into the ml-review soak-integrity audit (skill updated in PR #6953). Target: alarms drop to near-zero baseline; an alarm becomes a stop-the-line signal, not wallpaper.

items.source: Interface-drift agent sweep: every dataset family's iter_rows ends in `**_: Any` and ml/datasets/cli.py forwards key=value family args verbatim — a misspelled build param is silently swallowed and the builder quietly uses its default (the class behind the buildparams/version bugs).
items.title: Warn/fail on unknown dataset-family build kwargs instead of silently swallowing them
items.description: Add an unknown-kwarg check at the CLI→family boundary (compare passed keys against the family's iter_rows signature; warn loudly or fail). Keep **_ for forward-compat only if the check logs. Covers all families in ml/datasets/families/.
items.trigger_condition: Any ml/datasets-touching session.
items.resolution_criteria: A misspelled build param produces a loud warning or error in the build log, with a test.

items.source: Operator directive 2026-07-19 (in-chat): opposing new-vs-open trades = regime-transition signal (ride it, don't get crushed); timeframe-aware conflict tolerance (multi-day long can host hours-scale shorts); chop bleeds capital — dedicate a milestone. Design: docs/research/M26-regime-transition-conflict-DESIGN.md.
items.title: M26 regime-transition & directional-conflict intelligence — P0 bleed quantification is the entry point
items.description: Execute the M26 phased plan. P0 (Tier-1, first session): mine the intent-audit hold deltas + journal for every conflict event; realized PnL of held positions post-conflict vs close/flip/tighten counterfactuals, stratified by timeframe-ratio, strategy pair, regime cell, confidence gap -> findings doc with the $/R bleed number. Then P1 conflict taxonomy (TF-ratio coexistence matrix), P2 transition-score observe-only soak (M25 parity-first discipline), P3 backtest arms vs hold (confidence-gap flip / TF-coexistence / transition exit-tighten / gross throttle) on backtest_system --flip-policy extensions, P4 Tier-3 *_MODE rollout. No live change before P3 beats hold out-of-sample.
items.trigger_condition: Next ML session with capacity after the research-week WS items; or the next /ml-review (propose as the top experiment).
items.resolution_criteria: P0 findings doc committed with the quantified bleed + concentration; subsequent phases spawn their own items.

updates.note: P0 DELIVERED same-day (docs/research/M26-P0-conflict-bleed-findings-2026-07-19.md; miner scripts/research/m26_p0_conflict_bleed.py @029f3ae; trainer-diag #6963). 585 hold-rows -> 70 episodes -> 98 measured pairs. Held-worse-than-close ~75-77% overall BUT fat-tail split: same/near-TF -$2.3k held vs cross-TF>=4x +$3.5k (coexistence confirmed); htf_pullback_trend_2h 93.6% held-worse (-$6k) vs trend_donchian +$10.1k held (flip counterfactual -$21k — blanket flip dead again). NEXT: P1 taxonomy (empirical thresholds in the findings doc), P2 transition score weighted to same-clock clusters, P3 arms per strategy class (exit-tighten on the pullback/mean-reversion class is the biggest target). Rerun the miner after the WS-B shards land for MES/equities coverage.

P0 FULL-COVERAGE rerun on all 20 WS-B shards (121 measured pairs, was 98): every headline survives. Blanket flip dead (paper flip -$17.3k); cross-TF>=4x benign (held +$4.7k); SHARPENED: same/near-TF conflicts lose BOTH ways (held -$3.0k, flip -$7.1k, close beats hold 65.8%) -> the P3 primary arm is transition-triggered EXIT-TIGHTEN/flatten (M20 levers), confidence-gap flip demoted to secondary. Per-class split decisive: htf_pullback_trend_2h 93.6% held-worse -$6.0k vs trend_donchian hold +$10.1k. Full results in the findings doc full-coverage section. Next: P1 taxonomy implementation.
