items.title: MES 5m candle base is RTH-only (90 bars/day, 13:30-21:00Z) while the live head scores the full ~23h session -- caps RG4 labeling at ~35% and starves promotion power
items.source: WS-A MES RG4 re-read after the 07-19 base refresh: unlabeled stayed 1215/1863 despite fresh coverage through 07-17 20:55Z. Per-day diagnostic (trainer-diag #6984/#6985/#6986): datasets-out/market_raw/MES/5m/v002 has EXACTLY 90 bars/day, 13:30:00Z-20:55:00Z, zero intra-day gaps -- a US-RTH window -- while mes-regime-5m-lgbm-v2 logs ~255-260 shadow rows/day across the full CME session. 90/260 = 0.346 matches the labeled fraction 648/1863 = 0.348 exactly. The 44-day-freeze fix (BL-20260626-MES-BASE-STALE) solved staleness but NOT this: the labeling ceiling is structural session-window coverage, not recency.
items.description: Root-cause which layer forces RTH: scripts/ops/pull_mes_ibkr_history.sh passes use_rth=false to ml/datasets/percontract_pull.py (and adapters/ibkr_offvm.py defaults use_rth=False), yet the v002 rows (source=ibkr_offvm) are strictly 13:30-21:00Z. Suspects: (a) the continuous/resample stitcher (ml/datasets/continuous.py) dropping overnight bars, (b) the cycle's yfinance fallback rebuild writing RTH-shaped rows that keep the inherited source tag, (c) an IBKR-side whatToShow/contract-hours interaction. FIX = full-session MES 5m base. Payoff is immediate and retroactive: the 1215 already-logged unlabeled shadow rows re-label on the next RG4 read once overnight bars exist (~3x power without waiting weeks). SECONDARY finding: the head TRAINED on this RTH-only base but live-scores the full session -- 2/3 of its live scores are out-of-distribution vs training hours; the promotion packet must either restrict the head's influence to RTH or retrain on the full-session base.
items.resolution_criteria: MES/5m market_raw base carries the full CME session (~250+ bars/trading day); a fresh RG4 read shows unlabeled fraction < 0.2; the train-hours vs serve-hours mismatch is dispositioned (retrain on full-session base OR RTH-scoped serving) before any MES promotion packet goes to the operator.
items.trigger_condition: Before the mes-regime-5m-lgbm-v2 Tier-3 promotion packet (MB-20260627-002); also unblocks the M26 P0 MES episode coverage.

evidence_log.note: trainer-diag #6983 (RG4 re-read: 0.7143 TRUSTWORTHY, UNPOWERED 7/40 vol bars 1/5 episodes, n=648, unlab=1215), #6985 (90 bars/day vs 255-260 shadow rows/day), #6986 (13:30-20:55Z gap-free window).

updates.note: ROOT CAUSE FOUND + FIX SHIPPED (suspect (c)-adjacent, actually a coercion bug): pull_mes_ibkr_history.sh passes 'use_rth=false' as a key=value STRING param -> MarketRawBuilder.iter_rows takes it in untyped **adapter_kwargs (so ml/datasets/cli.py's annotation-driven _coerce_bool never applies) -> the adapter ran bool('false') which is True -> useRTH=1 sent to IBKR -> RTH-only bars (13:30-20:55Z, exactly the observed 90/day). Fix: _as_bool string-aware coercion at both iter_bars/iter_contract_bars call sites + 13 regression tests (bool('false') pinned False; garbage raises). REMAINING to resolve: (1) re-run the pull-mes-ibkr-history system-action on the live VM (Tier-2, needs operator OK) with the fixed code to rebuild full-session deep history; (2) trainer syncs + rebuilds MES market_raw/features; (3) fresh RG4 read re-labels the ~1215 backlogged rows; (4) disposition the train-RTH-vs-serve-23h mismatch (retrain on the full-session base is the default plan).

REPULL EXECUTED + RETRO-LABELING PROVEN: operator-approved pull-mes-ibkr-history returned 57,495 5m rows (vs ~19,181 every pre-fix nightly -- the predicted 3x); trainer synced + rebuilt MES base/features (rc=0); fresh RG4 labeled 1,829/1,867 rows (unlab 1215 -> 38, 98% coverage). VERDICT NOT YET EVIDENCE though -- two stacked issues: (1) NEW BUG FOUND+FIXED: replay_pregate_live's _threshold_from_market_features_meta read vol_threshold at metadata top level but builder v12 records it under build_params -> the read labeled at the 0.003 global default vs the build's data-driven 0.0003418 -> 0 volatile positives (degenerate). Fixed (build_params lookup + test). (2) The label definition itself moved with the full-session rebuild (median forward-vol now includes overnight), so the OLD RTH-trained head vs NEW labels is apples-to-oranges regardless -- the retrain path (tonight's nightly cycle trains mes-regime-5m/15m on the full-session base) is the honest route; RG4 the RETRAINED head tomorrow with the fixed threshold resolution. Remaining on this item: tomorrow's retrained-head RG4 + gate-check under the parity gates; then packet or honest negative.

OVERNIGHT ARC COMPLETE. (1) First automatic nightly pull on fixed code: 57,495 full-session rows -- fix permanent. (2) Nightly cycle retrained mes-regime-5m-lgbm-v2 on the full-session base (01:38Z run): macro_f1 0.7126, f1_volatile 0.6712 (RTH-era heads ~0.27). (3) DECISION-GRADE RG4 PASS: AUC 0.7217 TRUSTWORTHY, POWERED 660/40 vol bars across 19/5 episodes -- first fully-powered trustworthy regime read in the program; threshold correctly resolved from build_params metadata (#7015 fix verified). (4) gate-check: 8 gates PASS incl. oos_edge +0.365 macro_f1 purged-WF, ONE blocker: live_parity -- serving-fidelity 100% mismatch (transient: logged rows scored by the OLD artifact; clears as fresh rows accrue) + dead-on-live flags that were mostly FALSE POSITIVES from an over-broad universe (the check compared ALL dataset columns; the head consumes only 7 features, none macro). Fixed: dead-feature universe now scoped to the manifest's declared feature_columns (fallback to full universe when undeclared) + 2 regression tests. Remaining before the packet: re-certify with the scoped check once fresh live rows accrue from the retrained artifact (live VM registry sync), and confirm vol_bucket (a genuinely consumed feature) varies live.

items.title: promotion-readiness sweep memory-thrashes the 6GB trainer (~5GB RSS, D-state, 0-byte outputs) -- inline compute_oos_edge over every shadow model in ONE process; needs the per-model subprocess isolation the training cycle got
items.source: Two identical thrash episodes on 2026-07-19, BOTH killed: (1) 12:37 launch, killed 14:55 after 2h18m at 4.94GB RSS/D-state with 0-byte cli_stdout.json; (2) 15:31 relaunch on parity-gate code (d068089), killed ~16:35 after >1h at 4.98GB RSS, same 0-byte outputs -- so the regression is NOT the parity code. Inputs are tiny (shadow log 43MB/53k rows, backfill 5.4MB, registry 4.3MB/91 models); the hog is ml/promotion/stage_guard.py::run_stage_guard calling compute_oos_edge(entry, datasets_root=...) INLINE for every shadow-stage entry -- each is a full purged-walk-forward retrain/eval (dataset load + LightGBM folds), all in one long-lived process on the 5.9GB box. Same failure class as BL-20260717-TRAINER-SINGLE-MANIFEST-OOM (fixed for the TRAINING cycle via per-manifest subprocesses + cgroup cap); the readiness sweep never got that treatment, and the roster/dataset growth (91 models; WS-B added ~10 symbols to datasets-out on 07-19) pushed it over the cliff. The 07-19 report was recovered by re-running WITHOUT --datasets-root (shadow models hold on oos_edge insufficient_data -- attribution/drift/stages still report).
items.description: Fix shape (mirror the training-cycle memfix): (a) run each compute_oos_edge in a bounded subprocess (fresh interpreter per model, or per-model batches) so peak RSS is one model's working set, not the fleet's accumulation; (b) put MemoryMax + MemorySwapMax=0 on ict-promotion-readiness.service (it has NO cgroup cap today -- only ict-trainer.service does) so a regression fails fast instead of D-state thrashing the whole box for hours; (c) consider making the unit's ExecStart pass --datasets-root only to a scoped oos-edge phase. Interim workaround (documented): run the sweep without --datasets-root for the daily report; oos_edge evidence comes from the single-model gate-check CLI on the head under review (which is where promotion packets are assembled anyway, per the M25 reframe).
items.resolution_criteria: A full-registry promotion-readiness sweep WITH --datasets-root completes on the trainer within its timer window at bounded memory (no D-state thrash), OR the sweep is redesigned so oos_edge is only computed per-model at gate-check time and the sweep drops --datasets-root by design (doc updated).
items.trigger_condition: Next ml-review / trainer-unit hardening pass; before the registry grows further.

evidence_log.note: trainer-diag #6978/#6979 (first kill: 4.94GB D-state 2h18m, WS-B flock waiter rescued with seconds to spare), #6991/#6992 (second thrash on parity code: 4.98GB at 57min, inputs tiny, journal silent after heavy_lock_acquired), #6993 (kill + no-datasets-root relaunch). stage_guard.py notes the sweep already passes None for the parity gates pending per-model resolution -- the same follow-up should carry the subprocess isolation.

items.title: RESOLVED as FALSE ALARM: the "serving-parity skew" was the gate INSTRUMENT — score_fidelity coerced int features to float (_num) before predicting, shifting every re-score; live serving was byte-perfect all along
items.detail: ROOT CAUSE (2026-07-20 ~16:00Z, relays #7151 + #7153): ml/promotion/live_parity.py::score_fidelity passed the logged feature_row through _num (float-coercion) before predict_fn — dayofweek int 0 -> 0.0 and hour_of_day int 14 -> 14.0. lightgbm's frame build is dtype-sensitive, so the coerced re-score differs from the raw-row score by 3e-2..1.2e-1 on EVERY row -> the gate reported 100% mismatch against every artifact with a near-constant residual (the #7069/#7070 forensic signature that had wrongly ruled IN an environment skew). DECISIVE EVIDENCE: replaying post-deploy live rows through the exact serving path (ml.shadow.factory.resolve_predictors -> ShadowPredictor.predict on the RAW row) matched the live scores 20/20 with delta 0.00e+00 on BOTH btc-regime-15m-lgbm-fc-pcv-v1 and sol-regime-15m-lgbm-fc-pcv-v1 (relay #7151); the coerced path reproduced the mismatch (relay #7153). FIX: score_fidelity now predicts on the raw row exactly as logged (no coercion). The lightgbm==4.6.0 pin shipped earlier the same day (PR #7082 + pull-and-deploy) was NOT the cause but stays as good hygiene (equalized live/trainer stacks). NOTE: earlier MES + BTC/SOL "100% fidelity mismatch" reads carry the same instrument artifact — re-cert under the fixed instrument. The _num coercion remains correct in _norm_value (dead-feature distinct counting) — only the fidelity predict path changed.
evidence: issue #7070 (all-16-run sweep, max_d 1.67e-02 for every artifact)

items.title: Frozen-dataset retrain no-op — BLAST RADIUS SCOPED: 6 of 29 shadow/advisory models (incl. BOTH fc-pcv promotion candidates); advisory live head + MES + all v002 fleet UNAFFECTED
items.detail: The all-runs sweep (#7070) shows every retained run from 20260701T193912Z to 20260720T011421Z produces byte-identical predictions on the same feature rows (max_d/mean_d identical to 3 s.f. across all 16), matching the gate-check's cross_run_stability std('macro_f1')=0.0000 over 10 runs. A model retrained nightly on a GROWING dataset should drift slightly every run. Suspects: the fc-pcv dataset build is pinned/stale (not rebuilt with new rows), the trainer caches/skips when the dataset is unchanged, or the manifest trains on a frozen window. Consequence: the 'nightly retrain self-corrects drift' assumption in the drift-remediation playbook is FICTION for this head (and likely siblings — SOL fc-pcv shows std=0.0 over 3 runs; check the full fleet). Verify: compare the dataset build mtimes/row counts consumed by consecutive fc-pcv runs; check dataset_builds.jsonl for the market_features/BTCUSDT/15m family build cadence. Fix: ensure the nightly build actually appends fresh rows and the trainer consumes the updated version pin. BLAST-RADIUS SWEEP (relay #7077, 2026-07-20 09:05Z, all 29 shadow/advisory registry models — dataset mtime + last-2-run metrics identity): AFFECTED (frozen ds >2d + identical retrains): btc-regime-15m-lgbm-fc-pcv-v1 (v520, 18.6d stale), sol-regime-15m-lgbm-fc-pcv-v1 (v530, 14.0d), eth-regime-15m-lgbm-selfonly-v901ctrl + eth-regime-15m-lgbm-xasset-v1 (v901, 3.1d), execution-quality-baseline-v0 + setup-quality-audit-baseline-v0 (v001, 55.4d — constant baselines, low stakes). NOT AFFECTED: the ADVISORY live vol-gate head btc-regime-15m-lgbm-v2 (v002, 0.2d fresh, metrics changing run-to-run) and the whole v002 fleet incl. every MES head — LIVE TRAINING IS HEALTHY for everything on the nightly-rebuilt datasets. MECHANISM: scripts/ops/build_trainer_datasets.sh rebuilds ONLY DATASET_VERSION=v002 (--overwrite) nightly; experiment-pinned versions (v5xx/v9xx/v001) freeze at build date, yet their manifests sit in the nightly train roster — burning no-op retrains and making cross_run_stability read as fake-perfect. WATCH (fresh ds but last-2 metrics identical, non-baseline): eth-regime-5m-lgbm-v1, sol-regime-15m-lgbm-v1, sol-regime-5m-lgbm-v1 — verify eval-window rounding vs genuine skip. PROMOTION IMPLICATION: the fc-pcv candidates' live RG4 track record (BTC 0.627 over 16.8d) was accrued by ONE FROZEN artifact — internally consistent and arguably cleaner soak evidence, but the candidate is trained on data ending ~Jul 1 (BTC) / ~Jul 6 (SOL); the packet must state this and the operator chooses: promote the frozen artifact as-is (it owns the track record) or refresh dataset + retrain + restart soak clock on mechanics gates only. FIX SHAPE: (a) cycle-level guard — skip retrain when the manifest's dataset mtime predates the last run, log 'dataset unchanged' loudly (kills silent no-ops + saves compute); (b) per-head decision to either add the pinned feature builds (fc/pcv variants) to the nightly build or take pinned-manifest heads out of the nightly roster.
evidence: issue #7077 (fleet sweep: 6/29 frozen; advisory head fresh)
